Reflexes: [a Shiny reactive PREFILL is tested by exposing the computed default as a reactive + a PURE guard helper, NOT by reading input$<id> after update*Input – in testServer the update does not echo back into input$, so that assertion can never see it][guard a “default from data, user can override” input with a reactiveVal holding the last value the module wrote + a pure current is NULL/NA or == lastAuto check; prefill only when it passes, and key the observer on the DATA reactive (never the input it writes) so there is no feedback loop][the shipped-table-collapses-to-fallback false-GREEN (Learning 158) recurs for UI defaults too -> prove species differentiation with an INJECTED lookup table (TESTSP=90 vs RHESUS=210), not the bundled single-row one][prefer a STATIC UI input + server observer over a server-rendered uiOutput when the only need is a reactive DEFAULT – smaller blast radius and the existing UI test stays green][adding a reactive to a module’s returned list() is safe when existing tests assert membership with %in% (subset), not identity][a same-file new helper consumed only within its file (and calling an already-installed exported fn) does NOT trip lintr object_usage – the install(quick=TRUE) dance (Learning 158b) is only needed for NEW cross-file/exported symbols]. Apply: any Shiny module that should default an input from loaded data while staying user-overridable; any testServer test of an update*Input effect; any per-key UI default whose shipped lookup collapses to the fallback.

Learning 161 – The local fast R CMD check is NOT apples-to-apples with the PR CI matrix, and its extra WARNINGs/NOTE can be artifacts of the LOCAL tree + build flags rather than publishable-tree defects – prove each is outside the git tree before chasing it, and treat PR CI (which checks out the git tree on 5 platforms and builds vignettes) as the authoritative gate. Three concrete sources seen this session, none a real defect: (a) an untracked stray file with a non-portable name trips “checking for portable file names” – R CMD build sweeps in UNTRACKED files from the package dir, so a macOS/cloud-sync duplicate like tests/testthat/test_species_first_class 2.R (the ” 2” suffix, a space -> non-portable) lands in the tarball and WARNs locally, yet it is not in git so CI never sees it; ISOLATE by moving it aside (mv to /tmp) and re-running the check (WARNING -> OK confirms it was the sole delta), then restore it as-found (do not delete an uncommitted file without owner say-so, SAFEGUARDS); (b) --no-build-vignettes leaves inst/doc empty -> two spurious vignette WARNINGs (“Files in the ‘vignettes’ directory but no files in ‘inst/doc’” + “Directory ‘inst/doc’ does not exist”) – CI builds vignettes so they do not appear there; for a clean local read use devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) (suppresses the inst/doc check) or actually build vignettes; (c) --as-cran incoming-feasibility yields the STANDING archived-on-CRAN NOTE (“Package was archived on CRAN”, “New submission”) whenever the check reaches the network – permanent since 2025-07-29, not a finding. Net: a docs-only publish that returns “3 WARNINGs + 1 NOTE” locally can still be 0 publishable-tree findings; the proof is PR CI passing clean on the merge-result tree. (S170, publish S169 – PR #70, issue #46 item 2b)

What happened. Owner picked option 1 (publish S169’s item-2b UI prefill). One pre-publish step per the S169 handoff: a NEWS Changes bullet for the prefill, phrased to match item 2’s honest “no-op on shipped data” framing; re-rendered NEWS.md (Learning 155 config) -> 0 non-ASCII, confined insertion; spell_check_package 0 unrecognized before/after (dictionary covers “gestational”/“rhesus”/“macaque”; “prefill” already in WORDLIST from S169) -> no WORDLIST change. The build-equivalent pre-flight on the deliverable tree returned 3 WARNINGs + 1 NOTE; rather than hand-wave, I attributed each: the portable-file-names WARNING named the untracked stray test_species_first_class 2.R outright (log line: “Found the following file with a non-portable file name”), so I moved it to /tmp and re-checked (-> portable-file-names OK; remaining = the 2 vignette WARNINGs from --no-build-vignettes + the standing CRAN NOTE), confirming the stray was the sole non-artifact WARNING, then restored it exactly as found. Committed NEWS only (9c8e0d0f; the S170 1B stub stash-carried out of the PR). Pre-flight clean (fetch exit 0, origin/master 866da44f, 3-ahead/0-behind clean FF, merge-tree 0 conflicts, 10-file blast radius all expected). PR #70; all 10 checks PASS (ubuntu-devel 21m54s the long pole; codecov/project green per Learning 152) – the clean --watch exit re-verified fresh non-watch (Learning 157). AskUserQuestion-gated merge (owner: merge commit) with a guarded fresh pre-merge re-check -> merge commit 3446577a (verified MERGED firsthand); reconciled master via verified fetch + ancestor-gated reset --hard (both old-master 866da44f and merged-head 9c8e0d0f asserted ancestors first); stash-popped the stub; verified-merged-before-delete branch cleanup (local+remote deleted, gh api 404).

Reflexes: [local fast R CMD check is not apples-to-apples with PR CI – attribute every extra WARNING/NOTE to either the LOCAL tree (untracked files) or a build FLAG before treating it as a defect; PR CI on the git tree across 5 platforms is the authoritative gate][R CMD build includes UNTRACKED files from the package dir -> a stray file with a space/non-portable name (a macOS/cloud-sync ” 2” duplicate) WARNs locally but is not in git and never reaches CI; isolate by moving it aside + re-checking, then restore as-found – do not delete an uncommitted file without owner say-so][--no-build-vignettes empties inst/doc -> spurious “files in vignettes / no inst/doc” WARNINGs; use devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) or build vignettes for a clean local read][--as-cran incoming-feasibility re-emits the STANDING archived-on-CRAN NOTE when it reaches the network – permanent, not a finding][a NEWS edit usually needs no WORDLIST change: dictionary words (gestational, rhesus, macaque) and backticked code are not flagged – but run spell_check_package before/after to KNOW the delta (Learning 159)]. Apply: any publish/build-equivalent run that returns more WARNINGs/NOTEs than the prior session’s reported baseline; any local R CMD check after a macOS/iCloud/Dropbox sync (stray ” 2” duplicates); any time deciding whether a local check finding blocks a push to master.

Learning 162 – Renaming an EXPORTED package function is a public-API change: the safe, owner-approved shape is “add the new canonical name, keep the OLD name as a thin DEPRECATED alias” (both @exported), NOT a hard rename – the old name’s body becomes { .Deprecated("<new>"); <new>(args) }. .Deprecated() infers the OLD name from the call stack and builds a message naming the NEW one, so the RED test pins the deprecation by matching the NEW name: expect_warning(oldName(x), "newName") plus expect_identical(oldName(x), newName(x)) for behaviour-preservation; the new name’s own behaviour test (expect_identical(newName(3L), list(1L,2L,3L))) fails RED with “could not find function” until GREEN. Three rename-specific reflexes verified this session: (a) git grep IS the exhaustive inventory – it lists every tracked reference deterministically, so SPLIT the hits into must-change CODE (the def file via git mv, the in-package callers, roxygen @examples, the test call, NAMESPACE/man/ regenerated by document(), and the inst/_pkgdown.yml reference index – list BOTH names there since both stay exported+documented or pkgdown warns “missing topics”) vs DO-NOT-TOUCH history (SESSION_NOTES.md, *-cran-comments.md, docs/audits/*, TECH_DEBT_* describe the PAST state; rewriting them is scope creep). (b) A newly-added cross-file function throws a FALSE object_usage_linter “no visible global function definition for ‘’” in single-file lintr::lint() AND lint_package() until the package namespace knows it – object_usage resolves globals against the INSTALLED/loadable namespace, so the new symbol looks undefined while the sibling already-installed call (makeGroupMembers) resolves fine; pkgload::load_all() then re-lint -> 0, and CI installs before linting so it never fires there. Same root cause as Learning 158b (a NEW EXPORTED symbol -> false object_usage until install(quick = TRUE)); here load_all() suffices and devtools::check() does not run lintr at all. (c) A function NAME that lands in man-page PROSE (the alias page \title{Deprecated alias for makeGroupNum}) trips spell_check_package because titles are prose, NOT the \name/\alias/\usage/\code contexts the checker skips – the lever is inst/WORDLIST (matching the already-present grpNum, which is in a title for the same reason; insert in C-locale sort position) OR wrap the identifier in \code{}; verified 0-unrecognized after adding makeGroupNum. Build-equivalent devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) (Learning 161) = 0/0/0 confirmed the whole rename clean; the new deprecation warning is captured by the alias’s expect_warning and does NOT leak into the suite’s warning count (confirmed by isolating warning sources to the pre-existing test_modPyramid.R baseline). (S171, issue #29 – rename makeGrpNum -> makeGroupNum)

What happened. Owner picked #29 (a long-standing rename to match makeGroupMembers). Phase-0 git grep inventory: makeGrpNum is an @exported one-line convenience helper (R/makeGrpNum.R), called once in-package (fillGroupMembers.R:40), once in a roxygen example (fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio.R:68), once in a test (test_fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio.R:47), plus NAMESPACE/two man//inst/_pkgdown.yml; the target makeGroupNum was unused everywhere. A pre-RED AskUserQuestion scope gate took 2 owner decisions (deprecated alias over hard rename, because it shipped on CRAN; implement-this-session over plan-doc-first, because the inventory was done and the blast radius small); then a separate PRE-RED->RED gate, RED->GREEN, and GREEN->REFACTOR (skipped, owner-approved). RED: a new test_makeGroupNum.R (4 expectations) failing for the right reason (function absent / the old function does not warn). GREEN: git mv the file; makeGroupNum canonical (original body) + makeGrpNum deprecated wrapper (own roxygen, @seealso); switched the 3 callers; document() regenerated NAMESPACE (both exports) + the three man pages; inst/_pkgdown.yml lists both; inst/WORDLIST += makeGroupNum. Verification: devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) 0/0/0, suite 0 failed/0 errors (5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings, no deprecation leak), lintr 0 with the package loaded, spell 0, 0 non-ASCII. Committed on feature branch issue-29-rename-makegrpnum; NEWS deferred to the publish session.

Reflexes: [rename an EXPORTED function as new-canonical + old-deprecated-alias (both exported), never a hard rename, unless the owner accepts breaking external callers][.Deprecated("<new>") infers the old name from the call stack and names the new one in its message -> RED-pin it with expect_warning(old(x), "new") + expect_identical(old(x), new(x))][git grep is the exhaustive rename inventory -> split must-change CODE from do-not-touch HISTORY (session notes / cran-comments / audits describe the past)][a newly-added cross-file function trips a FALSE object_usage_linter “no visible global function” until the package is loaded/installed – pkgload::load_all() + re-lint -> 0; CI installs first so it never fires (same root as Learning 158b)][a function name in man-page PROSE (an alias \title{}) trips spell_check -> inst/WORDLIST (like the existing grpNum) or wrap in \code{}][list BOTH names in inst/_pkgdown.yml when both stay exported+documented, or pkgdown warns “missing topics”][a captured deprecation warning (expect_warning) does not inflate the suite’s warning count – verify by isolating warning sources, do not assume]. Apply: any rename of an exported function/object; any TDD task that adds a NEW cross-file or exported symbol (watch the object_usage false-lint); any new bare-prose word shipped in roxygen/.Rd/NEWS.

Learning 163 – An umbrella / tracking issue accumulates COMPLETED children over time, so its body – and even a predecessor handoff’s suggested-next – can be STALE. Before treating “work on the umbrella” as a planning task, re-verify the umbrella’s OWN acceptance criteria firsthand against (a) each sub/related issue’s state (gh issue view <n> --json state,closedAt) and (b) the actual code/tests; a satisfied umbrella should be CLOSED with an evidence-backed comment, NOT re-planned. (S173, close umbrella #45)

What happened. Owner picked the parent-ID cluster (#45). S172’s handoff had offered it and said “#45 … may want a planning session first,” and #45’s own body listed #31 + #28 as open sub-tasks. A firsthand check overruled both: all four umbrella acceptance criteria were already met – #31’s dam-exclusion window is gestation-derived from the existing maxGestationalPeriod with no parallel parameter (R/getPotentialParents.R:108-131), a regression test shows dam selection responds to maxGestationalPeriod (tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R:163), the former :92-93 “hack” TODO is gone, and #28 carries a written/ratified colocation data-model spec (S76/S77). Sub/related issues #31 (CLOSED 2026-06-14), #46 (species-keyed gestation, CLOSED 2026-06-22), and #48 (app wire-in, CLOSED 2026-06-16) are all done – so the correct deliverable was CLOSE, not plan. Because “work on #45” mapped to several very different deliverables (close vs plan #28 vs build #28) and the pick was the owner’s, I surfaced it via AskUserQuestion rather than assuming. The close-out comment cited every criterion against firsthand code-line / test-line / issue-state evidence rather than the umbrella body, and the outward-facing public action (post comment + close issue) was gated behind a second AskUserQuestion showing the exact comment text – the decision was already made, this was eyes-on-the-wording before it went public. Posted the comment (4773497458), gh issue close 45 -> verified state=CLOSED; then verified the umbrella’s one open child #28 stays OPEN and gated (closing the umbrella does not close its children – they were always meant to track independently). No code touched -> TDD N/A; Phase-3E N/A (no runtime change).

Reflexes: [an umbrella/tracking issue’s body + a predecessor’s suggested-next can be stale – it accrues done children; re-verify its OWN acceptance criteria firsthand (sub-issue states + code/tests) before choosing plan-vs-close][a satisfied umbrella should be CLOSED with an evidence-backed comment citing code lines / test lines / sub-issue states, not re-planned – this is the audit-delta reflex (check process history before re-running) applied to umbrellas][closing an umbrella does NOT close its still-open children – leave them open + gated, say so explicitly, and verify each child’s state after the close][gate the outward-facing/irreversible public action (post comment + close issue) behind an AskUserQuestion showing the exact comment text, even when the owner already chose “close” – the decision is made, this is eyes-on-the-wording before it is public/indexed][when “work on X” maps to several very different deliverables (close vs plan vs build) and the choice is the owner’s, surface it via AskUserQuestion, do not assume – FM #23 / observation-vs-decision]. Apply: any session whose task is an umbrella / epic / tracking issue; any time a predecessor handoff frames an issue as “needs planning” (verify it is not already done first); any outward-facing close/comment on a public issue.

Learning 164 – The Genetic Value Analysis has TWO independent rank paths fed by ONE upstream value, and the Shiny-displayed rank is NOT the library orderReport rank. reportGV computes indivMeanKin <- meanKinship(kmat) (R/reportGV.R:93) – the single choke point – then path (A) orderReport/rankSubjects (R/reportGV.R:150, R/orderReport.R:27-85) buckets + ranks for the library report, while path (B) the Shiny module OVERWRITES that rank with rank(report$indivMeanKin - report$gu) (R/modGeneticValue.R:204-206) for the table users actually see. CONSEQUENCE for any GVA-ranking change: fix at the choke point (the shared indivMeanKin) to move BOTH paths; a fix only in orderReport is invisible in the app. Verified the issue-#9 premise adversarially (real qcPed: top-20 GVA ranks are 100% founders, mean kinship 0.0027-0.0035 vs colony 0.0066) – missing-parent animals become U-id founders (addUIds/addParents via qcStudbook.R:198-199), get self-kinship 0.5 + zero to all others (kinship.R:77-88), so low mean kinship + high genome uniqueness (own gene-drop alleles) => false top rank. The noParentage bucket (orderReport.R:46-47) protects ONLY is.na(origin) & totalOffspring==0 & founder and shipped under issue #8, not #9. Two blast-radius boundaries: kinship() has 5 R/ callers (reportGV.R:87, modBreedingGroups.R:173, modSummaryStats.R:357, createSimKinships.R:58, cumulateSimKinships.R:61) so a GVA-only fix must stay at the reportGV level, NOT in shared kinship(); and test_orderReport.R:24,42 hard-codes the buggy ranking (countUnk(top 100)==34), so a correct fix breaks it BY DESIGN – update intentionally. (S174, plan issue #9)

What happened. Owner picked #9 (animals missing a parent falsely top-rank in GVA). Because the issue’s shape (one function vs many) was unknown, I scoped it BEFORE the plan-vs-implement decision: a 6-agent understanding workflow (wf_e8ff66e0-7ed) mapped founder creation, mean kinship, genome uniqueness + ranking, the results table + tests, and an adversarial agent CONFIRMED the top-rank premise against real data; then I spot-verified the load-bearing structural claims firsthand (reportGV.R:85-154, modGeneticValue.R:195-214, orderReport.R:27-85, test_orderReport.R) and ran a firsthand git grep blast-radius inventory. The map showed large-multi-module scope + ~8 genuine methodology decisions (a prior audit docs/audits/IMPLEMENTED_BUT_OPEN_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md:65-68 already classified #9 as a policy-hold needing an owner decision), so I posed plan-vs-implement + scope via AskUserQuestion; owner chose “write a planning doc” covering all three solutions (S1 sex-stratified breeding-age mean kinship; S2 flag/classify; S3 sire/dam columns). Wrote docs/planning/issue9-gva-unknown-parent-ranking-plan.md – evidence inventory, 8 design decisions with my recommendations flagged for owner ratification, 3 vertical slices (one session each, RED->GREEN->REFACTOR) with completion criteria + verification commands + STOP + here-be-dragons, and a §7 ratification checklist. No R//test/code touched -> the plan is the deliverable.

Reflexes: [a GVA-ranking change must target the shared indivMeanKin at reportGV.R:93 – the single choke point feeding BOTH the orderReport library rank AND the modGeneticValue.R:204 Shiny-display rank; an orderReport-only fix is invisible in the app][before plan-vs-implement on an issue of unknown shape, SCOPE it firsthand – understanding-workflow + adversarial premise-verification + grep blast-radius – so the recommendation rests on code state, not the (possibly stale) issue body (FM #6/#11)][confine a GVA fix to reportGV; shared kinship() has 5 R/ callers – changing it hits breeding groups + summary stats + simulations][a test that hard-codes current counts (test_orderReport.R:24,42) may ENCODE the bug -> a correct fix breaks it by design; schedule the golden-count update IN the plan (D8)][when an issue’s remedy is genetics/methodology (the exact substitution algebra, D2), the planning doc gives a recommended default but flags it for owner ratification / a /grill-me, not a unilateral code choice]. Apply: any change to GVA ranking / mean kinship / genome uniqueness; any large issue where plan-vs-implement is the owner’s call; any fix whose correctness depends on a methodology decision the owner owns.

Learning 165 – The Shiny Genetic Value module’s consumer chain is entirely COLUMN-AGNOSTIC, so a column ADDED at the reportGV demographics subset propagates to the displayed table and BOTH CSV exports with ZERO modGeneticValue.R change – and the library ranking helpers are all column-preserving. To surface a new per-animal field in the GVA output (issue #9 / S3 added sire+dam so users can see which top-ranked animals have unknown U-id parents), the ONLY production change is R/reportGV.R:129: demographics <- ped[probands, c(includeCols, "sire", "dam")] (sire/dam are guaranteed present because kinship() at reportGV.R:87 already consumes ped$sire/ped$dam, so the analysis would already have failed without them). The added columns cbind into finalData (reportGV.R:148) and survive every downstream hop because each one passes the WHOLE data frame: orderReport row-subsets into buckets then do.call("rbind", ...) (preserves all columns, orderReport.R:37-83), rankSubjects only sets value/rank per bucket (rankSubjects.R:35-48), filterReport is a one-line row subset (filterReport.R:18), the Shiny gvResults() only recomputes rank (modGeneticValue.R:204-206), geneticValues() renames ONLY indivMeanKin->meanKinship and gu->genomeUniqueness (:317-322), gvaView() filters rows via filterReport (:221-235), and both download handlers write.csv() the full frame (:302, :308). Three reflexes: (a) the COMPLETE RED surface for a GVA report-column change is just test_reportGV.R – it is the ONLY test pinning the report’s column NAMES (two expect_named(gvReport$report, ...) blocks); no other reportGV consumer (summary/print.summary/calcFEFG/rankSubjects/modInput_qcStudbook) asserts report columns positionally or by ncol (verified by grep before declaring RED), so GREEN broke nothing. (b) prove the propagation end-to-end with a testServer integration test, not just the unit test – assert the new columns reach gvResults(), the RETURNED geneticValues(), AND gvaView(), because those are the three reactive surfaces other modules / the table / the subset CSV consume. (c) for an ADDITIVE field, prefer extending the reportGV demographics subset DIRECTLY over broadening the EXPORTED getIncludeColumns() (whose exact return is pinned by test_getIncludeColumns.R and is a public contract) – smaller public surface, owner-ratified at the pre-RED gate. Slice independence: S3 (additive columns) needed NONE of the plan’s §7 D1-D8 ratification – those gate only the number-asserting Slice 2 RED; recognizing this kept the session to one slice and deferred the D2 /grill-me to where it belongs. Build-equivalent devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) = 0/0/0. (S175, issue #9 Slice 1 / S3 – sire/dam columns)

Learning 166 – The Genetic Value Analysis’s DISPLAYED ranking is dominated by genome uniqueness (gu, integer scale 0..50), NOT mean kinship (~0.003..0.017) – by 3-4 orders of magnitude – so issue #9’s “missing-parent animals falsely top-rank” has TWO independent causes and the owner’s 2020 mean-kinship remedy fixes only ONE. Verified on real qcPed (reportGV(qcPed, guIter=1000L), precompute workflow wf_7f819b92-a12): the app top-20 are 100% both-unknown U-id founders sitting at gu=50; substituting a corrected mean kinship (the F2/F3 the plan proposed) moves NOTHING in the displayed top-20 (same 20 founders, same ranks 1-20) because BOTH rank paths key on gu first – path (A) orderReport’s highGu bucket (gu>10, ranked by descending gu, R/orderReport.R:59-60) sits ABOVE the kinship tiers, and path (B) the Shiny rank(indivMeanKin - gu) (R/modGeneticValue.R:204) has gu dominate numerically. The de-elevation IS real but visible ONLY on a kinship-only ranking AND only after the BOTH-unknown founders are corrected (those founders drop ranks 1-20 -> ~89-135 – a workflow projection, NOT derivable from the frozen pedWithGenotypeReport$report). WHEN TO APPLY: before scoping ANY ranking fix, verify on real data WHICH signal actually drives the displayed order – a mean-kinship fix advertised as “fixes the false top-ranking” would ship a user-visible no-op. This reframed #9: Slice 2 = mean-kinship CORRECTNESS only (one-unknown animals), the gu axis flips IN-SCOPE (owned by the expanded Slice 3), and #9 cannot close on Slice 2 alone. THREE further load-bearing findings from the D2 /grill-me: (1) a single GLOBAL sexMean stand-in for a missing parent is genetically WRONG for long-lived, non-randomly-bred colonies (e.g. SNPRC baboons line-bred for ~half the colony over a decade) – the inbreeding distribution drifts across management eras, so the stand-in must come from the focal’s CONTEMPORANEOUS breeding peers (a per-focal getPotentialParents-style window: breeding-age AND present at conception), NEVER a colony-wide mean (the owner overrode the data-driven “global is simpler / identical top-20” recommendation on genetics grounds – exactly what the grill is for); (2) the breeding-age cutoff must be species- AND sex-specific from a table (extend speciesGestation with minMaleBreedingAge/minFemaleBreedingAge + a getSpeciesMinBreedingAge(species, sex, default=2L) accessor), NOT a hard-coded minParentAge=2 (a rhesus assumption) – and the species column is OFTEN ABSENT (qcPed and qcStudbook’s breederPed lack it), so the lookup MUST guard spp <- if ("species" %in% names(ped)) ped$species else rep(NA_character_, nrow(ped)) (the getPotentialParents.R:64-68 pattern), which means on qcPed the seeded rhesus 4/3 row is NEVER exercised (fallback 2) – a RED test hand-computing expected numbers with the rhesus cutoff would be WRONG (use 2); (3) the scalar MK_corrected = MK_current + sexMean/2 identity is exact BY CONSTRUCTION (a modeling choice applied as a scalar floor at indivMeanKin, with the self-term moving 0.5 -> (1+sexMean)/2), NOT agreement with a kinship() matrix rebuild (which differs ~1e-2) – do not write a RED test expecting an 8e-18 match against a rebuild. PROCESS PATTERN (two workflows, both high-ROI): (a) ground a /grill-me with a PRE-COMPUTE workflow that produces REAL numbers + an adversarial check of the algebra/invariants – this surfaced the gu-dominance finding and fixed S174’s self-noted gap (“D2 left as a recommendation, no worked numbers”); (b) adversarially REVIEW your OWN ratification record BEFORE commit – a 4-reviewer workflow (wf_1408109e-bf8) returned GO-WITH-FIXES, catching a MAJOR implementability gap (the absent-species-column path) and three stale §3/§6 statements that now contradicted the authoritative §8, defects a solo author misses. RATIFICATION RECORDING: keep the predecessor’s plan body intact and add an authoritative “Ratification Record” section that wins on conflict, with per-item SUPERSEDED banners on the now-stale decisions so an implementer who opens §3/§4/§6 cannot follow the pre-ratification design. (S177, ratify issue #9 §7 for Slice 2 via /grill-me; filed follow-up issue #73)

Learning 167 – Implementing the issue #9 Slice 2 one-unknown mean-kinship correction surfaced five load-bearing reflexes for this kind of GVA-scalar fix. (1) Compute the substitution from the UNCORRECTED snapshot. correctUnknownParentMeanKinship() snapshots original <- indivMeanKin and computes every focal’s sexMean = mean(original[cohort]) from that snapshot, THEN writes all corrections to a copy – because some cohort members are themselves one-unknown animals being corrected, an incremental in-place update would make the result depend on iteration order. The test_reportGV.R integration test pins this by recomputing the expected value from the uncorrected baseline; an order-dependent impl fails it. (2) The peer cohort is necessarily a subset of the probands, not the whole pedigree – sexMean averages indivMeanKin, which only exists for names(indivMeanKin) (the GVA population). This is a structural consequence of fixing at the reportGV.R:93 scalar level (ratified D1); the helper restricts candPed <- ped[names(indivMeanKin), ]. On a direct reportGV(qcPed) call the population defaults to ALL 280 animals (getGVPopulation returns all TRUE when ped$population is absent), so all 43 one-unknown animals are probands and their male cohorts are 17-26 strong – the tier-1 cohort never empties and the fallback never fires (verified firsthand, matches plan §8-A). (3) Inject after indivMeanKin <- indivMeanKin[probands] and before scale() (reportGV.R:94->95): that single assignment feeds the z-scores, the report indivMeanKin column (cbind at :151), AND both rank paths – one edit moves everything (Learning 164’s choke point). kinship() stays untouched (5 callers). (4) Extending a bundled data object is low-friction but has a fixed checklist. Adding minMaleBreedingAge/minFemaleBreedingAge to speciesGestation keeps getSpeciesGestation + test_getSpeciesGestation.R green (they use %in%/$gestation, not setequal/positional), but you MUST: edit data-raw/speciesGestation.R AND re-run it (Rscript data-raw/speciesGestation.R rewrites data/*.RData – the source edit alone does nothing), update the R/data.R \describe{} block, and devtools::document() (regenerates man/*.Rd) – skip any and devtools::check throws a code/doc-mismatch NOTE and the 0/0/0 gate fails. getSpeciesMinBreedingAge mirrors getSpeciesGestation’s lookup STRUCTURE but not its default (2 years, not 210 days), and matches sex case/whitespace-insensitively with a non-M/F -> default branch. (5) Lint: verify with the package namespace LOADED, because the CI mode (local::. install) resolves object_usage_linter cross-file false positives that a source-only lintr::lint()/lint_package() over-reports. .lintr enforces line_length_linter(80) + (via the “style” tag) implicit_integer_linter, and lint.yaml sets LINTR_ERROR_ON_LINT: true (ANY lint fails CI; tests/ is excluded). A bare lintr::lint_package() flagged my new cross-file calls to getSpeciesMinBreedingAge/correctUnknownParentMeanKinship as “no visible global function” – but so was a PRE-EXISTING call in fillGroupMembers.R:40, and master’s lint CI is green, proving CI resolves them; running pkgload::load_all(".") THEN lintr::lint_package() reproduces CI exactly (the object_usage warnings vanish, leaving only the real line_length/implicit_integer style lints to fix). Use min(x, 1.0) / x * 0.5 (explicit doubles), not min(x, 1) / x / 2. Strict TDD throughout (RED 3 units: the accessor, the cohort+orchestrator helpers, the qcPed integration; GREEN minimum; REFACTOR = lint only). Build-equivalent devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) = 0/0/0. (S178, issue #9 Slice 2 – one-unknown peer-cohort mean-kinship correction)

Learning 168 – Implementing issue #9 Slice 3 proved that a “charter” (the §8-F expanded-slice description) is NOT a “spec” (the §8-E authoritative formula Slice 2 had), and that REAL-DATA grounding BEFORE RED is what surfaces the decisions a charter leaves implicit. Five load-bearing findings. (1) A charter-level slice carries unratified decisions that must be surfaced as owner AskUserQuestions before RED. §8-F bundled four concerns and the load-bearing one (“address the gu inflation that pins founders at the top”) was ratified IN-SCOPE (D6) but had NO algorithm. Grounding split it into two readings: Reading B (rank AROUND gu – make the displayed rank classification-aware so flagged founders sink, gu untouched: implementable now, preserves the documented “a living founder still contributes” stance at calcGU.R:10-34 and all test_calcGU/test_calcA golden invariants, faithful to the already-ratified D7) vs Reading A (recompute/down-weight founder gu inside calcGU/calcA/gene-drop: reverses that documented stance, breaks the golden invariants, and is NOT RED-testable until an owner-ratified algorithm + replacement gu values exist – the Slice-3 analog of Slice 2’s §8-E gate). Owner picked Reading B. WHEN TO APPLY: when a slice’s charter says “address X” with no formula, enumerate the implementable-now interpretation vs the needs-ratification interpretation and let the owner choose – do NOT silently pick. (2) Simulate the proposed fix on REAL data before RED – a charter assumption can make the fix a no-op or a 44%-of-colony change. §8-F framed the both-unknown founders as “missing-data stubs,” but on qcPed ALL 124 both-unknown founders HAVE offspring (they are the colony’s real founding breeders, e.g. GHH9LB gu=50, 1 offspring), and the EXISTING noParentage bucket requires totalOffspring==0 – so preserving that gate flags ZERO of them (the visible fix is a NO-OP), while dropping it demotes all 124 (44% of the 280-animal colony). A readRDS(gv5000.rds) + simulated demote (both gate variants) revealed this; it was a genuine genetics-policy fork (demote-all-keep-imports vs flag-only) the plan never anticipated -> second owner AskUserQuestion; owner picked demote-all. (3) The U-prefix unknown-id predicate OVER-matches by construction yet is CORRECT on qcPed – verify the data, do not assume a bug. isGeneratedUnknownId = startsWith(id, getAutoIdPrefix("U%04d")) = startsWith(id,"U"), so it matches ANY id starting “U”, not just generated “U####”. qcPed has ZERO “U####” ids; its 43 “U…” parents looked like real ids that would be misclassified – but a 20-line probe showed all 43 are genuine unknown stubs (both parents NA, NO birth date, gen 0; examplePedigree carries 1372 “U” ids -> “U” IS the ONPRC unknown convention). So the predicate is right, Slice 2’s correction of the 43 one-unknown animals is sound, and the counts hold (124 both-unknown / 43 one-unknown / 113 known). A cross-cutting “bug” dissolved under a firsthand data check. (4) Clean classify->reconcile architecture: classify ONCE, mark via value=="Undetermined", demote in path B – one source of truth across BOTH rank paths. New internal classifyParentage(sire, dam) (U-id aware; built with out <- rep("known", n); out[anyU] <- "one unknown parent"; out[bothU] <- "both unknown" – NOT ifelse(), which returns logical(0) on empty input) cbinds a parentage column into reportGV’s finalData (auto-propagates to the table + both CSVs, Learning 165). orderReport, made robust to an ABSENT origin column (treat as all-NA so the bucket fires on real data) and keyed on rpt$parentage (fallback getFounders) with the totalOffspring==0 gate DROPPED, marks no-origin both-unknown founders as noParentage->“Undetermined”; genuine imports (origin present) stay in the kept/ranked bucket. Path B (modGeneticValue.R:204-206) ranks value=="Undetermined" LAST via report[order(demote, report$rank), ] then reseq 1..n – preserving EVERY row and the sequential rank, so the existing topN-slice and “rank is sequential” tests stay green; gu and its 5-caller blast radius untouched. (5) Phase-3E without a browser: drive the REAL module server on the shipped data via testServer. A runModularApp browser smoke is not feasible headlessly (the test-e2e-/shinytest2 harness is baseline noise), but testServer(modGeneticValueServer, args=list(pedigree=reactive(qcPed)), {...}) exercises the actual gvResults() reactive end-to-end on REAL shipped data – confirmed the displayed top-20 became 18 known + 2 one-unknown (0 both-unknown), all 45 LIVING both-unknown founders demoted to ranks 45-89 (“Undetermined”) – plus shinyApp(appUI(), appServer) builds. The DT table auto-renders the new column (no column spec). D8: replaced test_orderReport’s brittle countUnk(top100)==34/==21 golden counts (the ==21 line even measured the UN-ordered input, never orderReport) with named-animal behavior assertions on a deterministic origin-bearing fixture. Strict TDD throughout (RED 4 specs failing for the right reason; GREEN minimum; REFACTOR = lint only); full clean regression 0 failed / 0 error; build-equivalent devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) = 0/0/0. (S180, issue #9 Slice 3 – classify unknown-parent animals + classification-aware displayed rank)

Learning 169 – Publishing a slice that RESOLVES an issue’s surface complaint but deliberately DEFERRED a deeper sub-scope: close the original issue AND file the deferred scope as a NEW issue in the SAME publish session – do not keep the original open as a vague catch-all, and do not silently drop the deferred work. Issue #9’s displayed-ranking complaint was met by Slices 2+3 (Reading B – rank AROUND the genome-uniqueness statistic), while Reading A (genuine gu de-inflation inside calcGU/calcA/gene-drop, which reverses the documented calcGU.R:10-34 stance and breaks the golden invariants, so it needs its own §8-E-style ratification) was owner-deferred in S180. At publish the close-vs-keep-open is a genuine OWNER decision – it changes the PR body’s Closes #N vs Relates to #N AND the tracker’s shape – so surface it via AskUserQuestion with the reasoning, NOT a silent pick (owner chose Closes #9). Then HONOR the close by spinning the deferred scope into a fresh, self-contained issue (#76) that states its own acceptance criteria (ratification-first), names the plan section (§8-A/§8-F, D6) and the code stance it would reverse (calcGU.R:10-34), and links the shipped display-side fix (classifyParentage/orderReport/modGeneticValue) – so the backlog reflects exactly what remains, the closed issue is honestly closed (its complaint is met), and the deep-genetics work is grab-able cold by a future session. Complements [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]] (completed work -> CHANGELOG; the tracker holds OPEN items): a closed-with-explicit-follow-up beats a perpetually-open umbrella issue. The Closes #N keyword auto-closes on merge – verified firsthand (#9 closedAt was one second after the merge commit 5ada4b71); always re-verify the issue’s resulting state after the merge rather than trusting the keyword. Publish mechanics otherwise unchanged from the documented convention: push master first per the local-ahead Gotcha (FF-verify with merge-base --is-ancestor first), fold NEWS into the SAME PR (157a), do NOT merge blind (fresh non-watch gh pr checks re-query, 157), AskUserQuestion-gate the irreversible merge with a fresh state re-check (headRefOid==local, origin/master==expected, OPEN/MERGEABLE/CLEAN), ancestor-gated reset --hard + verified-merged-before-delete (146). One verification reflex worth keeping: a skip_on_cran()-gated testServer test reports as a silent “S” skip under a default local run – re-run the Phase-3E test file with NOT_CRAN=true so the demotion assertion actually executes rather than accepting the skip as a pass. (S181, publish issue #9 Slice 3 + file Reading A follow-up #76)

Learning 170 – Issue #73 Part 1 (generalize the speciesGestation lookup from rhesus-only to all 14 common colony NHP species) was a bundled-data + accessor change that combined a TYPE-SYSTEM change with an owner/veterinary methodology component; five reflexes. (1) Fractional biological values force a column TYPE change that cascades completely through the accessor AND its type-strict tests. The owner-supplied minimum breeding ages include 2.5 / 3.5 / 1.5 / 1.0, so minMaleBreedingAge/minFemaleBreedingAge moved integer -> NUMERIC. The cascade is mechanical but total: getSpeciesMinBreedingAge() as.integer() -> as.numeric() for BOTH age columns AND the default AND the empty-input return integer(0L) -> numeric(0L); the function default literal 2L -> 2.0; and EVERY expect_identical(..., 4L) in the test became ..., 4 (expect_identical is type-strict, so a numeric return against an integer literal FAILS – even the unchanged-VALUE fallback tests like getSpeciesMinBreedingAge("UNICORN","F") flip from 2L to 2). The gestation column stayed integer (no fractional gestation) so getSpeciesGestation + its structure test were untouched except for the new rows. (2) The biological VALUES are an owner/veterinary call – surface them, never invent them (the charter pattern, cf. [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] and Learning 168). The issue said “supply biologically appropriate values … a methodology/veterinary call”; the owner supplied a 14-species table of breeding-age + gestation RANGES. The range->single-value rule was ITSELF a decision surfaced via AskUserQuestion: minimum breeding age = LOW end (youngest plausible breeder); gestation = conservative MAXIMUM = HIGH end (a wider potential-parents window never misses a true parent). (3) Don’t silently overwrite a deliberate existing value just because the owner re-supplied it. Rhesus already carried gestation 210 (a documented conservative bound, ~1.27x the ~165 typical, cited Vinson & Raboin 2015); the owner’s supplied 164-166 was the TYPICAL figure. The issue scopes gestation to “where missing” and rhesus is NOT missing -> surfaced keep-210-vs-change-to-166 as an owner AskUserQuestion (owner: keep 210), avoiding a change to getPotentialParents behavior + its tests. (4) Prove blast radius with the DATA before assuming a behavioral change ripples. Rhesus female moved 3 -> 2.5, which SOUNDS like it shifts the GVA mean-kinship peer-cohort threshold – but a firsthand check confirmed NO shipped pedigree (qcPed/breederPed/examplePedigree) carries a species column, so on real data the correction always uses the species-absent default cutoff 2; the rhesus and new-species cutoffs are exercised ONLY by the accessor unit tests + the synthetic species-bearing fixture in test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R (which uses the rhesus MALE cutoff 4, unchanged). So the whole change was contained to two test files (test_getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R, test_getSpeciesGestation.R); test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R + test_reportGV.R were VERIFIED-unaffected (a grep of all four tests + the full regression), not assumed. Consequence carried from Slice 2/3: the new-species values have unit/fixture coverage but NO real-shipped-data exercise (inherent to the data; #73 itself ships no species-bearing dataset). (5) .lintr excludes tests/ but NOT data-raw/. The bare-numeric test literals (4, 2.5) are lint-exempt, but the data-raw/speciesGestation.R numeric vectors trip implicit_integer_linter and MUST be written as explicit doubles (4.0, 12.0) – CI runs lintr::lint_package(), which lints data-raw. Re-run Rscript data-raw/speciesGestation.R after editing (the .RData is the artifact; the source edit alone does nothing – Learning 167 item 4) and devtools::document() (man regen; NAMESPACE stays byte-identical for a data/internal change). Scope discipline: Part 1 (data) shipped as its own vertical slice; Part 2 (user-configurable override path – the accessors already accept breedingTable/gestationTable but nothing threads them through reportGV/modGeneticValue, and the Settings tab at appUI.R:223-228 is an empty placeholder) deferred to a separate session, so #73 stays OPEN. Strict TDD throughout (RED 49 assertions failing for the right reason; GREEN data + coercion minimum; REFACTOR doc + lint). Build-equivalent devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) = 0/0/0. (S182, issue #73 Part 1 – populate the species breeding-age table)

Learning 171 – Publishing a slice whose NEWS bullet SUPERSEDES an earlier dev-version statement: append a self-framing bullet (“previously X … now Y”), do NOT rewrite the prior-slice bullets. Issue #73 Part 1 generalized speciesGestation from rhesus-only to 14 species, but the dev-version NEWS.md already carried three earlier bullets asserting “the shipped species table currently defines only rhesus macaque (210 days)” (the getPotentialParents, the Potential-Parents-tab, and the speciesGestation-dataset bullets from prior slices). A new bullet saying “now 14 species” DIRECTLY contradicts them within the SAME unreleased section – the first real contradiction in this project’s incremental dev-NEWS (prior slices only ADDED non-conflicting bullets). Resolution that honors coherence AND the append-don’t-rewrite convention AND scope discipline (the deliverable is “publish Part 1”, not “rewrite Part-1-adjacent NEWS”): write the new bullet to NARRATE the progression – “The bundled speciesGestation table, previously seeded with only rhesus macaque, is now populated for 14 common colony NHP species …” – so a top-down reader sees the earlier “only rhesus” bullets as the prior increments and this bullet as the supersede, with NO prior bullet edited (no FM #20/#22 risk, no history rewrite). The fallback clause (“species not listed fall back to the defaults, so results are unchanged for pedigrees without a species column”) also keeps the earlier bullets’ “unchanged on existing data” claim true, since no shipped pedigree carries a species column. Two supporting reflexes. (a) Keep a NEWS bullet at SUMMARY level to hold spell_check_package at 0 delta – enumerating the 14 species common names (cynomolgus, vervet, tamarin, bonobo …) would have needed inst/WORDLIST additions; “14 common colony NHP species” is informative, ASCII-clean, and 0-delta (the species detail lives in the data set + data-raw). Code identifiers in backticks are stripped by spell_check_package, so backticked accessor names cost nothing (the existing getSpeciesGestation/getPotentialParents refs already prove the baseline is 0). (b) pandoc escapes #NN as \#NN mid-line and this is NORMAL, not a render trap – the rendered NEWS.md shows issue \#73, exactly matching the existing refs (\#31/\#49/\#38 are already in the file at 0-delta); \# renders as a literal # on GitHub/pkgdown, so it is consistent, not a defect – do not “fix” it. Publish mechanics otherwise textbook and unchanged (146/152/155/157/157a/159/161): no push-master-first this cycle because S181 ended the local-ahead drift ([[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]) so the branch cut directly off an in-sync master (verify master==origin/master firsthand before relying on this); PR diff Part-1-only (3 commits); did-not-merge-blind (fresh non-watch gh pr checks re-query); AskUserQuestion-gated merge with a fresh state re-check (headRefOid==local, origin/master==expected, OPEN/MERGEABLE/CLEAN); ancestor-gated reset --hard + verified-merged-before-delete (gh api 404). Apply: any publish whose user-facing NEWS bullet updates/expands a statement an earlier dev-version bullet already made (the next such case is #73 Part 2’s “now user-configurable”, which will supersede this very bullet’s “Making these values user-configurable is the remaining part of issue #73” – self-frame it again). (S183, publish issue #73 Part 1)

Learning 172 – Planning a config-EXTENSION feature in this project: getSiteInfo is a FIXED hardcoded-key schema, not a tolerant passthrough – so an optional override key follows the getConfigApiKey soft-lookup precedent, and a read-only grounding workflow’s load-bearing claims must be adjudicated FIRSTHAND. Issue #73 Part 2 makes the species breeding-age / gestation / fallback values user-configurable; the owner chose the config-file mechanism (over the empty Settings-tab UI) for all three. Three reflexes the planning grounding produced. (1) getSiteInfo() returns only ~17 hardcoded keys (7 config + ~10 system), each pulled via getParamDef() which STOPS on a missing key (R/getParamDef.R:11) – there is NO arbitrary-key passthrough. Adding inline per-species table keys to the config would force changes to getSiteInfo + the strict-lookup behavior; the clean path is a single optional key (a CSV path) read by a separate getConfigApiKey-style soft-lookup helper (R/getConfigApiKey.R returns "" if absent, is NOT in getSiteInfo/defaultSiteParams, pinned by test_setLabKeyDefaults.R) – ZERO getSiteInfo changes. The CSV header mirrors the bundled speciesGestation columns (species, gestation, minMaleBreedingAge, minFemaleBreedingAge), so a colony exports the bundled table, edits a few rows, and points at it – self-documenting, no parenthesized-list syntax for tabular data. This is the D1 recommendation. (2) A grounding workflow (here a 4-mapper read-only fan-out, wf_c21598f3-932) accelerates a planning inventory, but its load-bearing claims must be verified firsthand (FM #11/#20, Learning 6) – the mappers can DISAGREE or OVERSTATE. The four mappers split on D1 (config-subsystem specialist: CSV path; the chain mappers: inline keys); the config specialist had the stronger case on the merits (the fixed-schema fact above), which I adjudicated by reading the parser firsthand, NOT by vote. And the gestation mapper called modPotentialParents accepting gestationTable but not passing it to getPotentialParents (R/modPotentialParents.R:242) “the single most load-bearing finding … broken at the critical call site” – firsthand reading showed it is a PREFILL-ONLY no-op: the module always forces maxGestationalPeriod non-NULL (:239-240), and getPotentialParents consults gestationTable only when that arg is NULL (R/getPotentialParents.R:63-69), so passing the table there changes nothing today; the override’s real live effect is the gestationDefault prefill (:216-219), and the true gap is that appServer passes the module NO gestationTable at all (:307-310). The workflow found the files fast; the firsthand read corrected the framing. (3) The override accessors REPLACE, so a config override must MERGE onto the bundled table in the reader (the D4 dragon). getSpeciesMinBreedingAge/getSpeciesGestation treat a non-NULL table as a FULL replacement – a species absent from the supplied table falls to the fallback default, NOT to the bundled value. So a colony CSV listing a few species would silently mis-age every unlisted species (rhesus -> 2.0 / 210). The reader must merge user rows onto bundled speciesGestation (user wins per-species; unlisted keep bundled), leaving the accessors unchanged. Two more carried reflexes: the module-config wiring template already exists (modInputServer("dataInput", config = reactive(shared$config)) at R/appServer.R:106; modORIPReportingServer(..., siteConfig = reactive(...)) at :295) – the two target modules (modGeneticValueServer:266, modPotentialParentsServer:307) receive only pedigree, so the gap is precise; and a configurable default threaded above the accessor must default to NULL (not 2.0/210) so the accessor’s built-in is respected when no config is present, preserving the no-config == today invariant. PLANNING session: the deliverable is docs/planning/issue73-part2-user-configurable-plan.md (evidence inventory + D1-D7 for ratification + two vertical slices: GVA tab end-to-end, then Potential Parents tab); NO implementation (FM #18/#25); D1/D4 await owner ratification before Slice 1 RED. (S184, plan issue #73 Part 2)

Learning 173 – Ratifying a predecessor’s plan is its own discrete session in the plan -> ratify -> implement arc – and the ratifier re-verifies the load-bearing claims FIRSTHAND before presenting recommendations, never rubber-stamps. Issue #73 Part 2’s arc is plan (S184) -> ratify (S185) -> implement-per-slice (future). S185’s deliverable was NOT code: it was flipping docs/planning/issue73-part2-user-configurable-plan.md from Status: DRAFT to Status: RATIFIED -- ready to RED, recording the owner’s decisions, and resolving the §7 checklist – the gating step before any Slice 1 RED (the RED tests’ shape depends on D1=CSV-path and D4=merge, so coding before ratification is FM #19 plan-mode-bypass). (1) The ratifier surfaces the genuine owner calls via AskUserQuestion, not prose ([[observation-vs-decision]]). S185 asked four: D1 (config format), D4 (override scope), D5 (gestation reach), and a bundle of the four low-stakes details (D2/D3/D6/D7) as “accept all vs review individually” – respecting the 4-question max while still surfacing each load-bearing decision individually. The owner ratified all as recommended. (2) Before presenting a predecessor’s recommendation for ratification, read the code that the recommendation rests on – the predecessor may be right yet have left a sharp edge (FM #11/#20, Learning 6). S184’s D1/D4/R2 all held when re-read firsthand (getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R:39-41 substitutes the bundled table only when the override is NULL, then matches against ONLY the supplied table -> absent species fall to default, confirming D4-replace is real; getParamDef.R:12 STOPs, getConfigApiKey.R is the soft-lookup, confirming D1), so this was a confirm, not a correction – BUT the firsthand read SHARPENED R2: the accessors have NO NULL handling on default (out <- rep(default, n) -> rep(NULL, n) = numeric(0)/integer(0)), so an upper layer threading a configurable default must translate “no configured default” into NOT passing default to the accessor (omit the arg, or compute x %||% <built-in> AT the accessor call) – threading a bare NULL into getSpeciesMinBreedingAge/getSpeciesGestation’s default silently empties the result. This caveat was added to the plan’s R2 (the Slice 1 implementer reads it there) so the discovery survives. (3) Mark the ratification at every place an executor reads: a top “Ratification record” + a Status: RATIFIED banner + the §3 header note (“inline ‘Ratify …’ notes are now settled”) + the §7 checklist flipped to [x] with the chosen option – so an executor jumping to any section sees the decision is closed, not open. Ratification is a docs-only deliverable (no R/test change -> TDD code-phases N/A, Phase-3E N/A) committed direct to local master then pushed to origin ([[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]); do NOT bundle it with the Slice 1 implement (FM #18/#25). (S185, ratify issue #73 Part 2 plan)

Learning 174 – An optional config-file key whose example is documented as a commented # key = value line MUST have its soft-lookup strip # comment lines before getTokenList, or every user who ships the example config silently ACTIVATES the placeholder. Discovered implementing issue #73 Part 2 Slice 1 (the user-configurable species overrides). getTokenList (R/getTokenList.R) collapses ALL config lines into one string and splits on whitespace/commas/=; it does NOT strip # comments. It detects a parameter as “the token immediately before an =”, so a commented line # speciesOverridesPath = "/home/you/overrides.csv" yields tokens [#, speciesOverridesPath, =, /home/you/...] and speciesOverridesPath IS detected as an active param. getSiteInfo tolerates this because it only LOOKS UP specific hardcoded keys (center/baseUrl/…) which never appear as key = inside comments – spurious comment-derived params are simply never queried. But a getConfigApiKey-style soft-lookup takes the FIRST match by name (tokenList$tokenVec[idx][[1L]][[1L]]), so when a commented example line exists and no active line does, it returns the COMMENTED placeholder. The Phase-3E boot smoke caught it: appServer -> loadSpeciesOverrides() read the example config’s commented # speciesOverridesPath = "/home/you/colony_species_overrides.csv" as active and warned “file does not exist” at boot. Fix: strip full-line comments in the soft-lookup before tokenizing – lines <- lines[!grepl("^\\s*#", lines)] (in the shared getOptionalConfigToken helper, used by getSpeciesOverridesPath and the fallback-key lookups). Added two regression tests (a commented key is inactive; a commented config yields bundled behavior with no warning). NOTE: the SAME latent bug exists in R/getConfigApiKey.R (the example’s commented # apiKey = "paste-your-..." line would be read as the API key) – left UNFIXED as out of Slice 1 scope, but flag it if apiKey-from-example ever misbehaves. Three carried reflexes that worked cleanly this session. (a) R2 omit-when-NULL: a configurable default threaded above getSpeciesMinBreedingAge/getSpeciesGestation must NOT be passed as a bare NULL (rep(NULL, n) empties the result, Learning 173); the clean pattern is an if (is.null(default)) accessor(...) else accessor(..., default = default) at the call site, so the accessor’s built-in 2.0/210 applies for no-config. (b) D4 merge-in-the-reader: the accessors REPLACE, so loadSpeciesOverrides merges the user CSV onto bundled speciesGestation (user wins per-species; unlisted keep bundled) and returns the full merged table for BOTH breedingTable and gestationTable (the accessors pick their own columns). (c) Verify every RED numeric target with standalone R BEFORE writing the test (FM #11/Learning 6): I recomputed every cohort/correction expected value (B1-B4 unit fixtures, C1-C5 on qcPed via the real correction fn) with independent base-R before writing the assertions – zero targets were wrong at GREEN, and the species-augmented qcPed trick (add species="RHESUS", custom table male 2 vs bundled 4) let the breedingTable/gestationTable params be exercised through reportGV on the otherwise species-less qcPed. IMPLEMENTATION session: code + tests + close-out docs on branch issue-73-part2-slice1-gva-overrides; NOT published (PR is a separate session, FM #18/#25); the heavy slice (reader + 3 signatures + module + appServer) fit one session. (S186, implement issue #73 Part 2 Slice 1)

Learning 175 – A clean devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) (0/0/0) does NOT guarantee spelling::spell_check_package(".") = 0: the package’s spelling check (tests/spelling.R -> spell_check_test(vignettes = TRUE, error = FALSE, skip_on_cran = TRUE)) is SKIPPED under --as-cran (which devtools::check runs), so an unrecognized PROSE word in an .Rd/man page or NEWS ships clean through the implementing session’s build-equivalent. The publish session’s independent spell_check_package(".") run (Learning 159) is the safety net – run it, do not infer spelling-clean from a 0/0/0 check. Publishing issue #73 Part 2 Slice 1 (S187): S186’s close-out reported the build-equivalent 0/0/0 but did NOT run spell_check_package, so the noun fallbacks (introduced in S186’s man/loadSpeciesOverrides.Rd roxygen) shipped as an unrecognized word – the publish session’s baseline spell_check_package(".") flagged exactly it. Fixed within publish scope by adding fallbacks to inst/WORDLIST (the only lever – there is no tests/spelling.Rout.save, Learning 159), then re-running to confirm 0. Two reflexes that kept the NEWS edit 0-delta: (a) write NEWS prose in the project’s existing CLEAN idiom – the verb form “fall back” / “the values used for species not in the table” rather than the noun “fallback(s)” the dictionary lacks – and backtick every identifier (spell_check_package strips code spans), so the two new bullets added ZERO new flagged words (verified before AND after); (b) the WORDLIST addition is test/check infrastructure, not a user-facing package change, so it earns a CHANGELOG line, NOT a NEWS line ([[news-vs-changelog]], Learning 155). Learning 171’s self-frame-supersede prediction held exactly: 171 named “the next such case is #73 Part 2’s ‘now user-configurable’, which will supersede this very bullet’s ‘Making these values user-configurable is the remaining part of issue #73’.” The Slice 1 Changes bullet self-framed (“…are now user-configurable through the configuration file … This makes the Genetic Value Analysis configurable; making the Potential Parents tab configurable is the remaining part of issue #73”) – narrowing the prior “remaining part” to the Potential Parents tab – with the Part 1 bullet left UNEDITED (no FM #20/#22, no history rewrite). The rendered \#73 escape is normal pandoc, not a render trap (Learning 171b). Publish mechanics otherwise textbook (146/152/155/157/157a/159/161): master==origin/master==32155f3a verified FIRSTHAND so no push-master-first; PR diff Slice-1-only (3 commits: S186 feat 2ec4c329 + S186 close-out 81319c10 + S187 NEWS 7e4404e8); all 10 CI checks PASS re-queried FRESH non-watch (Learning 157b – never trust the --watch exit); AskUserQuestion-gated merge with a fresh OPEN/MERGEABLE/CLEAN + headRefOid==local re-check; ancestor-gated reset --hard (both old-master AND branch tip asserted ancestors of merge commit 54b11740, Learning 146); verified-merged-before-delete (gh api 404); the S187 1B stub stash-carried OUT of the PR and popped clean onto master. Issue #73 stays OPEN – Slice 1 (GVA tab) is live; Slice 2 (Potential Parents tab) remains, and its PR will use “Closes #73”. (S187, publish issue #73 Part 2 Slice 1)

Learning 176 – When you add an argument to a Shiny moduleServer whose name collides with an existing local reactive in that module, the local binding SHADOWS the argument inside every closure created after it, so references resolve to the reactive object (a function), not the argument value – a SILENT bug (no error; the value just goes wrong). Implementing issue #73 Part 2 Slice 2 (the user-configurable gestation override for the Potential Parents tab): modPotentialParentsServer already had a local gestationDefault <- reactive({...}) that is ALSO its public returned key (list(..., gestationDefault = gestationDefault), pinned by test_modPotentialParents.R). Adding a same-named gestationDefault = NULL argument would have made pedigreeGestationDefault(ped, gestationDefault = gestationDefault) inside that reactive pass the REACTIVE OBJECT, not the argument – getSpeciesGestation would receive a function as default. Fix: rename the INTERNAL reactive (gestationDefaultReactive) and keep the public returned key gestationDefault = gestationDefaultReactive; the argument is now unshadowed and resolves correctly. Do NOT instead dodge the shadow by capturing the arg into a local at module setup (gd <- gestationDefault at the top of the moduleServer body): that EAGERLY forces the argument promise during module CONSTRUCTION, and for a boot-loaded override (appServer passes shared$speciesOverrides$gestationDefault, populated by an observe that has NOT run when the module is built) the forced value is the pre-boot NULL. The argument MUST stay a lazily-forced promise – first forced inside the prefill reactive, which fires on pedigree load (observeEvent(pedigree(), ...) has ignoreNULL = TRUE, so it does not fire at boot), well after the boot observe has populated shared$speciesOverrides. So: rename the reactive, do not pre-capture the arg. R2 reused unchanged – thread the override default to getSpeciesGestation via omit-when-NULL (if (is.null(gestationDefault)) accessor(species, gestationTable = ...) else accessor(species, gestationTable = ..., default = gestationDefault)), never a bare NULL. Phase-3E config-completeness gotcha (cost me one smoke run): a config file that EXISTS but lacks the fixed site-config keys (center/baseUrl/…) crashes appServer boot – NOT via the Slice 2 wiring but via the PRE-EXISTING ORIP-tab line getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE) (appServer.R:294), which strict-parses the file and STOPs on a missing center (the tolerant loadSiteConfig() at boot :70 is a separate, tryCatch-wrapped call). An override-only config is NOT a supported file; a real config is the full bundled example site config PLUS the appended override keys (exactly how S186 ran Slice 1’s boot smoke). Run the appServer Phase-3E boot with file.copy(system.file("extdata","example_nprcgenekeepr_config",package="nprcgenekeepr")) + appended speciesOverridesPath/gestationDefault, not a minimal stub. The plain-param wiring (owner-chosen over a Slice-1-style reactive param, to match the existing gestationTable seam and preserve the #46 tests) works precisely because the override arg is forced lazily AFTER boot: verified firsthand – shared$speciesOverrides populated at boot (RHESUS gestation 999, gestationDefault 300, unlisted CYNOMOLGUS kept bundled 170), module prefill 999 (override) / 300 (species-less fallback), and no-config boot 210 (byte-identical to today). D5 prefill-only scope was already structurally true (the module forces maxGestationalPeriod non-NULL, so getPotentialParents never consults the table for the computed window) – Slice 2 touched only the prefill, never R/modPotentialParents.R:242. (S188, implement issue #73 Part 2 Slice 2)

Learning 177 – codecov/patch is PATCH-COMPOSITION-sensitive: a small publish patch can FAIL codecov/patch even when the identical wiring PASSED in a larger earlier slice, because patch coverage is the percentage of changed executable lines hit – a handful of structurally-uncovered glue lines dominates a small diff but is diluted in a large one. Publishing issue #73 Part 2 Slice 2 (S189): the FRESH non-watch gh pr checks 79 (Learning 157b) returned exit 1 with codecov/patch red – 76.93% of diff hit vs auto target 90.01% – while all 9 R CMD check/lint/pkgdown/test-coverage jobs passed and codecov/project passed (89.99%, -0.02%, flat). The shortfall was the 2 new appServer boot-wiring lines (gestationTable/gestationDefault passed to modPotentialParentsServer), which covr CANNOT execute – appServer is never run under coverage; S188 verified that wiring with a deparse-match test (source inspection, not execution) + the Phase-3E runtime smoke, neither of which covr counts. The module LOGIC (both pedigreeGestationDefault branches, the gestationDefaultReactive) IS covered by the executing module tests. Slice 1’s PR #78 had the SAME appServer-wiring blind spot yet codecov/patch passed at 94.24% – because Slice 1’s large, reader-heavy patch (19-test loadSpeciesOverrides reader) diluted the few uncovered lines; Slice 2’s ~13-line patch could not. So a red codecov/patch on a small publish patch is a composition artifact, NOT necessarily a deliverable defect – but PROVE it: get the codecov check-run summary (gh api repos/<o>/<r>/commits/<sha>/check-runs -q '.check_runs[]|select(.name|test("codecov"))|.output.summary' gives the “X% of diff hit (target Y%)” line), read the R diff, and attribute every uncovered line to either structural-non-execution (boot wiring, verified by other means) or a real test gap. THREE handling rules. (a) codecov/patch is not a merge gate here – master has no branch protection, so mergeStateStatus is UNSTABLE (not BLOCKED) and mergeable stays MERGEABLE; UNSTABLE = mergeable-with-non-passing-non-required-checks. Confirm via gh api repos/<o>/<r>/branches/master/protection/required_status_checks (404 = no required checks). (b) A publish session must NOT fix a red patch by adding test code – writing a test that boots appServer under coverage is implementation work (and reopens the fiddly Phase-3E getSiteInfo boot trap, Learning 176); bundling it into a publish is FM #18/#25. The right response is to SURFACE the red check + its diagnosed cause to the owner via AskUserQuestion (never merge a red check silently, never paper it over) and let them decide merge-now vs defer-for-coverage. (c) gh pr view has NO baseRefOid or (for issues) stateReason field – an invalid --json field makes the WHOLE query return empty, which a guard reads as a failure; this actually HELPED (my guarded merge refused to run on the empty values), but use only valid fields (state/mergeable/headRefOid for PRs; verify origin/master via git rev-parse separately) and verify a closed issue with state/closed/closedAt. Publish mechanics otherwise textbook (146/155/157/157a/171): NEWS Changes bullet self-framed to supersede Slice 1’s “remaining part of issue #73” line (Learning 171’s prediction, now fulfilled – “This completes issue #73”), 0-delta spelling, confined pure-insertion; master==origin/master==c512771a firsthand (no push-master-first); PR diff Slice-2-only 3 commits (S188 feat e012e6ad + S188 close-out 866b0a7f + S189 NEWS 8ce3f937); AskUserQuestion-gated merge gh pr merge 79 --merge -> 0980a028; issue #73 verified CLOSED (closedAt == merge time, “Closes #73” worked); ancestor-gated reset --hard (old-master c512771a AND tip 8ce3f937 both ancestors of 0980a028); verified-merged-before-delete (gh api 404). Issue #73 is now CLOSED – Part 1 (data) + Slice 1 (GVA tab) + Slice 2 (Potential Parents tab) all live on master. (S189, publish issue #73 Part 2 Slice 2)

Learning 178 – A backlog item / handoff that asserts a change is invasive (“reverses the documented stance”, “breaks golden invariants”, “needs replacement golden values”) states a HYPOTHESIS about blast radius, not a fact – verify it firsthand during ratification, because the clean realization of the ratified behavior is often far shallower than the issue assumed. Ratifying issue #76 Reading A (de-inflate genome uniqueness gu for both-unknown founders): the issue body AND the S189 handoff both said Reading A must change calcGU/calcA/gene-drop, reverse the R/calcGU.R:10-34 stance, and supply replacement golden values (issue acceptance criterion #1). A 10-agent research + adversarial-critique workflow (wf_67d4c94a-691) plus firsthand pipeline reads showed the owner-ratified “decline-to-credit” (report unknown-origin founders’ gu as 0) has a REPORT-LAYER realization in reportGV (~3-5 lines after classifyParentage at reportGV.R:184, before the cbind/$gu) that changes NONE of those functions or their golden tests and does NOT reverse the documented stance – same displayed numbers, far lower risk. The ratification deliverable legitimately concluded the issue OVER-SCOPED the change: criterion #1’s “replacement gene-drop goldens” became “zero gene-drop changes + new report-layer tests.” Treat “this is invasive” as the question the ratification answers, not a given. (a) The targeting trap, generalized – the classification set is NOT the gene-drop founder set. For unknown-parent work the U-id-aware “both unknown” classification set (classifyParentage.R:21: is.na(x) | isGeneratedUnknownId(x)) differs from the gene-drop isFounder/getFounders set: after addParents synthesizes U-stub rows with NA parents, a U-id-parented proband is “both unknown” by classification but a NON-founder at the gene-drop level (it inherits from its U-stub parents), while a literal-NA animal IS a gene-drop founder. Target the classification/report set where origin is known (= orderReport’s noParentage bucket, orderReport.R:38-62), NOT the gene-drop set. Mis-targeting the gene-drop set was the fatal flaw that made candidate A2 (tag phantom alleles at the mint point) unsound – it marks EVERY founder, not the Undetermined subset. (b) Decline-to-credit resolves to a single value. Because the target animals have BOTH parents unknown, all their gene-drop alleles trace to phantom mints (assignAlleles.R:33-36), so “exclude the artifact alleles” = gu -> 0 (no formula to tune). Apply to BOTH the report gu column and the $gu element for one coherent reported statistic; never write NA (it poisons rank()/order()); the Slice-3 demotion (flag-driven on value=="Undetermined", rankSubjects.R:37-44) composes on top unchanged, and imports (both-unknown WITH a recorded origin) are preserved. (c) Adversarial-candidate review earns its keep on a stance-reversal ratification. The workflow killed 3 of 4 candidates with VERIFIED fatal flaws – A4 rescales the wrong term (the inflation is in the numerator rowSums(rare) at calcGU.R:91, not the /(2L*iterations) denominator); A3 injects per-iteration randomness and breaks the seeded gene-drop goldens; A2 mis-targets all founders – and refined the survivor A1 (target noParentage, not the full “both unknown” set). Ratification is a docs-only DESIGN deliverable -> TDD code-phases N/A, Phase-3E N/A; recorded in docs/planning/issue76-gu-deinflation-ratification.md in the §8-E house style (provenance/authority header, load-bearing finding, authoritative rule, RED/GREEN charter, rejected candidates, firsthand anchors), with a one-line cross-ref added to the issue-9 plan §8-F; committed direct to local master then pushed to origin ([[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]); do NOT bundle with the implementation (FM #18/#25 – the ratify -> implement -> publish arc is 3 sessions min). (S190, ratify issue #76 Reading A)

Learning 179 – A gene-drop NON-founder’s genome uniqueness (gu) is structurally 0 at the default guThresh = 1, because every allele it carries is inherited from a parent that is ALSO in the analysed population, so no allele it holds is held by only one animal. This matters when writing a RED test that a U-id-PARENTED both-unknown animal (sire/dam are generated U-ids like “U0001”, not literal NA) gets de-inflated to gu = 0 (issue #76 Reading A – proving the predicate’s U-id-awareness): such a proband is “both unknown” by classifyParentage (is.na(x) | isGeneratedUnknownId(x)) but a NON-founder at the gene-drop level (Learning 178a – after addParents it inherits from its U-stub parents), so at guThresh = 1 its gu is ALREADY 0 and de-inflating it is INVISIBLE – the assertion expect_equal(gu["P1"], 0) would pass on the UNCHANGED code (not a real RED). Use guThresh = 2 (an allele held by <= 2 animals counts as rare): each of the proband’s two inherited alleles is then held by exactly {proband, parent} = 2 animals, so it counts as rare and the proband carries a strictly-positive gu (verified firsthand = 100 on the pre-change reportGV), making the de-inflation observable and the test RED-meaningful. Verified the threshold firsthand by running reportGV(fixture, guIter = 1000L, guThresh = thr) for thr in 1/2/3 BEFORE writing assertions (FM #11/Learning 6): at thr=1 the U-id proband is 0 (literal-NA founders 26-50), at thr=2 founders AND the U-id proband are all > 0 (the sweet spot), at thr=3 everything saturates to 100. General test-design rule: when a RED test must observe a value being DRIVEN to 0, first confirm the value is NON-zero on the unchanged code under the exact fixture+params, or the “failing” assertion silently passes. Robustness: assert only the DISCRIMINATING properties – == 0 for de-inflation targets (RNG-independent), > 0 for preserved imports/known animals (the guard that proves targeting), and $report gu == $gu element (same-run internal consistency) – NEVER exact seeded gu magnitudes, so the test survives RNG / R-version differences; a fixture WITH an origin column is REQUIRED to exercise the import-preservation branch (qcPed and makeValidTestPed both lack origin, so they only cover the no-origin -> all-Undetermined path). The GREEN realization was exactly the ratified ~12 lines (origin if/else mirroring orderReport.R:43-47, the undetermined mask, gu$gu[undetermined] <- 0.0) after reportGV.R:184 before the cbind; mutating the ONE gu data.frame covered BOTH the report column and $gu; all existing goldens held UNMODIFIED exactly as S190 predicted (verified: full regression 1225/0/0, devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) 0/0/0, calcGU/calcA/geneDrop untouched). Re-read modGeneticValue.R:224-231 firsthand (S190 gotcha #8) and confirmed the Slice-3 demotion keys on value == "Undetermined" NOT on gu magnitude, so de-inflating gu to 0 leaves the displayed ranking unchanged – the two corrections COMPOSE (Phase-3E: Undetermined ranked last 6-10/10 with gu 0, imports preserved at top). REFACTOR fixed only the 2 style lints GREEN introduced (line-wrap; 0 -> 0.0 for the double gu column, implicit_integer_linter); the 2 object_usage_linter warnings on classifyParentage/correctUnknownParentMeanKinship are pre-existing single-file false positives that resolve under lint_package. (S191, implement issue #76 Reading A)

Learning 180 – To clear a Shiny fileInput (reset BOTH the re-read behavior AND the displayed file name) WITHOUT adding a client-side dependency (shinyjs), combine three server-side moves: (1) render the file input dynamically – output$xUI <- renderUI({ key(); fileInput(session$ns("x"), ...) }) keyed by a reactiveVal counter, and bump the counter on “clear” so a FRESH empty widget replaces the old one (this is what clears the displayed file name – base shiny has no updateFileInput; the input id MUST be session$ns()-namespaced because renderUI content is not auto-namespaced inside a module); (2) blank companion text inputs with updateTextAreaInput(session, ..., value = ""); (3) hold a server-side “cleared” guard – clearedFilePath/clearedText reactiveVals set on clear, and skip a read when the current value is identical() to the cleared one (a newly chosen file gets a new temp datapath, and any edited text differs, so genuine new input still loads; the one accepted edge is re-entering the EXACT cleared string, which the guard blocks). Implementing issue #1 (Pedigree Browser “Clear Focal Animals” left a loaded CSV that silently re-read on the next Update and kept showing the file name; the list-clearing checkbox itself had worked since 2020). The guard (moves 2/3) is what makes the behavior UNIT-testable via shiny::testServer – assert through the module’s returned reactive that a cleared file/text is not re-read and that NEW input still loads (load a tempfile CSV, Update, clear+Update, then a 3rd Update with the SAME focalAnimalFile/focalAnimalIds still set = the browser-not-reset state -> stays empty); a UI-structure test (as.character(modPedigreeUI("id")) no longer contains type="file" but DOES contain the uiOutput container id) covers move 1 RED-first. What testServer canNOT verify is exactly the client-side half – the file name actually disappearing from the widget and the updateTextAreaInput round-trip – so that half needs an E2E (shinytest2 + chromote) assertion. Running this project’s opt-in E2E suite locally for real-browser Phase-3E evidence needs BOTH env vars: NPRC_RUN_E2E=true (the create_test_app() opt-in gate, helper-shinytest2.R) AND NOT_CRAN=true (else skip_on_cran() skips EVERY e2e test – a plain Rscript ... testthat::test_file(...) run silently skipped 9/9 until NOT_CRAN was set; this is the same NOT_CRAN condition the [regression-read] glossary names, just as critical for the e2e suite). chromote + Chrome ARE installed on this machine, so the browser tests DO run when both vars are set (verified: 9 pedigree e2e tests, 13 assertions, all pass; the new test reads the file-name display via app$get_js on the .input-group input[type=text] value and the textarea via app$get_value(input = "pedigree-focalAnimalIds"), confirming the raw tags$textarea(id = ns("focalAnimalIds")) binds as a normal shiny text input). E2E added to a behavior ALREADY implemented is a VERIFICATION test (passes on first run), not strict RED-first – be explicit about that; the RED-first coverage of the reset MECHANISM is the testServer UI-structure test (move 1). REFACTOR was correctly SKIPPED (owner-gated) – lintr clean on the changed R + test files, no duplication. Implement session: code + tests + close-out on branch issue-1-clear-focal-animals, NOT published (FM #18/#25; publish PR uses “Closes #1” + a NEWS Changes bullet, Learning 157a). (S193, implement issue #1 – clear focal animals)

Learning 181 – In a PLANNING session whose design rests on non-trivial statistics or empirical claims, an adversarial-critique workflow phase that ACTUALLY runs checks against bundled data is high-ROI: it catches plausible-but-wrong claims BEFORE they enter the plan and misdirect the implementer. Planning issue #2 (evidence-based GVA gene-drop iteration-count advice). A 7-agent investigate/design/critique workflow (wf_fdb7c410-95f, ultracode) produced a first design pass with two confident-but-wrong claims that the adversarial critic – prompted to REFUTE and to probe qcPed empirically – overturned: (1) the “homozygous-rare animals inflate the per-animal Monte Carlo SE by up to sqrt(2), so multiply needed N by (1+rho)” claim is FALSE at the default guThresh = 1 – a rare allele has population frequency <= 1 (a single copy in the whole population), so a homozygote can never carry one; per-column rare counts are effectively {0,1} and the Bernoulli SE sqrt(p(1-p)/(2K)) is adequate (slightly conservative), so guidance that would have ~doubled iteration counts was unfounded; (2) the rank-stability machinery was motivated by rank churn that does not measurably exist on the only bundled real pedigree – qcPed is degenerate-bimodal (~56% of probands at gu=0 incl. the issue #76 de-inflated set, ~43% near gu=50, ~2/280 in the mid-range; Spearman = 1.0 at every N >= 125), so a TDD test of “recommend a small N” on qcPed passes TAUTOLOGICALLY – a purpose-built dense-mid-range fixture must be built in RED FIRST or the test proves nothing. The critic also corrected a code mis-description (the GVA displayed rank is the bucketed orderReport/rankSubjects pipeline – gu > 10 cutoff, -trunc(gu), z <= 0.25 – NOT rank(indivMeanKin - gu)), which moved the convergence diagnostic from generic Spearman to boundary-density counts.
