Waited for the full R-CMD-check matrix (macOS/Windows/Ubuntu release + oldrel-1 + devel) + pkgdown + test-coverage + both codecov to go green; the devel leg finished last (22:58:53 success) and only then did I merge. The lone lint red was proven pre-existing whole-package noise via gh api repos/.../check-runs/<JOB>/annotations (12 findings: 10 in data-raw/fgSEValidation.R, 2 .github workflow warnings; NONE in this PR’s changed files) – the documented “surface the lone red with evidence” step (207(1)/210(1)). (2) Closing-keyword proof can be AIRTIGHT, not just “probably safe”. git log origin/master..branch --format='%H%n%s%n%b' | grep -nE '#[0-9]+' returned EMPTY – NO branch commit carried any #N ref at all (they write the bare-word “(issue 95)”), so the lone “close-out” token in the S230 subject is provably inert (a closing keyword fires only when IMMEDIATELY followed by #N). The rendered PR body grepped empty for clos|fix|resolv. And I authored the merge commit message keyword-free and ref-bare: gh pr merge 97 --merge --subject "Merge pull request #97 from rmsharp/issue95-optionC-slice2" --body "option C Slice 2: ... prepareKinshipOverrides helper (issue 95)" – so the merge commit that lands on master cannot fire (S229’s 3rd firing was exactly a close-out commit message). Post-merge gh api repos/O/R/issues/95 --jq .state -> open (the end-check that caught all of S229’s firings). Bright-line, three surfaces: branch commits (narrow adjacency test, they’re frozen) + PR body (authored empty) + the merge commit message you author at merge time (--subject/--body, keyword-free, ref-bare). (3) Local sync = stash-the-stub (Learning 210(2)), preceded by a git status re-check (Learning 215). Confirmed only the 1B stub + standing untracked HTML were dirty (no unexplained modified tracked file), then git stash push -- SESSION_NOTES.md -> git fetch -> git switch master -> git merge --ff-only origin/master -> git stash pop (clean pop – post-FF master’s SESSION_NOTES == the stash base). Verified master == origin/master == e893c90d and the Slice-2 code is present. The marker survived; no reset --hard near a possibly-owner-edited file. (4) NEW env nugget: status is a READ-ONLY special variable in zsh (the session shell, initialized from the user profile). A run_in_background poll script that did status=$(gh api ... --jq .status) died immediately with (eval):3: read-only variable: status (exit 1). Use neutral names (st, cc, concl) in ANY Bash snippet here; other zsh specials to avoid assigning include path, cdpath, pipestatus. The intended mechanism was sound: a run_in_background loop that polls a CI check-run and breaks on status == completed exits and re-invokes the agent – the right way to wait on an EXTERNAL CI leg the harness cannot otherwise notify on (here the devel leg finished before the relaunch was even needed, so a foreground re-check sufficed). (5) 1-and-done held: branch deletion stays the deferred SEPARATE hygiene step (Learning 208/210(4)) – left issue95-optionC-slice2 (local + remote) for the next session; did not bundle. General rule: on any arc with a Learning number, RE-READ that learning before executing the Nth time – routine is where regressions hide, and the documented controls (annotations-API proof, both-surfaces + merge-commit closing-keyword discipline with the post-merge end-check, stash-the-stub sync) are exactly what turn a triple-firing arc into a clean one. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 207/210/215/217 (the careful-admin arc) and integrates Learning 216 (the S230 slice it merged). This was an admin/merge session – TDD code-phases N/A. (S232, issue #95 option C Slice 2 – merged PR #97)

Learning 219 – The deferred branch-hygiene step (Learning 208/210(4)) is a clean ~5-command admin task when you verify-merged-firsthand against BOTH refs first; the -D-forcing “ahead 1” quirk does NOT bite a branch whose tip is a clean ancestor of master. Owner picked the deferred hygiene step from S232’s SUGGESTED NEXT: delete the merged branch issue95-optionC-slice2 (local + remote). Admin/branch-hygiene session – TDD code-phases N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. Third run of the S229->S230(a) deferred-hygiene pattern (slice1 deleted in S230(a); slice2 now). (1) Verify-merged-firsthand = four firsthand checks, not one. Before any delete: (a) git merge-base --is-ancestor <tip> master AND (b) git merge-base --is-ancestor <tip> origin/master – BOTH must pass (a branch merged locally but not on origin, or vice-versa, is a partial state); (c) local branch tip == origin/<branch> tip (identical, not diverged); (d) git log --oneline master..<branch> empty (no commits on the branch absent from master). Here the tip 421f80f2 passed all four against master == origin/master == cd20817e. (2) Safe git branch -d succeeded – the documented -D quirk did NOT apply. Learning 207/208 warn that a merged branch can show “ahead 1” (e.g. squash/rebase merges) so git branch -d refuses and you must confirm with git merge-base --is-ancestor <tip> master then -D. That quirk bites only when the branch tip is NOT a literal ancestor of master; here the PR #97 merge was a true merge commit so the tip IS an ancestor, and the safe -d (which refuses unless merged) exited 0. Rule: always try -d first; only reach for the merge-base-check-then--D path if -d actually refuses. (3) The remote delete is outward-facing -> confirm the EXACT command via AskUserQuestion before running it, then VERIFY removal. Did the local -d first (reversible – the commit lives on master, recreatable via git branch <name> <tip>), then asked the owner to confirm git push origin --delete issue95-optionC-slice2 before running it. After the push: git ls-remote origin <branch> returns EMPTY (= gone on GitHub) and git push origin --delete also prunes the local origin/<branch> remote-tracking ref (confirmed absent from git branch -r). GitHub did NOT auto-delete the branch on PR merge, which is why this step was needed at all. (4) 1-and-done / scope held: deleted ONLY the named merged branch; left the other stale branches (dev, module, rlabkey-version-floor) untouched (out of scope, owner did not ask). This closes the option C Slice 2 arc end-to-end: S230 RED/GREEN -> S231 push+PR -> S232 merge -> S233 hygiene. Carried as applied: [[check-status-before-destructive-git]] (re-checked git status/verify-merged before any delete), [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (read the arc’s learnings 208/210/215/218 first), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]] (close-out pushed to origin/master so local==origin); extends Learning 208/210(4) (branch hygiene as the deferred separate step) and 207 (the -D quirk) and completes the arc of 216/217/218. This was an admin/branch-hygiene session – TDD code-phases N/A. (S233, issue #95 option C Slice 2 – merged branch deleted)

Learning 220 – A firsthand numeric check is necessary but NOT sufficient to ground a genetics/statistics decision: you must also verify WHERE each quantity enters the computation (the data-flow), or you can ratify a directionally-wrong model with confident-looking numbers. Owner picked live-thread #2 -> a /grill-me on #95 follow-up rule (ii) (partial-residual / scaled add-back), bundled with C1.2. Decision/design session – TDD code-phases N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. The grill REVERSED ~5 sessions of shipped, owner-ratified work (D11 + option C Slices 1-2): the ratified disposition is to revert the override-triggered suppression of the +sexMean/2 prior to keep-all and REMOVE the option-C machinery (missingSideFor column, classifyOverrideMissingSide(), the suppress path). (1) The reframing and how it was found. Grounded the grill with a 4-agent read-only workflow (wf_1a1f64ba-976: numeric on qcPed + code/math + C1.2 scope + conservation-genetics methodology), then verified its load-bearing claim firsthand BEFORE presenting it – the claim overturns ratified D11, so trusting the subagents would have been reckless: overrides are written into kmat (prepareKinshipOverrides.R:49) BEFORE meanKinship (reportGV.R:148) and BEFORE the correction (reportGV.R:157). So a missing-side override pins ONE of N(=280) relationships and that value is ALREADY in original; the +sexMean/2 prior estimates the AGGREGATE of all N missing-side relationships, so observing one should shrink it by only ~1/N (~0.0048 SD), not drop the whole ~1.33 SD. Full-drop (rule i) – and its parent D11 blanket supersession – over-correct by ~N (≈280×), swinging an affected animal’s GV rank a median 86/280 the WRONG way (override animals look more genetically valuable). (2) Why S227’s “firsthand-numeric-grounded /grill-me” still missed it. S227 measured the wrong quantity – the override’s direct ΔMK / prior (≈10%, legitimately “new information”) – and never checked that the override value is ALREADY in original before the correction. A magnitude is not a data-flow proof. Rule: when a correction TERM is a scalar aggregate over N relationships and an override pins one of them already-in-the-matrix, the term should shrink by ~1/N; ground the decision on where each quantity ENTERS the computation, not just its size. (3) A /grill-me can legitimately conclude “revert recently-shipped, ratified work” – a valid, high-value outcome, not a failure; the remedy for a directionally-wrong genetics model is the reversal, documented. (4) Methodology corroborated: no conservation-genetics literature supports a partial-residual estimator; PMx (Hauser 2024) replaces one cell and recomputes, never lumping the prior into a scalar – which issue-#13 override-the-cell ALREADY does for observed pairs, so PMx is documented as considered-and-not-needed (D5). The +sexMean/2 prior is itself a package-only addition, not in Vinson & Raboin 2015. (5) Deliverable discipline (1-and-done): wrote the decision record + Phase-3 revert plan WITH the mandatory evidence-based grep inventory, the behavior invariant (keep-all), per-phase completion criteria, and dragons into issue95-optionC-targeted-suppression-plan.md §9 (+ banner + §8E “resolved” note) and a superseded-in-part pointer at D11 in issue13-kinship-overrides-plan.md; did NOT implement the revert (separate strict-TDD session). Closing-keyword discipline held on the issue comment (authored so the broad clos|fix|resolv grep is EMPTY; posted via gh api; #95 verified OPEN after). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]] (presented the reframing + a recommendation per decision, let the owner ratify each of D1-D5 rather than deciding for them), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (read §8 before re-running the numeric check – did the delta, not a full re-run), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 216 (the option-C slice this reverses) and supersedes in part the S214 D11 / S227 option-C ratification. This was a /grill-me DECISION/DESIGN session – TDD code-phases N/A. (S234, issue #95 rule (ii) -> revert option C to keep-all)

Learning 221 – When IMPLEMENTING a revert of shipped machinery, ground it with a read-only workflow that re-verifies the plan’s grep inventory firsthand against the CURRENT tree; a revert plan’s line-ranges reliably under-specify the mechanical SHAPE (braced-block guards vs the one-liner shown, trailing-comma cleanups when you remove a last argument, @noRd docstrings with NO generated .Rd safety net, and params that fall unused after a deletion). Owner picked “S234’s suggested next” = the strict-TDD revert of option C / D11 to keep-all (plan §9C). Strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session; RED 034de14b -> GREEN cae27e94 -> REFACTOR none; all gates via AskUserQuestion; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. (1) Verify the inventory firsthand even when the plan ships one. S234’s §9C carried a firsthand grep inventory; I still re-ran it via a 19-agent read-only workflow (wf_9298eddb-3a4) against current master. It confirmed completeness (empty planRefsNotFound; per-file missingSideFor counts matched exactly) AND surfaced what the plan’s line-ranges glossed: the suppress guard is a braced 8-line block at :168-175 (not the one-liner the plan TEXT showed; :166-167 are KEEP code), removing a last argument needs a trailing-comma fix on the preceding line, the prepareKinshipOverrides @noRd docstring has no generated .Rd to catch staleness (hand-edit required), and deleting the classifyOverrideMissingSide call left ped/candidateIds unused. A plan’s grep inventory is necessary but a revert edits the SHAPE of code (commas, braces, docstrings) the inventory does not capture – re-grounding firsthand is what catches it. (2) Surface the author’s-call forks a revert leaves open as a pre-RED AskUserQuestion. Two the plan deliberately left (“rejects/ignores”, plus the unflagged unused params): drop-vs-keep the now-unused ped/candidateIds (chose DROP – @noRd internal, completes the revert, costs +2 call-site edits) and ignore-vs-reject a stray missingSideFor column (chose IGNORE – the true revert ADDS no code; an old 4-column file still validates). Both change the RED tests, so settle them BEFORE declaring RED ([[observation-vs-decision]]). (3) The discriminating RED per kind of removal. A behavior FLIP (suppress->keep-all): flip the oracle (expect_equal(imk[[X]], original[[X]]) -> expect_gt(...)) and rename the test’s independent-model helper to the new semantics (i13_correctBlanketA -> i13_correctKeepAll, dropping its suppress branch). A removed PARAM: a signature guard expect_error(f(..., overriddenIds=...), "unused argument") (fails now – the param exists; passes after removal). A removed RETURN field: expect_null(res$suppressIds) + expect_named(res, "kmat"). A removed INPUT-validation path: expect_error(check(frameWithStrayCol), NA) (errors now via the domain check; passes after under ignore). RED confirmed each fails against the unreverted code with NO collateral parse error (reportGV 2, prepareKinshipOverrides 7, docs 4, correct* 1, check* 1). (4) Commit to EXACT doc phrases in RED and honor them in GREEN. test_kinshipOverrideDocs greps the rendered helpText/HTML; I pinned “missing one parent”/“kept”/“underestimate” in RED and wrote exactly those into the modGeneticValue.R helpText + genetic_value.html (and, for consistency, NEWS) in GREEN – the doc-consistency test (OC-R13, in reverse) couples code+docs so they move together. (5) Lockstep via the shared helper, not parallel edits. reportGV/gvaConvergence both route through prepareKinshipOverrides; I reverted the helper once (2-arg, list(kmat)) and made the identical 2-line edit at each call site – did NOT parallelize the GREEN source edits (interdependent: the helper signature change must match both call sites; read-before-edit each). (6) Phase-3E for a Shiny-doc + analytical revert, headless: (a) app-boot – shiny::shinyApp(appUI(), appServer) inherits "shiny.appobj"; (b) live-UI – as.character(appUI()) greps for the new keep-all copy AND the ABSENCE of missingSideFor; (c) behavior – reportGV with an override on a one-unknown animal keeps its +sexMean/2 (0.0092 > 0.0060) and applies the cell-write. NEWS.md is generated from NEWS.Rmd via rmarkdown::render (github_document) – edit the .Rmd, render, then inspect the diff for pandoc reflow artifacts (an & + a trailing parenthetical wrapped oddly; reworded to “and” + a plain sentence to fix it). (7) 1-and-done: the deliverable is the implementation, verified locally on branch issue95-revert-keepall; push + PR “Relates to #95” is the outward-facing NEXT session (the S231->S233 arc). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (fixed the 3 prose lines I authored at 81 chars; left the files’ pervasive long test_that titles as the established in-file style), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]] (close-out committed on the branch; reaches master at merge); implements Learning 220 (the S234 decision) and reverses Learning 214/216 (the option-C slices). This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session. (S235, issue #95 – revert option C to keep-all)

Learning 222 – When two deferred follow-ups share one structural blocker, a single /grill-me settles both, and “don’t build” is a definitive, high-value disposition – but ground a “don’t build” decision the same way you ground a “build” one (verify WHERE each quantity enters / whether the premise is even observable), not just that the effect is small. Owner picked “#95 follow-ups 2 / 3”; bundled both via a pre-grill scope AskUserQuestion (owner’s call). Decision/design session – TDD code-phases N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. Output = ratified D1-D4 in issue95-optionC-targeted-suppression-plan.md §10 (+ a follow-up-resolution pointer at D11 in issue13-kinship-overrides-plan.md). Dispositions: FU2 (both-unknown->one-unknown promotion) = won’t-build (not derivable); FU3 (shared-unknown-parent sib coupling) = accept (keep the independent per-focal loop) + document in the maintainer docstring. (1) The path-agnostic blocker is the SHARED through-line – which is why bundling worked. Both follow-ups die on the same structural fact: a kinship override carries no side information and an unrecorded parent has no id. FU2 can’t tell which of two missing parents an override informs; FU3 can’t tell whether two one-unknown animals share the same unknown parent. One grounding pass on the path-agnostic schema + the pipeline ordering serves both – when deferred follow-ups share a blocker, grill them together. (2) Verify the PREMISE is observable, not just that the effect is small (Learning 220 applied to a non-build). The decisive FU3 finding came from running the detection firsthand on qcPed: the 2 candidate sib-pairs (KY0D3C/HN5YTI; JPKPJC/PUS6EL) each share a known dam and both miss the sire – so whether they share the same unknown sire is unknowable (two offspring of one dam can have different unknown sires). “Negligible” was the grounding agents’ framing; “the premise is undetectable” is the stronger, load-bearing reason – and it only surfaced by checking what the data can identify, not the magnitude. For FU2, likewise: confirmed firsthand that the override is already in the matrix before the prior (so promotion would over-credit) and that no schema column carries side (so promotion isn’t derivable) – a “don’t build” grounded on data-flow + observability, not on “it’s rare.” (3) A grounding workflow’s SYNTHESIS step can degenerate to placeholder output – a schema guarantees SHAPE, not CONTENT; keep the load-bearing data in the return value. wf_964f8ea1-4bb: 6 read-only grounding agents (Explore/haiku) returned real structured findings, but the final synthesis agent (opus, 392s, 11 tool calls over a ~500k-token JSON input) satisfied the schema with junk (summary:"test", question:"q"). The script returned only synthesis, so the real grounding was nearly lost – recovered it from the agent transcripts (subagents/workflows/<wf>/agent-*.jsonl -> the last StructuredOutput tool_use input). Lessons: (a) return the raw grounding array too, not just the synthesis; (b) a single synthesis agent over a huge JSON is a failure point – synthesize yourself when you’ll run the grill anyway; (c) transcript recovery is the reliable fallback when a workflow’s return value is degraded. (4) Decision/implementation split held (FM #18). D3 scopes the FU3 note to a one-file @noRd maintainer docstring as a SEPARATE small session; wrote the recommended exact docstring text + insertion point (correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:102, before @param) + verify steps (no .Rd to regenerate; hand-add any wordlist term, not update_wordlist) into §10C so the implementer has it. Did NOT implement. D4: #95 closes when that note lands (deliberate, keyword-safe), not this session. (5) Closing-keyword discipline on the #95 comment (authored so the broad clos|fix|resolv grep is EMPTY; posted via gh api; #95 verified OPEN after). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]] (a recommendation per decision; owner ratified each of D1-D4), [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (re-verified the load-bearing claims firsthand against current master rather than trusting the haiku grounding agents), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (read §8/§9 + the prior-analysis grounding before re-deriving – did the delta), [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]] (recorded in §10C), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 220 (the over-correction reframing, here applied to FU2/FU3) and closes the #95 follow-up arc end-to-end (FU1/rule ii/C1.2 at S234; FU2/FU3 at S236). This was a /grill-me DECISION/DESIGN session – TDD code-phases N/A. (S236, issue #95 – follow-ups 2 & 3 dispositioned)

Learning 223 – The implementation tail of a deliberate decision/implementation split (FM #18) is a clean ~mechanical session when the deciding session pre-writes the exact text + insertion point + verify steps. S236 split the FU3 disposition into a separate small session and wrote the recommended docstring text, the insertion point (correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:102, before @param), and the verify list into §10C; this session (S237) executed it in one pass with 0 corrections. (1) A @noRd roxygen docstring is structurally un-pinnable AND un-spell-checked – so TDD is genuinely N/A, not a dodge. roxygen comments are stripped at build and are not part of the function object, so no testthat test can reach them (confirmed firsthand: test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R pins only the live-app ui and the genetic_value.html txt, never this file – [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], not the handoff’s word); and spell_check_package checks generated man/*.Rd + DESCRIPTION + vignettes, none of which a @noRd function produces, so the docstring text is never spell-checked either. The ONLY real build risk a docstring-only R-source edit carries is the R CMD check non-ASCII NOTE. (2) Keep docstring prose ASCII and match the file’s existing roxygen markup. §10C’s “recommended text” used markdown backticks + em-dashes; normalized to \code{} (this file’s house style, cf. its \code{sexMean} spans), ASCII -- (protects the 0/0/0 non-ASCII check), and US “modeled” (cf. :85) – all three are house-style normalizations of explicitly-recommended (not verbatim-mandated) text, surfaced in the report. Verified ASCII-clean + all lines <=80 + the 3 relevant test files green before committing. (3) Deliberate, keyword-safe issue close as the session’s LAST act. Added the docstring, verified, committed, THEN set issue 95 to CLOSED via gh api with a keyword-safe comment (no clos|fix|resolv adjacent to any #N), and verified #9/#13 stay closed + 95 closed at the very end (Learning 215/217/218). This closes the #95 arc end-to-end: S234 (reframe + revert decision) -> S235 (revert impl, PR #98) -> S236 (FU2/FU3 grill) -> S237 (FU3 docstring + close). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (<=80, lint-clean changed file), [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]] (no update_wordlist), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a documentation/admin session – TDD code-phases N/A. (S237, issue #95 – FU3 docstring landed, #95 closed)

Learning 224 – A whole-package lintr pass on this package is gated by one distinction: ARTIFACT vs REAL. lint_package() run locally reports object_usage_linter “no visible global function definition for ” for intra-package calls because the package namespace is not resolved; those vanish under pkgload::load_all() (object_usage -> 0) and NEVER appear in CI, which installs the package via local::. – so the ground truth for “what is red in CI” is the actual lint.yaml run log, not the raw local count. Owner picked the carried “systematic whole-package lint pass” (clear the standing lint-CI red); remaining open issues deferred until after CRAN resubmission. REFACTOR-class cleanup – TDD RED/GREEN N/A (no behavior change; the existing suite + the lintr gate are the safety net); 0 corrections / 0 overrides. (1) Separate artifact from real TWO ways before touching anything. load_all() then lint_package() dropped 74 -> 43 (all 31 object_usage gone); the lint.yaml CI log listed exactly those 43 and zero object_usage – two independent confirmations ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]] + [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]). Left all object_usage untouched; fixed only the 43. (2) The 43 are all behavior-preserving – line_length wraps, paste(.,collapse=", ")->toString, any(is.na)->anyNA, implicit 0/2->0L/2L, any(!x)->!all(x) (equivalent for non-NA logical), gsub("\r")->fixed=TRUE (CR matches identically), nonportable_path->file.path() – verified by targeted tests (calcFG/FGSE/apply/check) + full suite (3173/0/0, only baseline-noise warnings) + devtools::check 0/0/0. (3) Reflowing EXPORTED-function roxygen regenerates man/*.Rd. Six exported-fn files re-wrapped -> devtools::document() rewrote 6 .Rd (whitespace-only, identical words; rendered help unchanged) – they MUST be committed in sync. @noRd/internal-fn reflows do NOT regenerate any .Rd (Learning 223). Anticipate the man-page commit when a reflow touches an exported fn. (4) Splitting a long sprintf format string via paste0 can SHORTEN a label literal enough to trip a linter the full string did not – paste0(" bootstrap boot/delta = %.4f ...", ...) made nonportable_path fire on “boot/delta” (a label, not a path); the full original string never tripped it. Fix: bind the whole format string to a variable (fmtB <- "...") instead of splitting. A script not run by any test/check needs an explicit parse() check after editing (no test catches a split-string typo). (5) data-raw/ dev-runner idioms whose linter “fix” would be wrong get justified # nolint ranges, owner-confirmed – source()-ing test helpers (correct for a runner) and system.time(x <- ...) (times AND captures; splitting double-runs an ~11-min study) each get a # nolint start: <linter>. / # nolint end block (an inline # nolint overflows the very 80-char limit it would satisfy). The mechanical lints in the same file (paths -> file.path(), 2L, wraps, commented-code prose) are still fixed. (6) Outward-facing arc (owner chose full via AskUserQuestion): branch -> <=5-file batched commits (SAFEGUARDS blast-radius) with a re-lint after each -> PR #99 -> all 8 CI checks green incl. the lint job (4m7s) + the full R-CMD-check matrix -> merge 8deeecf -> branch deleted (verify-merged-firsthand, Learning 219). PR body keyword-safe; no #N so nothing auto-closes. (7) Edit one file’s multiple lints bottom-up ([[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], owner tip this session) so earlier edits do not renumber later targets. Carried as applied: [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class lint-cleanup session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S238, whole-package lint pass -> standing lint-CI red cleared, PR #99 / merge 8deeecf)

Learning 225 – Removing development-process references (GitHub issue numbers, “Slice N” vertical-slice jargon) from documentation is gated by one distinction: PUBLISHED vs INTERNAL. A roxygen #' block renders to a man page (and the pkgdown reference / ?fn) ONLY when the function is exported or @-documented; a @noRd block is stripped at build – maintainer-only, the SAME category as a plain # source comment. So the in-scope set for “clean the user-facing docs” is non-@noRd roxygen + vignettes/articles + NEWS, NOT the @noRd docstrings, NOT the source comments (extends S238 gotcha #2 / Learning 224(3): exported-roxygen reflow regenerates man/*.Rd; @noRd does not). Owner observed user-facing docs leaked “issue #82, Slice 1”-style tags and asked “can you defend their inclusion?”; treated as a QUESTION ([[observation-vs-decision]], FM #23) – firsthand inventory + honest defend/concede, then AskUserQuestion ratified “Published surfaces only”. REFACTOR-class docs-only; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. (1) Classify @noRd vs rendered BEFORE editing. grep @noRd per file: of 23 files with tags, 7 were @noRd (left untouched) and 16 rendered (cleaned + devtools::document() regen, then man/ re-verified for zero issue/slice + zero drift). An independent audit confirmed zero over-reach (the 7 @noRd + 36 source comments + NEWS issue-numbers all unchanged) and zero leaks (man/, vignettes, README, _pkgdown.yml, DESCRIPTION, inst ui_guidance all clean). Over-reach is the failure mode – “finishing the job” on the @noRd files would have deleted maintainer breadcrumbs the owner chose to keep. (2) What is DEFENSIBLE vs not. DEFENSIBLE: issue NUMBERS in NEWS (standard R changelog convention – tidyverse/CRAN norm) and in source comments (rationale breadcrumbs). NOT DEFENSIBLE: any “Slice N” a user sees (internal tracer-bullet unit), bare “(issue #NN)” parentheticals in rendered help a reader cannot follow, and an article structured around a dev plan (“the issue #82 plan, Slice 2 of three”). (3) Reword for MEANING, not blind-delete. Keep the policy NAME, drop the ticket (“decline-to-credit policy, issue #76” -> “decline-to-credit policy”); when a deleted tag was an antecedent, reword it too (data.R “the remaining part of that issue” would dangle -> “a separate planned enhancement”); keep the methodology concept, drop the ticket framing (fg-se kept “compute -> validate -> surface” + “validate before expose”, dropped “plan for issue #82 (Slice 2 of three)”); name a mechanism instead of a ticket (gvaConvergence “issue #76 de-inflation” -> “gu = 0 de-inflation”). (4) A markdown footnote LABEL is spell-checked. spell_check_package tokenizes [^label]; a digit-bearing label (issue28) slips through but a pure-letter rename (transloc) gets flagged – use a real dictionary word (location). NEWS.md is generated from NEWS.Rmd via rmarkdown::render (github_document) – edit the .Rmd, render, inspect pandoc reflow (only the changed paragraphs re-wrap). (5) Verify a meaning-preserving reword ADVERSARIALLY, not just “are the tags gone.” A 4-agent audit (wf_b3fff352-1be: semantic-roxygen / semantic-articles / scope-completeness / adversarial-correctness, each schema’d) confirmed no leaks/over-reach/broken //footnotes + all 16 .Rd parse via tools::parse_Rd() with zero re-document drift, AND caught one self-grep-invisible miss: the fg-se “ratified in the plan” -> “set for this study” softened the pre-registration nuance (bands fixed in advance – a calibration-credibility claim); restored to “specified in advance for this validation study”. A grep proves the tags are gone; only a semantic read proves the meaning survived. (6) 1-and-done: deliverable verified on branch docs-strip-process-tags (5 commits); push/PR is owner-gated. Surfaced (not done): inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config:55 “(issue #73 Part 2)” in a user-copyable config template (source-comment KEEP pattern). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class docs-only session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S239, remove dev-process tags from published docs -> branch docs-strip-process-tags, owner-gated landing)

Learning 226 – A documented R CMD check --as-cran gate result certifies a SNAPSHOT of the tree, not the package in perpetuity; it goes stale as the tree evolves, and a plan that records “tree unchanged since the gate” must be RE-VERIFIED, not trusted. The CRAN submission plan (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md / phase5-runbook) recorded Phases 1-4 DONE with a local --as-cran = 0/0/2 gate (S134, 2026-06-18) and asserted “the code/data/metadata tree is unchanged since the S134 gate.” That was true when written but had since gone stale: git log --since="2026-06-18" --name-only -- 'R/*.R' 'man/*.Rd' NEWS* 'data/*' 'tests/**' 'vignettes/*' showed ~74 commits touching most of R/, all man/, NEWS, data/*.RData, ~50 test files, and the vignettes (the kinship-overrides feature #13/#95, the FG-SE validation work, the S238 lint pass, the S239 doc-tag strip). So the “just run Phase 5b” answer would have been wrong; the owner picked “run the local re-gate now” via AskUserQuestion. VERIFICATION session – TDD code-phases N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. (1) Re-gate BEFORE the owner runs expensive outward checks. win-builder x3 + R-hub are ~2 owner-hours of async round-trips; discovering a regression there (or after submission) is far costlier than a ~4-min local re-gate. Build + check the TARBALL (not the source dir) from the package root so renv activates; run a TRUE --as-cran (no _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_ override). Result held: Status: 2 NOTEs = 0/0/2, both the documented false-positives (CRAN incoming-feasibility archived/new-submission; local HTML-tidy+V8 manual note absent on CRAN) – read 00check.log to CONFIRM the 2 notes’ identity, do not trust the bare count. (2) Timing is THIS package’s archival cause and it grows with the suite. Compared to the S134 baseline: tests 43s->74s (suite 1853->3173 assertions), vignette rebuild 16s->24s, examples ~stable 19->22s. All still OK with no single example near 5s, but on ~2-3x-slower CRAN/win-builder hardware tests could be ~150-220s – so re-read the --timings table every re-gate, and Phase 5b on slow hardware is the only step that truly RETIRES the timing risk. Do not add slow un-skip_on_cran’d tests / heavy examples without watching that table. (3) --as-cran SKIPS the module/E2E tests via skip_on_cran – which is correct (CRAN won’t run them either); do NOT set NOT_CRAN=true for the gate (that is for local module/E2E runs only). The routine build-equivalent prior sessions cite (devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)=0/0/0) is FASTER but does NOT build vignettes or run --as-cran – for a CRAN claim use the full tarball --as-cran --timings. (4) README.md SHIPS in the tarball (.Rbuildignore ignores only README.Rmd + README.*.png), so its badges render on the CRAN landing page and are part of CRAN prep. (5) Badge-audit method (reusable): fetch each badge SVG and read its aria-label/<title> (cranlogs / r-pkg.org / GitHub embed the rendered value THERE, not always in <text>), and test the LINK target’s resolution separately – a HTTP 200 on the image does NOT mean the badge is correct. Found 3 real defects in README.Rmd:12-22: (a) :18 CRAN grand-total-downloads points to the wrong package (grand-total/kableExtra, renders “8.7M”) instead of grand-total/nprcgenekeepr (~“13K”) – copy-paste; (b) :19 malformed markdown, a stray leading [ ([[![R-CMD-check]) renders a literal [; (c) :20 bogus DOI – doi-10.32614 / doi.org/10.32614 is only the CRAN prefix and resolves to a generic “DOI Name Values” page, whereas the real 10.32614/CRAN.package.nprcgenekeepr resolves now (->CRAN page) and is the canonical fix. The defects were SURFACED (owner question, treated as a QUESTION per [[observation-vs-decision]]) and kept as a SEPARATE deliverable – not bundled into the re-gate (one-deliverable / SAFEGUARDS mode-switch discipline). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a VERIFICATION session – TDD code-phases N/A. (S240, re-establish the local CRAN --as-cran gate on current master -> GREEN 0/0/2)

Learning 227 – Executing an audit’s surfaced-but-not-fixed findings (S240’s 3 README badge defects) is a clean ~mechanical session, but two non-obvious mechanics gate it: a shields.io static-badge fix that puts a / in the MESSAGE field must URL-encode it as %2F (the LINK target uses the plain value), and because README.md SHIPS in the tarball (Learning 226(4)) a badge-URL change RE-STALES the S240 --as-cran gate and must re-clear it. Owner picked “fix the 3 README badge defects”; the DOI target was the one genuine fork, posed as an AskUserQuestion ([[observation-vs-decision]]) among CRAN-package-DOI / cited-paper-DOI / drop-the-badge – owner chose the canonical CRAN package DOI. Docs-only REFACTOR-class; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. (1) The fixes (source README.Rmd, NEVER the generated README.md): :18 grand-total/kableExtra -> grand-total/nprcgenekeepr; :19 drop the stray leading [; :20 doi-10.32614 / doi.org/10.32614 -> badge doi-10.32614%2FCRAN.package.nprcgenekeepr + link doi.org/10.32614/CRAN.package.nprcgenekeepr. (2) shields.io %2F is load-bearing. A raw / in https://img.shields.io/badge/<label>-<message>-<color> breaks the path; encode it %2F and the badge renders the decoded DOI – verified firsthand by fetching the SVG and reading its aria-label/<title> (“doi: 10.32614/CRAN.package.nprcgenekeepr”), the FIX-side of Learning 226(5)’s audit method. The LINK uses the plain DOI and resolved HTTP 200 -> the CRAN package page (curl -L follows doi.org’s redirect). The grand-total badge now reads “CRAN downloads 13K” (nprcgenekeepr), not kableExtra’s “8.7M”. (3) Re-clear the gate, because README.md ships. Per Learning 226(1) a documented gate certifies a SNAPSHOT; a README badge change IS a package change, so re-ran R CMD build . + R CMD check --as-cran --timings <tarball> from the package root -> Status: 2 NOTEs = 0/0/2 (the two documented false-positives), and grep -niE 'URL|doi|cranlogs|shields|kableExtra' 00check.log was EMPTY – the only check-relevant signal for a URL edit is the URL check, and it flagged nothing. (A doi.org DOI link is exempt from CRAN’s redirect-NOTE; the 3 new URLs each return 200, so no new NOTE was structurally possible – the full gate is the authoritative confirmation of that reasoning, and honors S240 gotcha #1 that any package change re-stales the gate.) (4) build_readme() re-stamps the version-date line. r nprcgenekeepr::getVersion() renders “Version 2.0.0 ()”, so regenerating today moved (2026-06-19) -> (2026-06-29) – an EXPECTED side-effect, not a defect; the YAML front-matter date: "2025-07-25" is the AUTHOR’s field and was left untouched (FM #22, do not edit unrequested). The README.md diff is exactly the 3 badges + that one date stamp – confirm the diff is ONLY the intended change. Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; executes the defects SURFACED by Learning 226(5). This was a docs-only REFACTOR-class session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S241, fix the 3 README badge defects -> README.Rmd/.md, gate re-confirmed GREEN 0/0/2)

Learning 228 – A curated owner-facing RUNBOOK certifies a SNAPSHOT of the environment + repo state the same way a --as-cran gate certifies a code snapshot (Learning 226), and it goes stale the same way – before an owner executes an outward, expensive, async runbook, RE-VERIFY its load-bearing premises firsthand rather than trusting its own “verified (Session NNN)” lines. The CRAN Phase 5b runbook (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md, S135/S136) rested on three premises that had ALL flipped by S242: (a) devtools/rhub/gitcreds “absent” -> now installed (requireNamespace all TRUE; the owner also installed command-line hunspell mid-session); (b) the 2.0.0 code lives on an add-methodology working branch -> that branch is merged + deleted and the code is on master; (c) origin/master “still at 1.1.0.9000, must open a new PR to master” -> master==origin/master@2.0.0, in sync (git rev-list --left-right --count origin/master...master = 0 0, empty git diff --name-only origin/master..master, origin/HEAD->master). Owner picked the docs-only refresh via AskUserQuestion after I SURFACED (not assumed) both the stale state AND that the core Phase 5b steps (win-builder x3, R-hub, submit_cran()) are owner-run/outward ([[observation-vs-decision]]). Docs-only REFACTOR-class; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. (1) Whether a doc edit re-stales the --as-cran gate is decided by .Rbuildignore. cran-comments.md (^cran-comments\.md$) and docs/ (^docs$) are build-ignored, so they do NOT ship in the tarball and editing them leaves the S241 gate (0/0/2) intact – NO re-gate needed (the cheap firsthand check is grep .Rbuildignore). Contrast Learning 227: README.md SHIPS, so a badge edit DID re-stale it. (2) CRAN’s “possibly misspelled words in DESCRIPTION” comes from GNU aspell on the Title+Description fields – NOT from inst/WORDLIST and NOT from spelling::spell_check_package (hunspell+WORDLIST). To reproduce locally you need a command-line speller for utils::aspell() to shell to; macOS Homebrew hunspell installs WITHOUT dictionaries (the en_US “Can’t open affix or dictionary files” error) – point it at the R hunspell package’s bundled dict: Sys.setenv(DICPATH=system.file("dict",package="hunspell")). The faithful incoming-feasibility call is utils::aspell(<DESCRIPTION>, filter="dcf", program="hunspell"); filter="dcf" scans only Title/Description and EXCLUDES the <...> reference URL, so URL fragments (pmc/ncbi/nih/nlm/https) are NOT flagged – matching CRAN. This is faster + more faithful than wrapping a full R CMD check (which would also need DICPATH exported and isn’t what CRAN runs anyway). (3) hunspell’s dictionary != GNU aspell’s, so the local list is a PROXY; the authoritative list is win-builder’s. The proxy flagged exactly EHR, kinships, LabKey, Macaca, mulatta, Raboin (all correctly-spelled names/acronyms/Latin-binomial/valid-plural; the owner confirmed Raboin + EHR are “always detected”). Reconciled cran-comments.md NOTE 1 to that set: dropped "studbooks" (in the Description but NOT flagged by the proxy -> asserting it was flagged is unverified) and added the prominent flagged species name "Macaca mulatta"; kept the “for example” hedge and added a §4.2 note telling the owner to re-confirm the final list against the win-builder log. (4) When the environment cannot run the literal task as phrased, SURFACE it and do the faithful reproduction – do not silently substitute or give up. “Reconcile against the local --as-cran log” was not literally executable (no command-line speller -> the local check emits no word list); I surfaced that, the owner installed hunspell, and the utils::aspell dcf call (the same machinery the check uses) produced the faithful list. (5) An independent adversarial audit earns its cost on owner-facing prep: a fresh agent (blind to my reasoning) re-reproduced the spell set, the branch sync, the tooling presence, and rhub.yaml existence against the live repo = PASS on all, no misdirecting defects; it caught 2 minor polish items (an imprecise “~74 commits” narrative figure -> “dozens”; a Quick-sequence “no push” vs §3 “push anyway” tension -> clarified §3’s push as optional housekeeping). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a docs-only REFACTOR-class session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S242, refresh the CRAN Phase 5b runbook + reconcile cran-comments NOTE 1 -> both .Rbuildignored, gate untouched)

Learning 229 – A copyright/license notice written as roxygen (#') instead of a plain comment (##) LEAKS into the rendered man page, and the leak is STRUCTURAL not cosmetic: at the FIRST #' line of an exported block it becomes the \title{}, and elsewhere the copyright + blank line hijack \description{} and demote the real first paragraph into a spurious \details{} block. (CRAN-facing – man pages render on the package page + PDF manual.) Oriented for CRAN Phase 5b; the owner observed “copyright notices have not been updated” – treated as a QUESTION ([[observation-vs-decision]], FM #23): firsthand audit, then AskUserQuestion ratified “Full normalization”. REFACTOR-class docs/metadata; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. (1) Two header patterns, one leaks. Pattern A (187 files, CORRECT): copyright/part-of are ## lines SANDWICHED inside the roxygen block (between #' lines) – roxygen2 silently ignores non-#' comment lines, so they never reach the .Rd (proof: no Pattern-A file appeared among the 22 leaking man/*.Rd). Pattern B (25 files, the LEAK): copyright/part-of are #' – mid-block they land in the body / spurious \details{}; at line 1 they become the \title{} (getSpeciesGestation, getSpeciesMinBreedingAge, both exported). (2) The fix is a uniform line-level transform, not a structural move. ^#' Copyright(c) -> ## Copyright(c) AND ^#' This file is part of nprcgenekeepr -> ## (convert BOTH – converting only the copyright line would promote “This file is part of” to the new title), plus Copyright(c) [0-9][0-9-]* R. Mark Sharp -> 2017-2026 (the one regex also fixes the malformed bare 2026). This turns Pattern B into Pattern A in place – no relocation needed. DRY-RUN the perl (no -i) on each representative shape FIRST: line-1-leak (title), mid-block-leak, Pattern-A-year-only, malformed-year. For the 16 header-less files, PREPEND ## Copyright / ## This file is part of / <blank> (all 16 opened with a #' title, so the ##+blank sits safely above the block). (3) Verify a 400+-file sweep by COUNTS + DIFF-CLEANLINESS, never eyeballing. After applying: git grep "^#' Copyright" and "^#' This file is part of" must be 0; the year distribution must collapse to a single 2017-2026 bucket; git diff | grep '^[+-]' | grep -vE 'Copyright|This file is part of|^[+-][[:space:]]*$' must be EMPTY (proves zero code lines changed). Then devtools::document() and confirm man/ changed ONLY ~22 files, git grep Copyright -- man/ is 0, the 2 titles are real, NAMESPACE is unchanged, and R CMD check says “code/documentation mismatches … OK”. The decisive sync proof: re-run document() a second time -> git status --short man/ is EMPTY (zero drift = every committed .Rd is a faithful regen, not a hand-edit) – this is exactly what an independent blind adversarial agent used to confirm PASS A-I. (4) R/+man/ ship, so re-gate even for comment-only. Both are in the tarball -> the --as-cran gate re-stales (Learning 226/227); re-ran build + --as-cran --timings = Status: 2 NOTEs = 0/0/2 (the two documented false-positives unchanged) – a comment/title fix introduces no new note, but the gate is the authoritative confirmation. (5) “Full normalization” = whole-package; commit the CRAN-relevant core separately. The ratified option counted R/ (197 stale years / 25 leaks / 16 bare) but full normalization also covers tests/ (195) + data-raw/ (3); committed R/+man/ (0c77f4d1) separately from tests/+data-raw/ (d7556d5e) so the CRAN-relevant core is cleanly revertable, and FLAG the full 413-source-file scope to the owner (the option text was R/-scoped – extending to tests/ is correct for “full” but should be surfaced). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class docs/metadata session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S243, normalize copyright headers + fix roxygen man-page leak -> branch normalize-copyright-headers, owner-gated landing)

Learning 230 – A doc-style AUDIT grounded in “authoritative” mechanical grep counts can ship a WRONG count into its own analysis – so a separate ADVERSARIAL verification stage that RE-DERIVES the load-bearing numbers firsthand is what keeps the bad ground truth out of the deliverable, not the grounding itself. Owner picked issue #102 (roxygen2 documentation harmonization – analysis + recommendation across all 226 R/ files); the issue scopes it as analysis ONLY (implementation a separate follow-on). AUDIT-class deliverable – TDD code-phases N/A (the output is an assessment doc, no production logic); 0 corrections / 0 overrides. Workstream AUDIT_WORKSTREAM.md; deliverable docs/audits/ROXYGEN_HARMONIZATION_AUDIT_2026-06-29.md (.Rbuildignored ^docs$ -> does NOT ship -> gate untouched, no re-gate – contrast Learning 229 where R/+man/ ship). (1) My OWN mechanical count was the defect, and the adversarial stage caught it. A file-level loop claimed “166/167 exported functions have @examples; only withinIntegerRange.R lacks one” and I fed that into the workflow’s ground-truth COUNTS; the independent verify agent re-swept firsthand and found withinIntegerRange.R HAS examples and 22 exported files lack them (true coverage 145/167). The tell was arithmetic the verifier flagged: “146 @examples files vs 167 @export cannot leave only 1 missing.” SANITY-CHECK the counts you pass as ground truth (cross-foot the totals) – a wrong input silently became a fabricated “strength” in synthesis until an independent re-derivation killed it. (2) Deep-read agents assert false PREMISES; pass authoritative ground truth in and re-confirm rendered defects against the actual .Rd. Several inventory agents claimed “roxygen markdown is not enabled package-wide” – FALSE (DESCRIPTION:81 Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)); because I’d passed the correct global into the synthesis prompt the recommendation stayed right, but the false claim leaked into raw highlights. For the headline rendered-doc defects I read the generated .Rd myself (man/calcGU.Rd shows ONLY \{Genome Uniqueness Functions\}\{\} because calcGU.R:42’s explicit @description overrides ~34 lines of implicit text; man/convertSexCodes.Rd:26-30 shows literal \item\{F\} \{...\} braces) – the agent’s word is a lead, the rendered output is the proof. (3) The substantive finding: roxygen consistency here is a two-era CONVERGENCE, not a green-field design. Legacy files embed the copyright between title and body and order @return before @param; the newest files (getSpeciesGestation.R, applyKinshipOverrides.R, hasInvalidIdChar.R) already follow the cleaner convention – so the recommendation is “standardize the legacy majority onto the emerging modern style” across 8 dimensions (markup direction = raw-Rd since it dominates 1270-to-48; @param-before-@return; copyright ABOVE the block; @noRd-only internals; @importFrom over @import – owner-confirmed CRAN preference). SEPARATE genuine rendered-doc DEFECTS (8 classes: D1 calcGU description override, D2 duplicate _PACKAGE, D3 escaped-brace lists, D4 @param for a nonexistent candidates arg, D5 wrong-function @return text, D6 title typos, D7 dead imports, D8 bare-blank-line block breaks) from style preferences and flag them “fix regardless of harmonization.” (4) Calibrate severity HONESTLY against rendered impact, not volume. The copyright-PLACEMENT inconsistency is the single highest-VOLUME issue but ## lines render fine, so it is moderate (“high-volume / low-impact”), NOT high – and the genuinely rendered-impacting bare-blank-line breaks were broken out as their own defect (the verifier’s calibration note). Avoid both severity inflation (volume != severity) and deflation (the buried 22-file examples gap). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was an AUDIT-class analysis session – TDD code-phases N/A. (S244, roxygen2 documentation harmonization analysis -> docs/audits/ROXYGEN_HARMONIZATION_AUDIT_2026-06-29.md, implementation deferred to a follow-on)

Learning 231 – When IMPLEMENTING an audit’s “fix all instances of defect class X”, the audit’s cited LOCATION list is a SAMPLE, not an inventory – grep the WHOLE tree for the defect PATTERN and fix every hit, or you ship a half-fix that leaves the same defect visible in the very pages you “fixed”. Owner picked issue #103 Stage 1 (fix rendered-doc defects D1-D8 from the S244 audit §4). REFACTOR-class doc-correctness – no behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (1 owner gate via AskUserQuestion: the D2 approach). Branch issue103-stage1-defects, landing owner-gated (S243/PR-101 precedent for shipping R/+man/ edits). (1) The D3 enumeration undercounted. The audit listed 6 files for the escaped-brace \item\{X\} \{Y\} pseudo-list defect; grep -rn '\\item *\\{\|\\item\\{' R/ (run AFTER fixing the cited ones, to find the remainder) surfaced 3 more sites the audit never named – calcGU.R’s AlleleTable list (the file was cited for D1 only), the wholly-uncited getRequiredCols.R, and four MORE \itemize lists inside qcStudbook.R beyond the single column-list the audit pointed at (sex/status/ancestry codes + a column-rename list). 8 files carried D3, not 6. The completion proof is a full-tree rescan returning ZERO \item\{/\{-- in BOTH R/ and man/ – do that rescan, do not trust the citation count. (The audit even hedged “…also …” and “re-confirm line numbers” – read enumerations as illustrative.) (2) A duplicate _PACKAGE resolves cleanly by DELETING the legacy doc file, and roxygen auto-heals the alias. roxygen MERGES two _PACKAGE blocks into one <pkg>-package.Rd (its header said Please edit ... R/nprcgenekeeper.R, R/nprcgenekeepr-package.R), so the legacy R/nprcgenekeeper.R was injecting the WRONG \title{} (“Genetic Management Functions”), a “Primary Data Structure” preamble, and escaped-brace \seealso garbage, plus spawning a thin man/nprcgenekeepr.Rd alias page. Deleting it (the modern usethis nprcgenekeepr-package.R is the keeper) regenerated the package page with the CRAN-standard auto title (nprcgenekeepr: <DESCRIPTION Title>), the clean DESCRIPTION-derived description, clean “Useful links” \seealso, AND both \alias{nprcgenekeepr} + \alias{nprcgenekeepr-package} – the \alias{nprcgenekeepr} migrated to the package page automatically once the legacy @name nprcgenekeepr block was gone, so ?nprcgenekeepr still resolves. VERIFY by reading the regenerated .Rd aliases + the gate (deleting a doc file is a judgment call with content-loss -> gate it: I posed delete-vs-preserve-overview via AskUserQuestion). (3) A title-typo fix is a TWO-WAY WORDLIST edit. inst/WORDLIST held the OLD typos (obfucateDate, obfucateId, portential) – a prior session had added them so the typo’d \title{} lines passed spell_check_package. Fixing the typo ORPHANS those entries AND newly-flags the corrected compound token: obfuscateDate/obfuscateId (camelCase function names) are not dictionary words, so the corrected titles now trip the speller. Hand-edit WORDLIST in BOTH directions – replace the two function-name typos with their corrected spelling (they sorted right before the already-present obfuscatePed), DELETE portential (its fix “potential” is a real word needing no entry) – never update_wordlist ([[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]]). Re-run spell_check_package to confirm CLEAN. (4) D7 “dead import” removal is provably zero-impact when the host function is commented out. makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R’s @importFrom gplots/@import RColorBrewer/@importFrom grDevices produced NOTHING in NAMESPACE today – the block is @noRd and the function below it is fully ##-commented, so there is NO code object for roxygen to attach the import directives to, and roxygen silently drops the orphan block. Confirmed BEFORE removing: grep gplots\|RColorBrewer in DESCRIPTION = absent, in NAMESPACE = absent. So removal cannot create a “Namespace in Imports field not imported from” NOTE nor drop a live import. (The general rule: an @import* tag only reaches NAMESPACE if its block attaches to a real exported/@noRd OBJECT.) (5) For doc-only REFACTOR, the regenerated .Rd is the proof, the gate is the certifier. I read each fixed man/*.Rd firsthand (calcGU description restored; convertSexCodes/qcStudbook/getSiteInfo/getRequiredCols lists real; makeCEPH’s D8 truncation healed; checkParentAge’s previously-MISSING \item for damAge now present) – the rendered output, not the source diff, is what confirms the fix. Then the --as-cran gate (R/+man/ ship -> re-gate, Learning 226/227) = Status: 2 NOTEs 0/0/2 with Rd files OK / Rd cross-references OK / code-documentation mismatches OK / Rd \usage OK (the doc-check sub-lines are the relevant signal for doc edits; the 2 NOTEs are the documented false-positives). Also caught + fixed the ONE new lint I introduced (a D6 title fix pushed obfuscateDate.R:1 to 81 chars -> reworded “plus and minus” to “plus/minus”; the 2 remaining >80 lines are pre-existing # nolint-suppressed) ([[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class doc-correctness session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S245, fix roxygen doc defects D1-D8 -> branch issue103-stage1-defects, gate GREEN 0/0/2, spell CLEAN, owner-gated landing)

Learning 232 – “--as-cran gate GREEN” is NOT “CI green”: the local gate does NOT run lintr::lint_package(), and a line-number-keyed .lintr exclusion silently DESYNCS when an edit above it shifts lines – so opening a PR for CI is what catches the regression a local gate structurally cannot. Owner picked option 1 (“open a PR for CI validation”) to land the S245 Stage-1 doc work (issue #103, D1-D8). Landing/process action + a 1-line .lintr fix – no R-logic change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (1 owner gate via AskUserQuestion: the lint-fix approach). PR #104, base master, keyword-safe body (“Part of #103” – does not auto-close the multi-stage tracker). (1) The gate and the lint workflow are DIFFERENT checks. S245 verified its work with R CMD check --as-cran (GREEN 0/0/2) and called the branch “DONE + VERIFIED”, but --as-cran does NOT run lintr; the project’s lint is a SEPARATE CI workflow (.github/workflows/lint.yaml, lintr::lint_package() with LINTR_ERROR_ON_LINT: true). So a Stage-1-introduced lint regression rode silently into the branch and only surfaced on the PR’s first CI run – exactly what the owner’s “open a PR for CI validation” choice was designed to catch. After ANY R/ edit, either run lint_package() locally too or rely on the PR’s CI; do not equate gate-GREEN with CI-green. (2) Root cause: a hardcoded .lintr line-range exclusion desynced. .lintr had exclusions: list(..., "R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R" = 12L:55L, ...) – a line range calibrated to that file’s dead, fully-##-commented function body (the file has ZERO live code; the body started at line 12 on master). S245’s D7 deleted 3 dead @importFrom/@import lines ABOVE the body, shifting it up 3 lines, so the flagged my_palette <- ... line moved from 14 to 11 – OUTSIDE the 12L:55L window -> commented_code_linter fired. No # nolint was removed; pure line-number drift. General rule: any config that pins behavior to absolute line numbers (.lintr ranges, coverage excludes, # nolint at a remembered line) is brittle to edits above it – when you add/remove lines, re-align or de-couple the pinned ranges in the SAME commit. (3) Diagnose a CI lint regression by proving NEW-vs-PRE-EXISTING first, then the exact diff. gh run list --workflow lint.yaml showed every recent master lint run = success and the prior normalize-copyright-headers PR = success, so THIS PR was the first failure => the regression is the branch’s, not pre-existing (this is the decisive cheap check – do it before theorizing). Then git diff master..branch -- <file> + grep nolint <file> pinned the mechanism (3 lines removed above a line-keyed exclusion; no nolint involved). (4) Prefer WHOLE-FILE exclusion over a brittle line range for 100%-dead-code files. Owner chose (via AskUserQuestion, [[observation-vs-decision]]) to change "R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R" = 12L:55L -> "R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R" (unnamed whole-file entry, like inst/application/tests/vignettes) – removes the line-number coupling so a future shift cannot break it again; justified because grep -nvE '^\s*#' proved the file has zero live code and it has zero references in R//man//NAMESPACE/live tests. Verified firsthand BEFORE pushing: lintr::lint_package(".") = 0 lints. (5) When the owner asks to “open a PR for CI validation,” own the RESULT, not just the act. Opening the PR is one gh pr create; the deliverable’s VALUE is the validation outcome. Monitored CI to completion (background gh pr checks poll), and when it went red, diagnosed + fixed + re-ran to GREEN rather than reporting “PR opened” and stopping (FM #13 literal-minimum). Final: 10/10 checks PASS, gh pr view --json = state OPEN / mergeable MERGEABLE / mergeStateStatus CLEAN – the full 5-platform R-CMD-check matrix + lint + pkgdown + coverage is a stronger certification than the local gate alone; the merge/landing stays the owner’s decision (do NOT merge unilaterally). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a landing/process + lint-config-fix session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S246, open PR #104 for CI validation + fix the CI-surfaced lint regression -> branch issue103-stage1-defects, CI GREEN 10/10, PR mergeable, owner-gated merge)

Learning 233 – Merging the feature branch you are CURRENTLY ON: stash the Phase-1B stub first, because the mandatory SESSION_NOTES.md stub is an uncommitted edit that BLOCKS the post-merge checkout to master. Owner picked “merge PR #104” to land Stage 1 of issue #103 (D1-D8 + the .lintr fix) onto master. Landing/process action on already-CI-green doc work – no R-logic change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. (1) The 1B-stub + merge-the-current-branch collision. Phase 1B mandates writing a “Session N claimed” stub to SESSION_NOTES.md BEFORE technical work; when the session’s deliverable IS merging the branch you are on, that stub becomes an uncommitted edit to a file that DIFFERS between the branch (it carries the S245/S246 sections) and master (it does not), so gh pr merge --delete-branch’s automatic git checkout master would refuse with “Your local changes to SESSION_NOTES.md would be overwritten by checkout.” Fix: git stash push SESSION_NOTES.md before the merge, then git stash pop after – the post-merge master SESSION_NOTES.md == the branch’s S246 version (the merge brought it across), so the stash (recorded against that same S246 base) re-applies with ZERO conflict. (General rule: before merging the branch you’re standing on, the working tree must be clean w.r.t. any file that differs between branch and target – stash it.) (2) A --merge PR merge of an already-CI-green branch needs NO local re-gate. The merge commit 9b512b1e has two parents (170324d5 master + 3beaf2bc branch tip) but git diff 9b512b1e 3beaf2bc is EMPTY – master never moved after the branch forked, so the merged tree is byte-identical to the tip CI validated 10/10 (full 5-platform R-CMD-check matrix + lint + pkgdown + coverage). The standing “R/+man/ ship -> re-gate” rule (Learning 226/227) is SATISFIED by that CI run; re-running the local --as-cran gate would only re-certify an identical tree. Re-gate is for NEW edits, not for landing already-validated commits – prove identity with git diff <merge> <tip> = empty. (3) --delete-branch leaves a stale remote-tracking ref; confirm + prune, don’t trust git branch -r. gh pr merge --merge --delete-branch deleted the remote branch (verified authoritatively: gh api repos/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/branches/issue103-stage1-defects = 404 “Branch not found”) AND the local branch, but git branch -r still listed origin/issue103-stage1-defects until git fetch --prune removed the stale ref (it also pruned the long-dead normalize-copyright-headers from PR #101). The API 404 is the proof the remote branch is gone; the lingering local ref is just unpruned. (4) Match the repo’s merge-strategy precedent unless told otherwise. The owner said only “merge PR #104”; the established way to land R/+man/ work here is a merge commit (a4de6a84 Merge pull request #101), so gh pr merge --merge (not --squash) preserved the well-formed S245/S246 commits and their messages – offered squash as an alternative, owner did not object. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a landing/process session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S247, merge PR #104 -> Stage 1 of #103 landed on master 9b512b1e, branch deleted local+remote, local==origin)

Learning 234 – A “relocate comments” REFACTOR sweep across hundreds of files is proven safe by two DETERMINISTIC whole-corpus checks, never by eyeballing the diff. Stage 2 of issue #103 (copyright-placement, audit Finding 8): an R-scripted transform moved the ## Copyright(c) ... / ## This file is part of nprcgenekeepr pair from inside each roxygen block to file-top, above the first #' line with one blank separator (the getSpeciesGestation.R exemplar) – 203 of 225 files moved, 22 already-correct skipped. REFACTOR-class; no behavior change, no rendered-doc change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (1 owner gate: full-sweep vs sample-first). (1) The two checks that make per-file review unnecessary. (a) ZERO man/ drift: after devtools::document(), git status --porcelain man/ is empty – decisive because roxygen2 IGNORES every ## (and plain #) comment, so relocating/removing them CANNOT reach any .Rd; the only edit roxygen could notice is the blank-#'-seam collapse, and merging two blank doc lines is a no-op for paragraph breaks. (b) ZERO code-line change: a code-only projection diff per changed file (diff <(git show HEAD:f | grep -vE '^[[:space:]]*#|^[[:space:]]*$') <(grep -vE ... f); empty for all 204) proves no R logic moved. Together (a)+(b) cover the ENTIRE change surface, so a 200+-file sweep needs no manual diff review. (2) The diff LOOKS bigger than the change. Title #' lines appear as paired -/+ with IDENTICAL text because git reframes the relocated-block hunk as delete-old + add-new; that is cosmetic reordering, not change – do not mistake it for risk, and do not write a naive “unexpected line” grep (it fires on every moved-but-identical title); the right code-safety check is the comment-stripped projection in (1b). (3) Classify-then-characterize BEFORE writing the transform. Two read-only R passes (classify each file ABOVE/INTERLEAVED by comparing the copyright-line index to the first-#' index; then characterize edge cases) surfaced every special case up front – 2 files carrying the pair TWICE (two single-function blocks each -> de-duplicate to ONE pair at top), 9 mod*.R with a # section banner at line 1 (prepend-at-very-top puts the pair above the banner uniformly), and 1 already-above-but-separator-missing file – so the transform became a SINGLE deterministic function (remove all pairs, collapse the resulting double-#' seam, prepend pair+blank) instead of a pile of ad-hoc fixes. (4) Blast-radius gate. A 203-file mechanical sweep is high blast radius -> gated the start via AskUserQuestion (full-sweep vs sample-first), committed clean first, branch-based with landing owner-gated (Stage 1 precedent). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class doc-structure session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S248, copyright-placement sweep -> branch issue103-stage2-copyright-placement, code commit af08b5bf, UNPUSHED, landing owner-gated)

Learning 235 – A line-shifting REFACTOR sweep clears CI lint on the FIRST PR run when (and only when) the predecessor ran lint_package() AFTER the sweep – closing the Learning-232 gap that bit Stage 1. Owner picked “1” (Land Stage 2) and, via AskUserQuestion, the landing METHOD “open a PR for CI” (option (a) of the S248 landing gate) to land Stage 2 of issue #103 (the copyright-placement sweep). Landing/process action on already-locally-verified doc-structure work – no R-logic change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (1 owner gate via AskUserQuestion: the landing method – PR vs direct-merge vs review-first). PR #105, base master, keyword-safe body (“Part of #103. Stage 2 of 8” – does not auto-close the multi-stage tracker). (1) First-pass green BECAUSE the line-shift condition was already lint-checked. The sweep moved the ## copyright pair to file-top, shifting line numbers in all 204 files – EXACTLY the condition that desynced Stage 1’s line-keyed .lintr exclusion (Learning 232: the 12L:55L range vs a 3-line shift). But S248 ran lintr::lint_package() = 0 AFTER the sweep and recorded it, so any desync was already surfaced/excluded; CI lint came back PASS (3m44s) on the FIRST run – no diagnose-and-fix cycle, in contrast to S245->S246’s red-then-green. The local lint_package() is a FAITHFUL proxy for CI lint (both run lintr::lint_package()), so once it reads 0 the PR’s lint check is predictably green; the PR round-trip’s residual value is the 5-platform / R-devel matrix coverage the local gate cannot give, NOT lint disagreement. Rule: after ANY line-shifting R/ edit, run lint_package() BEFORE opening the PR – that is what makes the first CI run green and keeps the Learning-232 regression from riding in silently. (2) The close-out-docs commit re-triggers the FULL CI matrix. The lint/pkgdown/R-CMD-check/test-coverage workflows trigger on pull_request: with NO paths-ignore, so committing the markdown close-out docs (CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES) to the PR branch re-runs the entire ~18-min matrix even though zero R//man//NAMESPACE changed. It stays green (markdown cannot break R CMD check or lint – lintr lints only R/, and those top-level files already exist in the green tree), and the payoff is that the merge-tip the owner lands is itself CI-validated (the S246->S247 precedent: S246’s close-out commit WAS the validated tip S247 merged). EXPECT the second run and a transient mergeStateStatus UNSTABLE before it resolves – do not mistake it for a problem. (3) Own the RESULT (FM #13), gate the METHOD (not just the act). The owner’s “Land Stage 2” left the landing METHOD open, so I posed it via AskUserQuestion (PR / direct-merge / review-first) rather than assuming the Stage-1 precedent, then monitored CI to completion via a background gh pr checks poll and confirmed 10/10 + mergeStateStatus CLEAN before declaring done – did NOT merge unilaterally (the merge stays the owner’s call, matching the S246->S247 split). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a landing/process session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S249, open PR #105 for CI validation -> Stage 2 of #103, CI GREEN 10/10, PR mergeable/CLEAN, owner-gated merge)

Learning 236 – A roxygen TAG-REORDER sweep is proven safe by ZERO man/ drift – with ONE exception roxygen does NOT canonicalize: @author/@references keep their SOURCE order, so they must never be swapped. Owner: “merge PR #105; work on #103 this session” -> (1) merged PR #105 (Stage 2 landed on master, merge commit 12c7c353), (2) issue #103 Stage 3 – block-order normalization (audit Finding 2): a deterministic R transform reordered roxygen tags to canonical order across 194 files / 206 blocks. REFACTOR-class doc-structure – no behavior/rendered-doc change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (1 owner gate via AskUserQuestion: the @examples/@export order). (1) roxygen2 canonicalizes rendered .Rd section order REGARDLESS of source tag order – EXCEPT @author and @references, whose source order IS preserved into \author{}/\references{}. So reordering @return/@param/@examples/@importFrom/@export produces byte-identical .Rd (the man/-zero-drift proof), but swapping @author vs @references (or interleaving their content) DOES change .Rd. First pass swapped them per a naive canonical key (references<author) and man/kinship.Rd was the lone drifter – content moved between \references{} and \author{}. Fix: give @author and @references the SAME sort key so a stable sort PRESERVES source order (only kinship.R has both). General rule for any tag-reorder: tags that map to free-text .Rd sections roxygen does not reorder (author/references) must keep source order; tags roxygen canonicalizes (param/return/value/arguments/seealso/examples/usage/details) can be freely reordered and proven safe by zero drift. (2) Plain-# comments INSIDE a roxygen block split a naive #'-contiguous parser, but roxygen2 SPANS them. 8 files carry interruptions: # nolint start/end wrapping a long line (kinship.R) and ## ## rmsutilityr <fn> source-attribution notes (checkRequiredCols/colChange/qcStudbook/…). roxygen treats the doc block as continuing across these (kinship’s .Rd had all the post-nolint content), so it merges tags across the gap. Safe handling: reorder each #'-fragment INDEPENDENTLY and leave the plain-# line fixed in place – this is correct when the interruption sits at a clean tag boundary (every fragment’s max canonical key <= the next fragment’s min key, so no tag needs to cross it) AND when no .Rd-bearing tag’s CONTINUATION spans the gap. kinship.R violated the latter (a # nolint wrapped a @references continuation line), which is why only the author/references-same-key fix (1) – not the fragment-independence – made it drift-free. (3) The deterministic safety suite for a tag-reorder sweep (no eyeballing 194 diffs): (a) zero man/ drift after devtools::document() – the rendered-surface proof; (b) code+plain-comment projection diff = 0 (strip #' lines, compare HEAD vs working) – proves no code/comment line moved; (c) per-block assertion sort(region_before) == sort(region_after) IN the transform – proves only a PERMUTATION, no line content altered; (d) lint_package() = 0 (line shifts must not desync a .lintr line-keyed exclusion – Learning 232/235); (e) re-characterize -> 0 residual deviations (goal achieved); (f) --as-cran 0/0/2 with Rd files/cross-refs/usage OK. man/-drift CANNOT cover @noRd files (they generate no .Rd) – that surface is covered by (b)+(c) [provably a content-preserving permutation into canonical order, and @noRd renders nothing so there is no output to break] and, here, an independent 9-agent adversarial review of all 45 changed @noRd diffs (0 defects). (4) A self-CONTRADICTORY spec is an owner gate, not a guess. The audit’s Finding 2 template said @examples LAST (after @export) in two places, but its “secondary disorder” bullet AND its named reference-exemplar files (getSpeciesGestation.R) put @examples BEFORE @export – a genuine 3-vs-3 contradiction over a corpus-wide convention. Both choices are purely source-cosmetic (zero .Rd/NAMESPACE impact). Posed it via AskUserQuestion ([[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] when the source of truth is contradictory) -> owner chose @examples LAST. (5) GitHub network-outage workaround: REST survives when GraphQL + git-over-HTTPS do not. During a TLS-handshake-timeout outage, gh pr merge/gh pr view --json (GraphQL) and git fetch/git push (HTTPS) timed out, but gh api (REST) worked with retries – merged via gh api -X PUT repos/.../pulls/105/merge -f merge_method=merge, confirmed merged:true, then DEFERRED the local master FF-sync + remote-branch delete + Stage 3 push to close-out and completed them when the network recovered (a merged PR on the remote is authoritative; the local sync is just bookkeeping). Stage 3 work itself is 100% local (edit R, document(), gate, lint), so the outage did not block the deliverable. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a landing/process + REFACTOR-class doc-structure session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S250, merge PR #105 + issue #103 Stage 3 block-order normalization -> branch issue103-stage3-block-order, commit fb9a92ab, gate 0/0/2, man/ zero-drift, owner-gated landing)

Learning 237 – A backtick->\code{} markup sweep is rendered-NEUTRAL when markdown = TRUE (roxygen already expands `x` to \code{x} in the .Rd), so the success test is ZERO man/ drift – EXCEPT where the +5-char expansion pushes a source line past 80 chars, forcing a reflow whose .Rd drift is whitespace-only. Owner pick = “stage 4” -> issue #103 Stage 4 (audit Finding 4): 45 backtick spans -> \code{} across 30 R/ files. REFACTOR-class doc-markup – no R logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change; rendered docs unchanged; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (2 owner gates via AskUserQuestion). (1) Rendered-neutral, NOT a rendered change. DESCRIPTION:81 sets Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) with NO @noMd overrides, so roxygen already converts source `x` -> \code{x} in the generated .Rd. Changing the SOURCE backtick to \code{} therefore yields a byte-identical .Rd (26 of 30 files) – the same zero-man/-drift proof as a tag-reorder. (My opening phase declaration wrongly said it would “change rendered output”; reading DESCRIPTION:81 corrected it – read the markdown setting BEFORE declaring the phase.) (2) The audit’s count (“48 backticks / 37 files, small/safe”) undercounts ~2x and omits the EXCLUSION traps. Reality: 105 backticks on #' lines, of which only 90 (= 45 spans) are genuine \code{} targets; the other 15 must NOT be converted – backtick-quoted NON-SYNTACTIC R names INSIDE @examples (`si re`, relClasses$`Relationship Class`, `n` in ## example comments), the markdown INLINE-R `r lifecycle::badge('experimental')` (converting breaks the badge), and broken `word' typographic quotes (`and'/`or' -> a separate rendered defect). Classify each backtick by its line-number-vs-its-block’s-@examples boundary, and beware MULTI-BLOCK files (set_seed.R has 3 blocks -> the “first @examples line” heuristic misleads; cross-check export status + the actual owning block). (3) Conversion adds 5 chars/token -> some lines cross the 80-col lint limit -> reflow, but reflow moves .Rd line breaks. The tension: NOT reflowing => lint_package() fails ([[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]); reflowing => man/ drift. Resolution: reflow with MINIMAL non-cascading word-moves – often move the FIRST word of the overflowing line UP to the prior line (which has slack), avoiding the down-cascade through a packed paragraph – then PROVE the .Rd drift is whitespace-only via a whitespace-normalized HEAD-vs-working comparison (Rd fills paragraphs, so intra-paragraph line breaks render identically). 4 of 30 files needed this. NB: do not break a line right after a / joining two \code{} (e.g. \code{"M"}/\code{"F"}) – the newline renders as an inserted space; break at a real word boundary instead. (4) The deterministic safety suite for a markup-token sweep: (a) backtick count drops by exactly 2x the target-span count and the RESIDUAL count == the exclusions (byte-verified list); (b) word-sequence projection – canonicalize BOTH `x` and \code{x} to a bare token + collapse whitespace, then HEAD == working for every changed file (proves only backtick->\code + whitespace moved; no word dropped/added/reordered; covers @noRd files too, which generate no .Rd); (c) man/ drift = byte-identical OR whitespace-normalized-identical; (d) lint_package() = 0; (e) spell_check_package CLEAN; (f) --as-cran 0/0/2 with Rd line widths / code-documentation mismatches / Rd OK. (5) Run the --as-cran gate from the PROJECT ROOT (renv active), not a scratchpad cwd. R CMD build from outside the project makes the copied package bootstrap renv against an empty cache and fail with “dependencies … are not available”; from the root the project .Rprofile activates the renv library (deps present), and .Rbuildignore excludes renv/renv.lock/.Rprofile from the tarball so the vignette-install step does not re-bootstrap.
