Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class doc-markup session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S251, issue #103 Stage 4 markup unification -> branch issue103-stage4-markup, code commit 002b77c9, gate 0/0/2, 45 conversions / 30 files / 6 exclusions, get_and_or_list defect deferred)

Learning 238 – A DIRECT-MERGE landing of an already---as-cran-certified branch needs NO local re-gate and gets NO CI, so the local cert of the EXACT merged commits is the SOLE certification – confirm the merge brought the right content with a CHEAP deterministic sanity check on the landed master tree, not a gate re-run. Owner: “land stage 4” -> via AskUserQuestion the landing METHOD “direct-merge” (the Stage-3 precedent 72f5391e) to land issue #103 Stage 4 (markup unification) onto master. Landing/process – no R-logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (1 owner gate: the landing method). (1) Direct-merge has NO CI safety net (unlike the PR path of Learning 235), so there is no 5-platform re-validation – the only certification is S251’s local --as-cran 0/0/2 (+ lint_package() = 0 + zero man/ drift) on the EXACT commits being merged. Prove identity instead of re-running the ~15-min gate: master never moved since the branch forked, so git diff 87abc0fc..ff0ca8fd is exactly S251’s two commits (002b77c9 + 3f8c1865) – Learning 233 (2)’s “a merge of an already-validated branch needs no re-gate” applies to a direct-merge too (the identity argument is the same; only the certifier differs – local gate here vs CI there). (2) BUT direct-merge loses CI’s independent re-check, so substitute a CHEAP deterministic sanity check on the LANDED tree matched to the change – for this markup sweep: backtick chars on #' lines == 15 (the 6 intended exclusions, == S251’s 105->15), \code{Pedigree} == 25, \code{kValue} == 10, and the deferred get_and_or_list.R:10 defect still PRESENT (correctly NOT folded in). Confirms the merge brought the CONVERTED content (not a stale/pre-conversion tree) in seconds. This is the direct-merge analogue of CI’s role: not a re-gate, a content-identity spot-check. (3) The stash-stub -> checkout -> merge --no-ff -> push -> pop -> branch -d sequence (Learning 233 (1)) reapplies the 1B stub cleanly because the post-merge master SESSION_NOTES base == the branch base the stash recorded against. Stash ONLY the tracked-modified files (SESSION_NOTES.md + .DS_Store) so the untracked PED_GV_AUDIT*.html standing keep is undisturbed; --no-ff matches the Stage-3 merge-commit precedent. (4) A LOCAL-ONLY branch (never pushed) needs only git branch -d after the merge – no remote ref, so no gh api 404 check / git fetch --prune (that cleanup in Learning 233 (3) was specific to a PR-landed branch deleted via --delete-branch). And the predecessor’s close-out docs ride the merge onto master, so this session’s close-out simply appends to them on master and pushes – no branch round-trip ([[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a landing/process session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S252, direct-merge Stage 4 of #103 -> landed on master merge commit ff0ca8fd, branch deleted local-only, local==origin)

Learning 239 – Converting a whole-package @import pkg to explicit @importFrom is provably safe when the proof is a NAMESPACE diff of EXACTLY the import(pkg) line removed – and you get there by a BARE-name census BEFORE editing, not by trusting the audit’s count. Owner pick = “stage 5”; via AskUserQuestion scope = futile.logger only (defer shiny/Matrix), landing METHOD = PR-for-CI. REFACTOR-class import-declaration refactoring – no new behavior; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (2 owner gates: scope + landing). (1) An @import/@importFrom change is NAMESPACE-only -> the proof surface is the NAMESPACE diff + the check/test runtime, NOT man/ drift. Import tags never appear in .Rd, so man/ drift is ZERO and irrelevant (unlike the Stage 1-4 rendered-doc proofs); the success test is that document() produces EXACTLY the predicted NAMESPACE delta. PREDICT the delta from a usage census first, then confirm – here “one line removed: import(futile.logger)” held byte-for-byte. (2) A whole-pkg import(pkg) survives in NAMESPACE as long as ANY file keeps @import pkg; to drop it, ALL @import pkg tags must go. Convert each to @importFrom pkg <fns-it-uses>; a file that uses NO function of pkg (a DEAD @import – here getPedDirectRelatives.R) is REMOVED, not converted (nothing to import). Do not leave one “for safety” – it defeats the stage. (3) The census: find every BARE (unqualified) call to pkg’s functions; pkg::fn-qualified calls need no import and are unaffected. Grep code lines (exclude #') for the package’s function patterns. Every bare futile.logger call was flog.debug (6 files), already imported via other files’ @importFrom (appServer/modInput); all other refs were futile.logger::-qualified – so removing import(futile.logger) could only orphan a bare non-flog.debug call, of which there were NONE. That census IS the proof the delta is exactly the import() line. (4) Runtime-verify a NAMESPACE stage with the --as-cran import checks + an EXPLICIT smoke against the INSTALLED namespace, NOT load_all(). checking dependencies in R code ... OK + checking R code for possible problems ... OK (catches “no visible global function for flog.debug”) + examples/--run-donttest/tests OK run against the freshly-installed pkg with imports ENFORCED – the authoritative runtime check. pkgload::load_all() makes ALL functions visible regardless of imports -> it would HIDE a missing-import bug; for the explicit smoke, prepend the .Rcheck dir to .libPaths() and library() the installed build (confirmed whole-pkg futile.logger gone; bare flog.debug fired; getPedigree() -> 280 rows). The handoff’s “verify method dispatch” warning targets S4 pkgs (Matrix, deferred) – flog.debug is a plain function, no dispatch surface. (5) The audit’s enumeration was STALE – re-grep the live tree. The S244 audit’s “8 easy = 7 futile.logger + 1 RColorBrewer” was wrong: RColorBrewer’s dead @import was already removed by an earlier D7 cleanup (no @import, not in DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE; only grDevices::colorRampPalette survives in a commented-out body), so the real scope was 7 futile.logger, one dead. grep -rn "@import " R/*.R for the ACTUAL whole-pkg tags before scoping (Learning 231: cited locations are a sample, not an inventory). (6) Op gotcha: a shell var set as VAR=... ; Rscript -e '...Sys.getenv("VAR")...' is EMPTY in the child unless exported – the first runtime smoke silently loaded the OLD renv namespace (tell: “loaded from: …/renv/library”), proving nothing about the new tree; hardcode the path or export, and ASSERT the load path (grepl("s253gate", system.file(...))) before trusting a smoke. (7) Landing = PR-for-CI (owner gate): CI GREEN 10/10 first run (lint 3m54s + 5-platform R-CMD-check incl. devel 18m28s + pkgdown + coverage + codecov), PR #106 MERGEABLE/CLEAN – multi-platform confirmation that the import removal resolves identically everywhere (R namespace mechanics are platform-independent, so CI’s value is breadth-of-confidence, not a different failure mode). Did NOT merge unilaterally (owner’s call, S246->S247 / S249 split). The close-out-docs commit re-triggers the full matrix (Learning 235 (2), no paths-ignore) – accepted as the cost of an ACCURATE handoff (the CI result must be known before the close-out commit); the real fix (a paths-ignore workflow config) is a separate gated decision, not folded in. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class import-declaration session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S253, issue #103 Stage 5 import conversion -> branch issue103-stage5-imports, code commit 9811a945, NAMESPACE delta = import(futile.logger) removed, gate 0/0/2, full NOT_CRAN 0/0, runtime smoke OK, PR #106 CI 10/10 green)

Learning 240 – Dropping a redundant @keywords internal from an @noRd block is the CLEANEST possible REFACTOR: the tag is INERT on an @noRd block (roxygen generates no .Rd for it, and @keywords never touches NAMESPACE), so the proof is zero man/ AND zero NAMESPACE drift – and NO runtime smoke is needed (unlike a true @import NAMESPACE stage, Learning 239). Owner-directed two-item session: (1) merge PR #106 (Stage 5 landing), (2) issue #103 Stage 6 – internal-marker cleanup (audit Finding 5). (1) landing/process; (2) REFACTOR-class doc-metadata; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (1 owner gate via AskUserQuestion: the Stage-6 landing method -> direct-merge). (1) @keywords on an @noRd block reaches NO output. @noRd suppresses .Rd generation, so @keywords (which only ever populates a \keyword{} field in a generated .Rd) is dead metadata there, and @keywords never affects NAMESPACE – therefore removing it from @noRd helpers is provably zero-man/-drift AND zero-NAMESPACE-change. Predicted it from that semantics, then PROVED it: devtools::document() left man/ and NAMESPACE untouched and git diff <pre-stage6>..<merge> -- NAMESPACE man/ is empty. Even cleaner than the Stage-5 import change (which DID move NAMESPACE) – no runtime smoke is warranted because nothing rendered or dispatched changed. (2) Policy: @noRd for unexported helpers; reserve @keywords internal for the package-level doc only. Removed 5 redundant tags – 4 in modPotentialParents.R (flattenPotentialParents/firstPedigreeSpecies/pedigreeGestationDefault/prefillGuardAllows) + 1 in readFocalAnimalIds.R, each paired with @noRd – and KEPT nprcgenekeepr-package.R:4 (the package doc, which IS documented, so its @keywords internal is meaningful). (3) The audit’s Finding-5 enumeration was STALE (Learning 231 again). It listed 4 files incl. nprcgenekeeper.R (the duplicate legacy _PACKAGE doc, Finding D2) which NO LONGER EXISTS (resolved earlier -> 3 files now), and implied ~1 occurrence in modPotentialParents.R which had since grown to 4 internal helpers -> real scope = 5 removals / 2 files, not the implied ~2. grep -rn "@keywords internal" R/ before scoping – the audit is a sample, not an inventory. (4) A clean PR merge waits for CI 10/10 GREEN + mergeStateStatus CLEAN, not UNSTABLE. At orientation PR #106’s re-run (triggered by S253’s close-out-docs commit, Learning 235 (2)) was IN_PROGRESS -> watched it to 10/10 via a background gh pr checks --watch rather than merging into UNSTABLE (which would waste the PR-for-CI cert S253 chose); then gh pr merge 106 --merge --delete-branch -> merge commit, local FF-sync, stub-stash pop. Did NOT merge before owner direction or unilaterally (FM #13 / S246->S247 split). (5) Direct-merge cert = local --as-cran + landed-tree sanity check (Learning 238 reused). No CI on a direct-merge, so the freshly-run local gate (0/0/2) on the exact committed tree IS the cert; confirmed the merge brought the right content with a cheap deterministic check on master (the @keywords internal census = only the package doc; the merge stat = only the 2 R/ files, 5 deletions; the NAMESPACE/man/ diff empty). (6) Two owner-directed deliverables in one session is owner-set scope, not FM #2 scope creep. The owner said “merge pr #106” then “then stage 6”; handled as two sequential items with full verification each and ONE combined close-out – the “1 and done” rule guards against AGENT self-direction, which did not happen here. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a landing/process + REFACTOR-class doc-metadata session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S254, merge PR #106 [Stage 5, merge commit fc0b2b72] + issue #103 Stage 6 internal-marker cleanup -> code commit b3a3fbe0, direct-merged via 318ed491, 5 @keywords internal removed / 2 files, gate 0/0/2, zero man/+NAMESPACE drift)

Learning 241 – The troff/LaTeX single-quote idiom `word' (open-backtick, close-apostrophe) is a rendered-doc DEFECT under roxygen markdown = TRUE, and the fix is \sQuote{} – verified by RENDERING the .Rd, not by reading the source diff. Owner pick = “get_and_or_list” -> fix the carried-open defect in R/get_and_or_list.R:10 (@param conjunction). REFACTOR-class documentation fix – no logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (2 owner gates via AskUserQuestion: fix approach \sQuote{}; landing method direct-merge). (1) Why `word' breaks under markdown mode. With DESCRIPTION markdown = TRUE, every backtick is an inline-code delimiter, so `and' or `or' with `and' pairs the 1st+2nd backticks into a code span (\verb{and' or }), leaves or' literal, and leaves the trailing single `and' as a stray backtick -> the garbled .Rd This is usually \verb{and' or }or' withand’. The author intended typographic single quotes (the troff ``x’ idiom), which markdown does not honor. **(2) The fix is `\sQuote{}` (or `\dQuote{}`), the canonical Rd macro for directional single/double quotes -- and it SURVIVES markdown mode.** CommonMark only backslash-escapes ASCII punctuation, so `\sQuote{and}` passes through unchanged (backslash+letter stays literal; braces are not markdown-special) and roxygen emits it verbatim into the `.Rd`, where Rd renders it as 'and' in HTML and PDF. Confirmed empirically: after `document()`, `man/get_and_or_list.Rd` reads `\sQuote{and} or \sQuote{or} with \sQuote{and}` and `Rd2txt` renders 'and' or 'or' with 'and'. **(3) This defect class is INVISIBLE in a source diff and even in a naive `.Rd` scan -- you must RENDER to verify.** The source looks like intentional quoting; the `.Rd` `\verb{...}` looks like a valid macro. Only rendering (`tools::Rd2txt`/`Rd2HTML`) exposes the garble. So the proof surface for a rendered-doc fix is the RENDERED output, plus `man/` drift limited to the one file and an empty NAMESPACE diff. **(4) A doc-only fix has no RED/GREEN but IS rendered-drift -- unlike the Stage 1-6 zero-drift work.** No behavior/logic/NAMESPACE change (function body, return, examples, exports all untouched; no dedicated test needed -- the fn is exercised indirectly by 6 R files), so TDD RED/GREEN is N/A and it is REFACTOR-class; but unlike the Stage 4 markup sweep (rendered-neutral) or Stage 6 (`@noRd`-inert), this INTENTIONALLY changes the rendered `.Rd`, so the success test is "rendered output now correct", not "zero drift". Keep line length <=80 by wrapping the roxygen prose across two `#'` lines (Rd fills paragraphs -> intra-paragraph breaks render identically), [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]. **(5) grep confirmed NO sibling word'`` defects** --grep -rn “`[a-zA-Z]*‘” R/matched only this one line (Learning 231: verify the live tree, do not assume the class is isolated). **(6) Landing = direct-merge (owner gate), cert = local–as-cran0/0/2 + landed-tree sanity check (Learning 238).** Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class documentation fix -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S255, fixget_and_or_listrendered-doc defect -> branchfix-get-and-or-list-squote, code commitb0d8e3e8, direct-merged viaa23e4eb9,x3 / 1 R file +.Rd`, gate 0/0/2, rendered ’and’/‘or’/‘and’)

Learning 242 – Adding runnable roxygen @examples is REFACTOR-class doc work, but UNLIKE the Stage 1-6 zero-drift stages it BOTH changes rendered man/ AND changes what R CMD check EXECUTES (the examples now RUN) – so the proof surface is “checking examples ... OK under --as-cran”, not “zero drift”, and each drafted example should be RUN (via load_all) BEFORE placement to avoid a failed-gate round-trip. Owner pick = “Stage 7 – examples policy” (issue #103 audit Finding 1 / §7). REFACTOR-class; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (3 owner gates via AskUserQuestion: scope [complete Stage 7], policy-doc home [new docs/ file], landing [PR-for-CI]). (1) The guard ladder – one rung, lowest honest: bare-runnable (cheap/deterministic/side-effect-free, inputs from data//inst//inline literals, file writes to tempdir()) = DEFAULT and preferred (it is continuously checked); \donttest = safe-but-genuinely-SLOW only (it runs under --run-donttest, so NOT for network dependence); \dontrun = genuinely un-runnable (live LabKey/EHR/network, launches the app, or needs an unshipped file). if (interactive()) is RETIRED as an example guard -> use \dontrun for app launchers. (2) “One guard ladder” requires fixing EXISTING mis-rung guards, not just adding new examples – a completeness critic caught that adding-examples-only would leave the ladder half-harmonized: 5 \dontrun blocks actually ran clean (inline/pedGood inputs, message-only side effects) -> demoted to bare-runnable (makeFounderStatsTable, makeGeneticSummaryTable, logModuleEvent, safeExecute, runQcStudbook); 4 LabKey \donttest need a live EHR -> promoted to \dontrun (getDemographics, getLkDirectAncestors, getLkDirectRelatives, setLabKeyDefaults). (3) The audit’s exemption list was WRONG for 2 of 32 (Learning 231 again – an audit is a sample; read the BODY, not the name): shouldShowChangedColsTab (returns a logical) and processQcStudbookResult (returns a list of data.frames) were labeled “tab-UI” but contain NO Shiny code -> they are directly callable and DO need examples (add-set = 9, not 7). The census otherwise confirmed the audit with ZERO drift (180 exported, 148 with @examples, 32 without). Correctly-exempt: 18 mod*UI/Server, appUI/appServer, the genuine tabPanel() builders getChangedColsTab/getErrorTab, and the deprecated alias makeGrpNum. (4) A file-writing example stays bare-runnable by targeting tempdir() (saveDataframesAsFiles, matching its own test). (5) A new docs/ policy file needs a .gitignore whitelist: .gitignore has docs/* + per-subdir !docs/<dir>/ whitelists, so a new docs/conventions/ROXYGEN_EXAMPLES_POLICY.md is IGNORED until you add !docs/conventions/ + !docs/conventions/**; docs/ is .Rbuildignored, so a dev-process policy there ships nowhere ([[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]]). (6) A read-only discovery Workflow (parallel census of exported-without-examples + guard inventory + example-data inventory, then per-utility draft + adversarial actually-run verify, then a critic over the classifications; 18 agents) gathered evidence BEFORE the owner scope gate – it confirmed the audit and surfaced the 2 mislabels + the mis-rung guards. (7) Landing = PR-for-CI (owner gate): adding/un-guarding examples makes them EXECUTE on every CI platform, and CI is the ONLY cross-platform verification (the local --as-cran gate is macOS-only) – so PR-for-CI is worth more here than for a rendered-neutral stage. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class documentation session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S256, issue #103 Stage 7 examples policy -> branch issue103-stage7-examples, code commit d6e6b8dc, PR #107; +@examples on 9 fns, 2 if(interactive())->\dontrun, 5 \dontrun->runnable, 4 \donttest->\dontrun, policy doc, gate 0/0/2 examples ... OK)

Learning 243 – A roadmap “stage” that bundles TWO judgment-heavy findings is multi-session work – split it by Finding at an owner scope-gate, and re-scan MULTI-EXPORT files before trusting a first-title-per-file census. Owner pick = “Stage 8” (the LAST #103 stage), but Stage 8 = Finding 3 (title voice, ~60-70 rewrites) AND Finding 6 (@inheritParams/@family de-dup, 66-file ped drift); the audit itself said “consider splitting across sessions.” REFACTOR-class, rendered-drift; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (3 owner gates via AskUserQuestion: slice = Finding 3; Shiny = exempt; approve-table = approve-all; landing = PR-for-CI). (1) Present the scope reality, then let the owner pick the slice. A cheap deterministic census (grep titles + classify voice; grep @param ped/ids/kmat/threshold distinct descriptions) sized both findings BEFORE any workflow – Finding 3 = ~100 exported non-conforming titles, Finding 6 = @param ped in 66 files with ~20 distinct descriptions – proving one session can’t hold both. The scope gate is the AUTHOR’s call to POSE, the owner’s to DECIDE (do the heavy discovery Workflow AFTER the gate, on the chosen slice only – S256’s over-heavy-workflow weakness). (2) A first-#'-title-per-file census UNDERCOUNTS titles in multi-export files. Each mod* file exports BOTH a UI and a Server function, each with its OWN title (X Module - UI Function at the top, X Module - Server Function ~100 lines down); autoIdFormat.R exports Get+Set. The census caught only the first -> re-scan every file with >=2 @export before finalizing the work-list (here it revealed 9 hidden mod Server titles, tied to the Shiny decision). (3) Title voice is rendered-drift but NAMESPACE-neutral -> proof = every title matches its approved target (0 mismatch) + empty NAMESPACE diff + --as-cran Rd/examples OK. Titles populate \title{} only; no @export/import change. PREDICTED then PROVED the empty NAMESPACE diff. The success test is NOT zero-drift (99 .Rd intentionally change) – it is “titles harmonized + gate green”. (4) A guarded bulk-edit script + full re-census is the safe way to apply ~100 deterministic title edits; reserve hand-edits for special cases. The script replaces the title paragraph (first run of non-blank non-@tag #' lines) with one imperative line, GUARDED by “the file’s current first title line is a prefix of the recorded old_title” and “paragraph <=4 lines” (skip+report otherwise) – 96/100 applied, 0 skips; then re-extract every title and assert == target (0 mismatches). The 4 explicit-@description reverts were hand-edited. (5) Reverting a malformed braced @description{...} to implicit fixes a latent D1-class defect AND reorganizes – render to confirm. roxygen’s multi-paragraph rule: 1st para = title, 2nd = description, LATER paras -> \details. So dropping @description{/} on findGeneration/groupAddAssign moved their secondary paragraphs into \details (all text preserved – Rd2txt confirmed no literal braces, grep confirmed “maximal independent set”/“Subsequent trips” still present). Two inline @description reverts are trivial prefix-drops. (6) A duplicate SOURCE file needs both blocks synced so the ONE generated .Rd is deterministic. getSimSires.R DEFINES and @exports getPotentialSires (a near-identical duplicate of getPotentialSires.R); only man/getPotentialSires.Rd exists and NAMESPACE exports it once. Editing one block’s title could let collation order pick either -> set BOTH to the SAME new title (“List potential sires”); the duplicate file is a separate CODE defect surfaced for a future dedup session (out of Finding-3 scope). (7) Shiny mod*/app titles exempted (owner), parallel to the Stage-7 @examples exemption – descriptive X Module - UI/Server Function is a recognized module convention; converting all 20 (11 UI + 9 hidden Server) to Build the X module UI is churn for arguable benefit, and would introduce the informal “GeneKeepR” name in appUI/appServer. (8) Landing = PR-for-CI (owner gate): large man/ churn (99 pages) benefits from cross-platform Rd checks (line-width/cross-reference can vary by platform); the local --as-cran gate is macOS-only. Do NOT merge unilaterally (FM #13 / owner’s call). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class documentation session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S257, issue #103 Stage 8a title voice -> branch issue103-stage8a-title-voice, code commit 8e76e49a; 100 exported titles normalized + 4 @description reverts, 99 .Rd, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 0/0/2 examples ... OK, PR #108 for CI)

Learning 244 – De-dup @param drift with @inheritParams by DELETING ONLY THE TARGET @param LINE – that makes donor choice collision-proof; keep formals that share a NAME but not a MEANING on SEPARATE donors; and an @family tag on a @noRd function is inert. Owner scope-gate (via AskUserQuestion) = slice “Safe formals + @family” of issue #103 Stage 8b – Finding 6 (@inheritParams/@family de-dup), deferring the big @param ped drift (66 files, ~46 genuine-variant meanings) to its own session. REFACTOR-class doc work; no R-logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change (NAMESPACE diff empty); TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (2 owner gates: scope; landing = PR-for-CI). (1) @inheritParams donor inherits ONLY the callee formals that are (a) in the callee’s signature, (b) NOT documented locally, AND (c) documented on the donor – so if the edit removes ONLY the one @param <formal> line, the ONE now-undocumented shared formal is the ONLY thing inherited. Donor choice therefore carries ZERO collision risk regardless of the donor’s other formals (e.g. @inheritParams getParents on getOffspring(pedSourceDf, ids) inherits only ids, never pedSourceDf; @inheritParams calcGU on calcA inherits only threshold, leaving alleles/byID locally documented). This kills the audit’s “pick a minimal-signature donor” worry – any function that documents the formal well is a safe donor. (2) A formal shared by TWO meanings needs TWO donors – never one. @param threshold names an integer allele-rarity max-copies count in calc* (donor calcGU) AND a numeric minimum-kinship level in group formation (donor filterThreshold); merging them into one donor would silently mis-document one cluster. Census by DISTINCT description text (not just occurrence count) to catch this: 6 occ / 4 distinct split cleanly 3-and-3 across the two meanings. A single function (getAnimalsWithHighKinship) legitimately carries TWO @inheritParams tags (kmat from meanKinship, threshold from filterThreshold) – roxygen allows it. (3) De-dup only where meaning is truly generic; keep genuine variants bespoke. @param ids = 24 occ / 15 distinct -> only 12 were the plain “character vector of animal IDs” (converted); the other 12 carry real callee-specific meaning (“or NULL to restrict the analysis”, “to be flagged as part of the group/population”, “original IDs”, “to test”) and were LEFT bespoke. kmat similarly: 5 generic -> meanKinship; 3 “dense, symmetric, id-named” / “proband” variants kept. The de-dup target is drift, not count. (4) An @family tag on a @noRd function is INERT – exclude it. roxygen generates no .Rd for @noRd, so the family “See also” renders nowhere and the function never appears in siblings’ family lists. calcFounderContributions (an internal @noRd helper) was dropped from the genetic value analysis family; only the 8 EXPORTED calc* got the tag. (5) A multi-export file needs the @family tag on EVERY exported block, anchored on the function definition. Each mod* file exports a UI and a Server function (18 blocks / 9 files), each its own #' block; the guarded apply script found each block by walking up from the name <- function line to its own @export and inserting @family there – tagging only the first would drop half the family. The 4 @noRd helpers in modPotentialParents.R correctly got no tag (no @export to anchor on). (6) document() IS the @inheritParams correctness gate. Inheritance resolves at document() time; an unresolved/misspelled donor warns loudly. A clean document() + EMPTY NAMESPACE diff + --as-cran code/documentation mismatches ... OK together prove the de-dup introduced no doc/signature mismatch. (7) Adversarial per-callee verification BEFORE editing pays off on subtle-semantics doc work. A 24-agent read-only Workflow (one agent per proposed @inheritParams, reading the callee BODY + donor to judge donor-accuracy AND meaning-loss; one per family for membership + export structure) returned 20/20 CONFIRM and surfaced the @noRd-family-inert point, the ancestors-vs-relatives naming nuance, and two out-of-scope defects – cheaper than a failed-gate round-trip or a shipped wrong description. (8) Pass Workflow candidate data INLINE, not via args – a nested args object arrived undefined (args.inherit.map threw); embedding the plan as const in the script was robust. (9) Surfaced but DEFERRED (scope discipline, FM #8): getPedDirectRelatives.R’s description body + @return still say “Gets direct ancestors from labkey study schema” (wrong for a source-agnostic pedigree fn; title already fixed in 8a) – a description-accuracy defect noted, NOT fixed mid-de-dup; likewise reportGV as a family anchor and the @param ped half left for their own sessions. (10) Landing = PR-for-CI (owner gate): 42 man/ pages + the package’s FIRST @family tags benefit from cross-platform Rd/cross-reference checks; local --as-cran is macOS-only. Do NOT merge unilaterally (FM #13). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class documentation session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S259, issue #103 Stage 8b de-dup -> branch issue103-stage8b-dedup, code commit bafaa1e8; 20 @inheritParams conversions / 19 files + 25 @family tags, 38 R/ + 42 .Rd, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 1 NOTE examples ... OK, PR for CI)

Learning 245 – De-dup of @param ped (Finding 6’s big half) is CLUSTER-aware, not single-donor: the 46 distinct descriptions split by MEANING into (a) pure-phrasing-drift clusters – generic “pedigree information” and “…IDs listed in candidates” – and (b) requirement-bearing clusters – required-fields (id/sire/dam), complete-pedigree (req. fields + “no partial parentage” / “stops with an error”), demographic (birth/death/departure/exit), and genuine one-offs (nullable, “not checked”, cross-ref). Only the (a) clusters collapse safely to ONE donor each (reportGV for generic, getPotentialSires for candidates); the (b) descriptions carry real per-function information that a blind @inheritParams would ERASE, so they stay bespoke (owner scope-gate picked (a) only this session; (b) deferred). REFACTOR-class doc de-dup – no logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (1 owner scope-gate). (1) Adversarial per-callee verify catches MIS-clustered files that the census text alone would wave through. filterPairs.R had copy-pasted the candidates text (“including the IDs listed in candidates”) but has NO candidates formal and no candidate linkage in its body – so pointing it at the candidates donor would assert something factually false. A 28-agent read-only workflow (reads each callee BODY + donor; judges donor-accuracy AND meaning-loss) returned 27 CONFIRM / 1 KEEP_BESPOKE, flagging exactly that file. Matching the census cluster is necessary but NOT sufficient; the body decides. (2) The over-inheritance proof for a MULTI-param donor is the per-.Rd diff, not just the aggregate NAMESPACE diff. getPotentialSires documents ids (via its own @inheritParams getParents), and 3 candidate callees have an ids formal not literally @param-documented – a naive “undocumented formal the donor documents” check flags a collision. But those 3 already @inheritParams getParents (which supplies ids and wins by FIRST-MATCH order), so ids is safe. Proven empirically after document() by asserting every changed .Rd changed ONLY \item{ped} and that \item{ids} was UNCHANGED in those 3 files. This complements S259’s “delete only the target @param line” rule (which prevents over-inherit for LOCALLY-documented params) by also covering the inherited-elsewhere case: keep the earlier @inheritParams first, and append the new one at the old @param ped position (which already sits after it). (3) A source de-dup can render byte-identically – 12 of 27 files here produced NO .Rd delta because their old @param ped text already matched the donor exactly; the SOURCE still got cleaner (one @inheritParams vs a drifted @param). So the collapse proof is the SOURCE-level re-census (@param ped 67->40 occurrences / 46->33 distinct), not the count of changed man pages. (4) code/documentation mismatches ... OK under --as-cran is the @inheritParams correctness gate (an unresolved donor also warns at document() time); here it passed with examples/tests/Rd/vignettes all OK and the sole archived-maintainer NOTE.

Learning 246 – A roxygen @param description CONTINUES across blank #' lines (they are paragraph breaks, not block terminators) until the NEXT @tag – so a byte-identical census that stops at the first blank line SILENTLY MERGES a caveat-bearing description into its caveat-free base cluster, hiding exactly the requirement-bearing text that MUST stay bespoke. Owner scope-gate = “byte-identical clusters only” of issue #103 Stage 8b-ped continued (the requirement-bearing clusters S259/S260 deferred). REFACTOR-class doc de-dup – no logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change (NAMESPACE diff empty, 0 man/ changes); TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (2 owner gates: scope; landing = direct-merge). (1) The census bug that nearly corrupted two GVA functions. A first-pass census extractor broke each @param ped block at the first blank #' line; that made calcFEFG (base + blank + “The pedigree must have no partial parentage …; calcFEFG stops with an error otherwise”) and calcRetention (base + blank + “It is assumed that the pedigree has no partial parentage”) normalize IDENTICAL to the caveat-free base (calcFE / calcFounderContributions) and mis-cluster as “byte-identical” – a blind @inheritParams calcFE would have DELETED both caveats (the exact corruption the handoff warned of). Fix the extractor to continue through blank #' lines, stopping only at the next @tag/non-roxygen line (corrected census: 40 occ / 33 distinct, was 31); and READ the actual block before editing as the backstop – that read is what caught it here, before any edit. (2) SHA1-equal clusters are the SAFEST possible collapse – proof is ZERO man/ delta. Chose only the 3 SHA1-equal clusters (C3a sire+dam x4 donor trimPedigree; C5-base req-fields x2 donor calcFE; C4 id-only x2 donor setPopulation); after document(), git showed ONLY the 5 R/ files changed with man/ AND NAMESPACE UNCHANGED. Each rendering inheritor’s \item{ped} renders byte-for-byte from the donor, so its .Rd is untouched -> the SOURCE re-census (@param ped 40->35) is the collapse proof, not any man-page diff (cf. Learning 245 (3)). (3) @inheritParams DONOR works cleanly INTO an @noRd function (rendered-invisible, source-only de-dup) but an @noRd function is a POOR DONOR (no .Rd to inherit from) – flip the direction. C4’s resetGroup is @noRd -> made the exported setPopulation the donor; C5-base’s calcFounderContributions is @noRd -> inherits from the exported calcFE. document() clean, no orphan .Rd, no warning; 2 of the 5 collapses (calcFounderContributions, resetGroup) are therefore source-only (no rendered benefit, still valid drift-reduction). (4) A donor that documents ONLY the shared formal is over-inheritance-proof by construction (calcFE documents ped alone); for multi-param donors (trimPedigree: probands/ped/removeUninformative/addBackParents; setPopulation: ped/ids) the S259 “delete ONLY the target @param line” rule + locally-documented-wins keeps inheritance to ped alone – PROVEN by the empty man/ diff (no new \item{} appeared). (5) A factual scope correction is NOT a re-gate. The owner’s decision was “byte-identical clusters only”; the census bug meant C5-base/C4 delivered 1 inheritor each (not the gate’s stated 3+1) because calcFEFG/calcRetention were never byte-identical. Excluding them is REQUIRED by the owner’s own criterion – reported transparently and proceeded without a redundant AskUserQuestion (FM #7/#23; [[observation-vs-decision]]). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class documentation session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S262, issue #103 Stage 8b-ped continued -> direct-merge to master; 5 @inheritParams byte-identical collapses / 5 R/, 0 man/ changes, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 1 NOTE with code/documentation mismatches ... OK, lint 0, spell clean)

Learning 247 – Prose similarity is NOT semantic similarity: a near-identical @param description can itself be a COPY-PASTE bug, so READ THE BODY before collapsing a handoff-suggested “near-identical pair.” This generalizes Learning 246 (which fixed the census extractor) up one level to the handoff’s own cluster claims: a predecessor’s “collapsible pair” is a hypothesis to verify against the code, not a fact to execute. Owner scope-gate (via AskUserQuestion) = “id/sire/dam cluster (3)” of issue #103 Stage 8b-ped continued (the phrasing-variant + demographic clusters S262 deferred). REFACTOR-class doc de-dup – no logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change (NAMESPACE diff empty); TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (2 owner gates: scope; landing = direct-merge). (1) The C6 “demographic pair” was a mirage. S262’s handoff said createPedTree + setExit were “a near-identical pair (1-word Oxford-comma diff -> collapsible with trivial normalization)” – their @param ped prose IS near-identical. But createPedTree’s BODY uses only id, sire, dam (its own line-21 comment: “This function uses only id, sire, and dam columns”; zero birth/death/departure/exit references), whereas setExit genuinely computes exit from death/departure. So createPedTree’s demographic @param ped is copy-paste drift FROM setExit, and collapsing the “pair” would have CEMENTED the wrong description onto createPedTree. Reading the body refuted the pair and turned it into a surfaced defect (fix createPedTree to id/sire/dam in its own session). Lesson: treat a predecessor’s cluster claim exactly like a census cluster – necessary signal, but the body decides (cf. Learning 245 (1)). (2) A phrasing-variant collapse CHANGES the callee’s rendered text to the donor’s, so it DOES produce man/ churn (unlike S262’s byte-identical, 0-man/-delta collapses) – and the collapse can double as a bug fix. The 3 collapses (convertRelationships, getFounders, removeAutoGenIds -> @inheritParams getDescendantPedigree) each rewrote one \item{ped} to the donor’s canonical text; removeAutoGenIds’s rewrite also corrected its “dame” -> “dam” typo. The over-inheritance proof is still the per-.Rd diff (ONLY \item{ped} changed; \item{kmat}/\item{ids} intact and in order) plus the source re-census (@param ped 35->32). (3) A SECOND @inheritParams supplies an undocumented shared formal only if no EARLIER @inheritParams documents it (first-match wins). convertRelationships already had @inheritParams meanKinship (for kmat); before adding @inheritParams getDescendantPedigree at the old @param ped position, verified meanKinship documents ONLY kmat – so the new tag cleanly supplies ped and getDescendantPedigree’s other formal probands (not a convertRelationships arg) is ignored. Check the donor is EXPORTED (has an .Rd to inherit from) – getDescendantPedigree is; getPedigreeSource/unknown2NA are @noRd and were correctly excluded/kept-bespoke. (4) Right-size the verify + the landing to the churn. A 3-collapse slice warranted a 3-agent read-only refuter Workflow (not the 24-28-agent fleets of S259/S260) – one adversarial agent per proposed collapse, all 3 CONFIRM. And 3 pure-prose .Rd changes (NAMESPACE-neutral, Rd cross-references ... OK locally) warrant DIRECT-MERGE (S262 low-churn precedent), not PR-for-CI (which S259/S260 reserved for 42-52-.Rd churn / executing examples). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class documentation session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S263, issue #103 Stage 8b-ped continued -> direct-merge to master; 3 @inheritParams getDescendantPedigree phrasing-variant collapses / 3 R/ + 3 .Rd, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 1 NOTE with code/documentation mismatches ... OK, lint 0, spell clean; C6 pair refuted, createPedTree + removeDuplicates surfaced)

Learning 248 – A duplicate function-definition .R file (two @exported roxygen blocks defining/documenting the SAME object) is INVISIBLE in NAMESPACE but shows up as a DOUBLED .Rd: roxygen2 silently MERGES the two same-topic blocks, so the man page cites BOTH source files on line 2 (% Please edit documentation in R/getPotentialSires.R, R/getSimSires.R) and renders \usage/\value/\description/\examples TWICE. Deleting the redundant file + document() collapses each to one copy with an EMPTY NAMESPACE diff – roxygen dedupes the two @exports to a single export() line, so removing one file never moves NAMESPACE (predicted, then proven: sha 2560cecd... identical before/after). Owner approach-gate + landing-gate via AskUserQuestion on issue #103’s last NON-doc-only surfaced item. REFACTOR-class dead-duplicate removal – zero behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (2 owner gates: approach = delete + re-document; landing = direct-merge). (1) The doubled .Rd is the tell of a duplicate function-definition file – and a latent issue #109 doc defect. The rendered help page for getPotentialSires showed its usage, value, description, and full example block twice (line 2 named R/getPotentialSires.R, R/getSimSires.R); the fix (delete the dup, re-document) reduced the .Rd to a single clean copy – so grepping man/*.Rd line-2 comments for entries that cite two source files is a cheap detector for the whole class. (2) Deletion-safety for a duplicate .R is a five-part FIRSTHAND chain, not a trust of the handoff’s “likely delete”. (a) diff the two files -> the function BODY is byte-identical (only cosmetic roxygen whitespace + a 1L vs 1 example differ), so source load order is immaterial to runtime; (b) the surviving file is the CANONICAL one (name matches the function, owns the .Rd, uses the correct integer 1L); (c) every reference across R/ + tests/ + vignettes + inst/_pkgdown.yml is to the FUNCTION getPotentialSires (survives in getPotentialSires.R), NONE to the file – a bare-string sweep for getSimSires hit only SESSION_NOTES.md; (d) NAMESPACE diff empty; (e) the file is committed/tracked (SAFEGUARDS “verify committed before rm”) with no source()/Collate path reference. (3) “Was this file ever a REAL distinct function that got clobbered?” needs git log --all -S "<name> <- function", not just the working tree. The file is named getSimSires.R -> a reader might fear it once held a real getSimSires (breeding-sim, cf. issue #10) that a bad copy-paste overwrote, which would argue for RESTORE not DELETE. git log --all -S "getSimSires <- function" returned EMPTY across all history -> the function NEVER existed; the filename was aspirational. Nothing to restore -> delete is unambiguously correct. (4) A non-doc-only deletion reuses the Learning 239 verify triad: empty NAMESPACE diff + single-file man/ diff + INSTALLED-namespace smoke (not load_all). Confirmed document() touched ONLY getPotentialSires.Rd (no downstream @inheritParams getPotentialSires churn – the ~10 callees’ .Rd were untouched because the merged param text was unaffected), full test suite 0/0 (1334 contexts; test_groupAddAssign + test_makeGroupMembers are the getPotentialSires regression gate), --as-cran 0/0/2 with code/documentation mismatches ... OK, and – against the .Rcheck INSTALLED namespace – getPotentialSires still exported + callable end-to-end (returned a length-3 vector), getSimSires correctly absent. (5) Right-size: a fully DETERMINISTIC evidence chain (diff + grep + git-log + build gate) does NOT need an adversarial Workflow. The load-bearing claim (“safe to delete”) is proven by byte-level diff + NAMESPACE identity + installed smoke, not a probabilistic finding needing independent refutation (cf. Learning 239’s deterministic import proof); prior sessions right-sized the same way. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a REFACTOR-class dead-duplicate-removal session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S268, issue #103 -> direct-merge to master; deleted R/getSimSires.R [byte-identical duplicate of getPotentialSires], regenerated getPotentialSires.Rd collapsed 2x->1x, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 0/0/2 with code/documentation mismatches ... OK, lint 0, spell clean, installed-namespace smoke OK)

Learning 249 – A dormant, never-exercised parameter branch is NOT a safe no-op: RUN it before trusting either a “has no effect” characterization OR a predecessor’s one-line suggested fix – both are hypotheses, and strict TDD’s RED step is what falsifies them. getPedDirectRelatives(unrelatedParents = TRUE) was flagged by S269 as “no effect (same as FALSE); GREEN = assign the discarded addIdRecords() return.” Running it on a pedigree with a referenced-but-absent parent (Z named as a dam with no ego record) falsified BOTH: it CRASHES (replacement has 1 row, data has 0), and assigning the return errors at the SAME line – because addIdRecords() retrieves rows FROM fullPed (fullPed[fullPed$id %in% ids, ]), and here fullPed == ped with the unrelated ids absent from ped BY CONSTRUCTION, so the retrieval is always 0 rows and addToPed$sire <- NA fails on a 0-row frame. First STRICT-TDD behavior fix of the long #103 doc stretch. Behavior fix to an exported fn – RED -> GREEN; REFACTOR none needed; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (owner gates via AskUserQuestion: open issue #114 first; approach = implement the documented placeholder behavior; PRE-RED->RED, RED->GREEN, GREEN->REFACTOR; NEWS = yes; landing = PR-for-CI). (1) A predecessor’s suggested GREEN for dead code is a HYPOTHESIS; RED is what falsifies it. S269’s “assign the return” was plausible from reading the branch statically, but the branch had never run. Writing the RED test FIRST surfaced the crash AND proved the naive fix wrong BEFORE any implementation – the exact payoff of tests-first on a never-executed path. This lifts Learning 247 (a handoff’s cluster claim is a hypothesis to verify against the body) from doc de-dup to behavior work: the handoff’s fix is a hypothesis too. (2) The right fix SYNTHESIZES rows; do not reuse a helper built for a different shape. addIdRecords() is designed for a TWO-TIER case (a fullPed strictly larger than partialPed, ids PRESENT in fullPed) – its only real caller addBackSecondParents.R:51 passes such ids and it is correct there. In getPedDirectRelatives there is ONE ped and the unrelated ids exist nowhere, so no retrieval helper can produce them; the branch must CONSTRUCT placeholder rows (relatives[rep(NA_integer_, n), ] for an all-NA row with the right columns, then $id <- unrelated, $sire/$dam <- NA, rbind, reset rownames). Leave the correct-for-its-own-caller helper untouched (scope discipline, FM #8). (3) Running the actual function IS the proof – no adversarial Workflow needed for a deterministic crash. A ~20-line REPL probe under pkgload::load_all (the FALSE result, the TRUE result via tryCatch, the simulated “assign the return”, and the documented TARGET output) established every load-bearing claim deterministically; a fan-out of skeptic agents adds nothing over executing the code (right-sizing, cf. Learning 248 (5)). (4) A behavior change to an exported fn takes the FULL non-doc gate + Phase-3E installed-namespace smoke + NEWS + PR-for-CI – unlike the S262-S269 doc-only direct-merges. Verified: RED (2 test_that added; the placeholder test FAILED with the documented error, the no-unrelated-parents guard passed), GREEN (both pass), full suite 0 real failed / 0 real errors (1166 real contexts, NOT_CRAN=true), lintr 0, spell_check_package clean, NAMESPACE diff EMPTY (signature unchanged), --as-cran 0/0/2 with code/documentation mismatches ... OK + a re-grepped testthat.R [66s/66s] OK, and a .Rcheck installed-namespace smoke (imports enforced, NOT load_all) returning the Z placeholder row with NA parents. NEWS.Rmd dev-version bullet added + NEWS.md regenerated (\#114 matches the file’s existing escaped issue-ref style). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was a STRICT-TDD behavior-fix session – RED -> GREEN executed; REFACTOR = none. (S270, issue #114 -> branch fix/issue-114-unrelated-parents, fix commit 4bbfb32a, PR #115 for CI; R/getPedDirectRelatives.R synthesize-placeholders + 2 tests + NEWS, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 0/0/2, lint 0, spell clean, Phase-3E installed-namespace smoke OK)

Learning 250 – gh pr merge --rebase --delete-branch run from the PR branch does the REMOTE merge FIRST (the PR merges and a “Fixes #NN” issue closes immediately), THEN tries to switch off the branch to delete it locally – so ANY uncommitted working-tree file aborts that second step (local changes to <file> would be overwritten by checkout) and leaves BOTH the local and remote branch undeleted with the merge already applied. The merge is NOT rolled back by the failed delete. Owner-directed landing of PR #115 (the S270 issue #114 fix); merge method owner-gated (AskUserQuestion) = Rebase. Landing/merge of already-CI-certified code – no new R/ change, no local re-gate (10/10 green CI + a proven byte-identical tree ARE the certification); TDD N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. (1) Ensure a FULLY clean tree before invoking gh pr merge --delete-branch, not just the known standing-keep. This session self-inflicted the abort: .DS_Store (the tracked standing keep) was stashed pre-emptively for the rebase-pull, but the S271 SESSION_NOTES stub written moments earlier on the same branch was NOT – and that dirty file is what blocked the post-merge checkout. Stash/commit EVERYTHING (or run the merge from a clean state) first. (2) If it aborts, VERIFY the merge before any git surgery, then finish cleanup by hand. gh pr view <N> --json state,mergedAt,mergeCommit + gh issue view <NN> --json state confirmed MERGED + CLOSED before I touched the tree ([[check-status-before-destructive-git]]) – do not assume the whole command failed just because its last step did. Then: stash the dirty file, git checkout master, git pull (fast-forward to the merge commit), pop the stashes back (carefully, preserving any UNRELATED stash lower in the stack), and complete the --delete-branch intent manually. (3) delete_branch_on_merge=false at repo level means NOTHING auto-deletes (gh api repos/OWNER/REPO --jq .delete_branch_on_merge) – the branch must be deleted explicitly: git push origin --delete <branch> (remote) + git branch -D <branch> (local). (4) A REBASE-merge gives the landed commits NEW, non-ancestor SHAs, so the local feature branch is not an ancestor of master and git branch -d (safe) REFUSES it; use git branch -D (force) – but ONLY after proving the content is on master: git diff <branch> <mergeCommit> --stat must be EMPTY (identical trees; the rebase preserved content, only re-parented it). Right-sized verification: a merge of tree-identical, CI-certified code needs a Phase-3E runtime smoke on the merged tree (ran test_getPedDirectRelatives.R, all pass), NOT a redundant local --as-cran. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]]. This was a landing/merge session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S271, issue #114 -> PR #115 rebase-merged to master @ 79125a0b [fix ce9edad0], issue #114 CLOSED, branch deleted local + remote, Phase-3E smoke on merged master OK)

Learning 251 – In a roxygen2 markdown-mode package (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)), a roxygen line preceded by a blank #' line and indented 4+ spaces becomes a markdown code block -> the generated .Rd wraps it in \preformatted{}; any raw Rd markup the author also put on that line (\code{...}, \link{...}, \strong{...}) then renders literally (verbatim), because markup is not processed inside \preformatted{}. That is issue #109’s error class (R/addAnimalsWithNoRelative.R:11 -> man/addAnimalsWithNoRelative.Rd: the man page showed the text \code{available[[i]] <- setdiff(...)} instead of the code). REFACTOR-class doc rendering fix – no logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides (3 owner gates: audit scope = confirmed-class-only, fix form = drop the \code{} wrapper, landing = direct-merge). (1) Fix = DROP the redundant \code{} wrapper and leave the indented line as a plain code block (markdown-mode idiom); do NOT un-indent it – that preserves the author’s intent to display the snippet. (2) Audit the class from the GENERATED .Rd, not the R/ source. The authoritative detector brace-matches each \preformatted{...} block in man/*.Rd and flags any \code/\link/\strong/… inside -> exactly 1 hit across all 203 .Rd. A grep of R/ source for indented \code{} lines OVER-REPORTS badly (~40 hits) because \item \code{} list entries and wrapped @param/@return prose are ALSO indented but render correctly – they are not blank-line-preceded code blocks. The rendered artifact is the arbiter: a real instance of the class appears inside \preformatted{} in the .Rd; for 39 of the 40 source hits it does not. (3) tools::checkRd() run standalone reports a “Non-ASCII contents without declared encoding” note that is a FALSE POSITIVE when DESCRIPTION declares Encoding: UTF-8 – R CMD check --as-cran reads the encoding and passes (clean per S270), so do not chase it. (4) Right-sized: a deterministic, artifact-proven finding (brace-matched detector + empty NAMESPACE diff + --as-cran Rd checks) needs no adversarial Workflow – a Workflow is for probabilistic/semantic audits, not a mechanical class the generated file settles exactly (owner picked confirmed-class-only over the offered broad multi-agent audit). Sibling of Learning 248 (a reusable detector for a distinct roxygen documentation-error class). (S272: issue #109 markup-in-preformatted fix; direct-merge to master; --as-cran 0/0/2 GREEN)

Learning 252 – R CMD check --as-cran/checkRd/document() cannot catch factually-wrong-but-valid-Rd documentation; only a doc-vs-code READ can – and the dominant source is copy-paste drift between SIBLING functions. Issue #109’s second clause (“audit the code for similar documentation errors”) was answered by a broad multi-agent semantic audit of all 203 man pages that found 38 confirmed doc errors, every one of which passes the full build gate cleanly (3 Critical / 22 Moderate / 13 Minor; 15 factual-mismatch, 12 wrong-return, 10 wrong-param, 1 misleading-render). Owner scope-gate (AskUserQuestion) = “Broad audit”. Analysis-only, REFACTOR-class; no R/ modified; fixes deferred to an owner-gated follow-on; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. (1) The deterministic layer is a red herring for #109 – clear it FIRST, then read. A pre-pass proved every mechanically-decidable class clean: all 8 S244 defects D1-D8 fixed; the #109 markup-in-\preformatted{} class = 0 (S272 holds); \link xrefs 100% resolve (206 \alias topics); doubled-.Rd = 0; empty \code{} = 0; data-doc \item columns == actual names(); and crucially devtools::document() = zero warnings + zero man/ drift, so the LITERAL reading of the issue title (“roxygen2 errors in functions within R”) is already false – the reported bug was a rendering error (fixed S272), and the real remaining surface is semantic accuracy, which no tool checks. Doing this pre-pass first (a) prevented re-reporting known-fixed items and (b) scoped the expensive agent fan-out to the one class that needs judgment. (2) The root cause is systemic copy-paste, not 38 independent typos – so fix the PATTERN. Prose describes a sibling function or an earlier version of the code: a getPotentialSires @param ped/@param minAge donor bug that cascades to calculateSexRatio/removePotentialSires via @inheritParams (4 findings, ONE donor edit); checkChangedColsLst/checkErrorLst (logical-scalar returns documented as “NULL / the list”); correctParentSex/removeDuplicates (reportErrors=TRUE alternate return undocumented); kinshipMatricesToKValues/kinshipMatrixToKValues (identical wrong “diagonal = 1.0” – this package’s kinship() uses coancestry, self-kinship (1+F)/2 = 0.5); saveDataframesAsFiles/makeExamplePedigreeFile (both document fileType="xlsx" but stopifnot accepts "excel"); getPedMaxAge/getPyramidAgeDist (both claim “live animals only” but neither filters); makeCEPH (documents the relationship cascade that lives in its caller convertRelationships). Several findings were confirmed by RUNNING the documented example (addAnimalsWithNoRelative returns a list not a df; offspringCounts has no id column; geneDrop column order reversed) – the running function is the arbiter, extending Learning 251’s “the generated .Rd is the arbiter”. (3) THIS is where an adversarial Workflow earns its keep – unlike the deterministic Learnings 248/251. A single detector settles a mechanical class exactly (248/251, rightly done solo); a “audit for similar errors” mandate spans an open-ended SEMANTIC space no detector covers, so it needs fan-out breadth + adversarial skepticism: 17 find-batches (~12 man pages each, doc read vs. source/body) -> per-finding verify (default-REFUTE, read the actual files) -> session-author firsthand re-verification of all 3 Critical + a cross-class Moderate/Minor sample + the 1 refuted (getRequiredCols – correctly refuted: “(optional)” qualifies the value form “Date or NA”, not column-omissibility; that the SAME pass confirmed the near-identical \code{N}-typo in getPossibleCols while refuting this shows the calibration held). 56 agents, 0 errors, 39 candidates -> 38 confirmed / 1 refuted (2.6% refute rate = well-scoped finders + same-bar verify, corroborated by the author sample holding in every case). (4) Do-the-delta ([[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]): read the prior audit BEFORE fanning out. docs/audits/ROXYGEN_HARMONIZATION_AUDIT_2026-06-29.md (S244) + the #103 work already covered harmonization (voice, @param de-dup, ordering) and its rendered defects D1-D8; #109’s lens is errors, a DIFFERENT axis. The agents were handed the already-clean deterministic results + the KEEP-BESPOKE / harmonization exclusions so they reported only genuine reader-facing errors, not #103 style nits. (5) Workflow tooling gotcha: the args value reaches the script as a JSON STRING, not a parsed array – slicing it silently yields character-chunks (a first run failed instantly with “batch.map is not a function”, 878 “batches” from a 10527-char string). Parse defensively at the top: const ROWS = typeof args === 'string' ? JSON.parse(args) : args. [[workflow-args-arrive-as-json-string]]. (6) FM #22 caught in the wild: mid-session git status showed .gitignore + inst/WORDLIST modified – NOT mine; a transcript grep (no agent touched them) + mtimes (21:09-21:11, before the workflow ran) proved they were the OWNER’s manual edits (the two small S272-suggested items: build-artifact gitignore rules + the errored WORDLIST hand-add). Left untouched; committed only the audit deliverable by explicit path ([[check-status-before-destructive-git]]). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. This was an AUDIT-class analysis session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A; the 38 fixes are a separate owner-gated deliverable. (S273, issue #109 broad audit -> docs/audits/ISSUE_109_DOC_ERROR_AUDIT_2026-07-04.md; 38 confirmed / 1 refuted; no R/ change; deterministic pre-pass all-clean; multi-agent semantic sweep + adversarial verify + author firsthand re-verification)

Learning 253 – An @inheritParams donor fix corrects INHERITED params but NOT a callee’s LOCAL duplicate of the same param – so after a donor edit, grep for local copies the audit’s “callees fix themselves” cascade analysis will miss. Fixing the 38 S273-audited doc errors (owner scope-gate via one AskUserQuestion, 4 questions: all-in-one / doc-fix-direction / defer #9 to #110 / direct-merge). REFACTOR-class, doc-only; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. 34 R/ files → 38 man/*.Rd (the extra 4 = the donor cascade). (1) The cascade discovery. Fixing getPotentialSires @param ped (candidates→ids, #34) propagated via @inheritParams to SIX pages – calculateSexRatio (#26), removePotentialSires (#32), AND three the audit never flagged (groupAddAssign, fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio, makeGroupMembers). But the audit’s §4 “the @inheritParams callees fix themselves” was WRONG for one param: removePotentialSires carried a LOCAL @param minAge (lines 7-9) with the same stale “group formation / kinships ignored” text as #11; a local @param OVERRIDES the inherited one, so the donor fix could not reach it – the audit enumerated the ped instance (#32) but missed the sibling minAge. Deleting the local duplicate so it inherits the corrected donor is the pattern fix the audit intended, and it is a 39th fix beyond the 38 findings. Lesson: a donor fix is only complete after a completeness grep proves no callee shadows the donor with a local copy. (2) Distinguish genuine pattern-instances from legitimate uses in that grep. grep 'minimum age to consider in group' hit 9 files; only the 2 non-group-formation siblings (getPotentialSires, removePotentialSires) were wrong – the other 7 ARE group-formation/kinship functions where the wording is accurate (the thorough audit correctly did not flag them). Same for xlsx (correct in file-readers, wrong only as a documented fileType value), “live animals” (correct in calcGU), POSIXct/YYYYMMDD (correct in real date functions). The grep finds candidates; the code decides which are bugs – do not blanket-fix a matched string. (3) The find→apply→verify Workflow shape for a batch of well-specified mechanical edits (vs. Learning 252’s open-ended semantic audit): (a) a parallel read-only fan-out (one agent per source file) returns byte-exact edit specs – each anchor copied verbatim, verified against the function body; (b) a SINGLE controller (me) applies them with a strict exact-match script (content.count(anchor) == 1 or ABORT that edit and report) – keeps tree ownership (SAFEGUARDS) and makes a paraphrased anchor fail loudly instead of clobbering; agents do NOT edit in parallel (same-file races; worktree-per-file + merge is infeasible when man/ regenerates once at the end); (c) a parallel adversarial re-verify (one skeptic per finding reads the REGENERATED .Rd against the code) → 37/37 CONFIRMED_FIXED, 0 problems, and it correctly left pre-existing #103-class typos (kinshipMatricies, columms, “length on”) untouched. --as-cran’s code/documentation mismatches … OK is a necessary but WEAK check (it only catches @param-vs-signature and \usage drift, not prose-vs-behavior); the doc-vs-code re-read is what actually certifies a semantic fix. (4) Clear a spell_check_package() flag by wrapping the identifier in \code{}, not by editing WORDLIST – spelling skips \code{}/\item{name} content (that is why the package’s hundreds of \code{camelCase} identifiers never trip it), so ignoreHerm and proc (from proc.time()) cleared once wrapped, which is ALSO the correct doc style. This avoided touching inst/WORDLIST, which carried the owner’s uncommitted errored addition (FM #22) – a WORDLIST edit would have forced a bundle-or-ask decision; the \code{} fix sidestepped it entirely. (5) FM #22 held: committed only my files by explicit path; left the owner’s .gitignore (build-artifact rules – which also silently gitignored this session’s tarball/.Rcheck) and inst/WORDLIST untouched. Phase-3E N/A (stated): doc-only, empty NAMESPACE diff, no runtime surface. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[workflow-args-arrive-as-json-string]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (S274, issue #109 fix 37/38 findings + 1 audit-missed sibling; direct-merge to master; --as-cran 0/0/1 GREEN; 37/37 adversarial verify)

Learning 254 – A finding deferred to another open issue is often NOT actually blocked by it – before re-deferring, test whether the defer-reason blocks the minimal correct fix, not the ideal final one. Finishing issue #109 by fixing its one remaining finding (#9 runModularApp, which S274 deferred to #110) and CLOSING the issue (owner scope-gate via one AskUserQuestion = “Fix #9 now + close #109”, over close-only / fix-without-closing). REFACTOR-class, doc-only; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. 1 R/ line → 1 man/*.Rd. (1) The defer-reason didn’t block the minimal fix. runModularApp’s @details said “Use \code{\link{runGeneKeepR}} to run the original monolithic version” – false today (runGeneKeepR() is a lifecycle::deprecate_soft alias whose body just calls runModularApp(); its own doc says the monolithic app was retired). S274/the audit deferred it to #110 because a full replacement wording might depend on #110’s rename decision (rename runModularApp→runGeneKeepR?). But the sentence is wrong REGARDLESS of #110, and stating the current fact (“runGeneKeepR() is a soft-deprecated alias for this function” – the audit’s own recommended fix, mirroring runGeneKeepR’s reciprocal @seealso) neither presumes the deprecation persists nor blocks a future rename (a rename rewrites this doc anyway, as part of #110). Lesson: distinguish “the ideal final wording is blocked” from “any correct fix is blocked” – the latter is what actually justifies deferring; a stale FALSEHOOD should be corrected to today’s reality now, not left standing while an unrelated design question waits. (2) Run the R CMD build/check --as-cran gate from the PACKAGE ROOT, not the scratchpad – this repo uses renv. Backgrounding the gate from the session scratchpad failed instantly at the vignette-install step (dependencies 'anytime','bslib','data.table',… are not available): with no .Rprofile in cwd, renv never activated, so .libPaths() pointed at a bootstrap library with nothing installed. .Rbuildignore excludes renv/renv.lock/.Rprofile (so the built tarball stays clean), but the build/check subprocess still needs the deps, which only the repo-root .Rprofile→renv library (~/Library/Caches/org.R-project.R/.../renv/library/nprcgenekeepr-…) provides. Fix: ( cd <repo-root> && R CMD build . && R CMD check --as-cran nprcgenekeepr_*.tar.gz ); the tarball + .Rcheck land in the repo root but are now gitignored (owner 6d445e0d). Prior sessions ran it there implicitly; this makes the renv-cwd dependency explicit so no session repeats the failed scratchpad run. (3) Close-out completeness for a multi-clause/batch issue: #109 had three clauses (reported rendering bug S272 / “audit for similar errors” S273 / fix all 38 findings S274+S275); closing was correct ONLY after the LAST finding was fixed + gated – verified all 38 addressed (37 S274 + #9 here + the 39th sibling S274) before closing. The naming question (#9’s cousin) correctly lives on as a SEPARATE issue (#110); closing #109 does not swallow it. Verify (firsthand): document() touched only the 2 intended files; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; lintr 0 (new line 75 ≤80); spell_check_package clean (no WORDLIST churn); --as-cran GREEN 0/0/2 with code/documentation mismatches … OK + Rd cross-references … OK (the \link{runGeneKeepR} target still resolves) + testthat.R [67s/67s] OK. Phase-3E N/A (stated): function body unchanged, empty NAMESPACE diff, no runtime surface. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]]. This was a REFACTOR-class doc-fix session – TDD RED/GREEN N/A. (S275, issue #109 fix finding #9 runModularApp + CLOSE #109; direct-merge to master; --as-cran 0/0/2 GREEN; NAMESPACE empty diff; lint 0; spell clean)

Learning 255 – Reversing a lifecycle deprecation is a clean, backward-compatible SWAP when both names stay exported – and STRICT-TDD-able if you make the RED tests genuinely flip. Issue #110 questioned the 2.0.0 decision to deprecate the informative runGeneKeepR() in favor of the low-information runModularApp(). Owner design-gate (AskUserQuestion) = “Reverse, keep both names” (over reverse-and-REMOVE-runModularApp / keep-as-is): runGeneKeepR() becomes the canonical, undeprecated function holding the shiny::shinyApp(appUI(), appServer) + runApp body; runModularApp() becomes a deprecate_soft(when="2.0.0", what="runModularApp()", with="runGeneKeepR()") alias forwarding to it. BEHAVIOR/API change (deprecation direction reversed) -> STRICT TDD, each phase gated; direct-merge landing owner-gated; 0 corrections / 0 overrides. (1) The swap is backward-compatible and NAMESPACE-neutral. Both functions stay @exported, so the NAMESPACE diff is EMPTY and existing runModularApp() callers keep working (soft warning, no hard break); only the @importFrom shiny shinyApp runApp moved from the alias’s roxygen to the canonical function’s (package-level, so no NAMESPACE change). Un-deprecating a recently-soft-deprecated function is low-risk. (2) RED-test design for FLIPPING a deprecation – two traps. (a) A naive “runGeneKeepR() is NOT deprecated” test using expect_no_warning(..., class="lifecycle_warning_deprecated") PASSES TRIVIALLY against the still-deprecated current code, because deprecate_soft is SILENT from a non-global/test caller – so it is not a valid RED signal. Fix: wrap the call in withr::local_options(lifecycle_verbosity = "warning") to FORCE the soft-deprecation to warn, so the assertion actually fails RED against current code and passes only once the deprecate call is removed. (b) The strongest RED signal is the DELEGATION DIRECTION: mock the sibling (local_mocked_bindings(runGeneKeepR = function(...) "GK")) and assert runModularApp() returns the sentinel – currently it returns the shiny sentinel (calls shiny directly), so it fails RED cleanly. (c) Mock BOTH shiny::shinyApp+shiny::runApp (.package="shiny") AND the sibling delegate in EVERY test, so that whichever code path runs (pre- or post-change), no real Shiny server launches during the RED run – a failing test must fail fast, not hang. local_mocked_bindings(.package="shiny") DOES intercept explicit shiny::fn() calls (it rebinds the namespace that :: reads). (3) A rename is only DONE when the docs that NAME the function are updated too (FM #13 literal-minimum). Left as-is, README/vignettes/NEWS would tell users to call the now-deprecated name, contradicting the code. The shared vignettes/manual_components/_running_shiny_application.Rmd is a child chunk of BOTH README.Rmd and 2 vignettes (one edit, three surfaces); plus 9 runModularApp() mentions across 5 vignettes/articles/*.qmd, a NEWS.Rmd dev-version bullet (issue-ref (#110) – the NEWS-issue-ref exception to [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]]), a loadSiteConfig.R doc \link, and CLAUDE.md. This also incidentally cleared the “for testing purposes” stale phrasing S275 flagged (runModularApp’s roxygen was rewritten as a deprecation note). (4) Two renv-env doc-render gotchas. (a) devtools::build_readme() FAILS here – it does a fresh clean local_install that aborts on missing deps; render directly instead: pkgload::load_all("."); rmarkdown::render("README.Rmd", output_file="README.md") (and same for NEWS.Rmd). (b) rmarkdown::render can leave a stray README.html byproduct in the working tree; if it is still there when R CMD build . snapshots the package, the tarball includes it and --as-cran raises a NON-STANDARD extra NOTE (“Non-standard file/directory found at top level: README.html”). Remove render byproducts BEFORE building (my first –as-cran run showed 3 NOTEs for exactly this; removing README.html and re-running returned the standing 0/0/2). Verify (firsthand): full suite 0 fail/0 error/0 true offenders; lintr 0 on 3 changed R files; spell_check_package clean; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; --as-cran (clean tree) GREEN 0/0/2 (standing benign pair) with code/documentation mismatches … OK + Rd cross-references … OK (both \link targets still resolve) + testthat.R [68s/68s] OK. Phase-3E DONE (not N/A – this was a behavior change): the reversed contract runs against the built+installed package via testthat.R inside –as-cran, AND a direct load_all behavioral smoke confirmed runGeneKeepR() emits no deprecation while runModularApp() emits one whose message names runGeneKeepR(). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]]. This was a BEHAVIOR-change STRICT-TDD session (RED->GREEN->REFACTOR, each AskUserQuestion-gated). (S276, issue #110 reverse the deprecation -> runGeneKeepR() canonical / runModularApp() soft-deprecated alias; direct-merge to master; --as-cran 0/0/2 GREEN; NAMESPACE empty diff; issue #110 CLOSED)

Learning 256 – A doc-audit “candidate code-fix” can be a REAL behavior bug hiding behind a doc that was reconciled to the buggy code; decide which of doc/code is authoritative from the SURROUNDING intent, then fix under STRICT TDD. Issue #109’s audit finding #31 flagged makeSimPed()’s @description as a doc-vs-code mismatch; S274 (doc-only) “fixed” it by rewriting the DOC to match the code (“Existing known parents are overwritten”). This session (owner-picked from the S276 candidate list) asked whether the CODE was the defect. It was: makeSimPed’s loop assigned each listed id’s sire/dam UNCONDITIONALLY (sample(vec,1L), or NA for an empty vector), never checking whether the existing parent was already known – so an animal with one KNOWN and one unknown parent had its known parent silently OVERWRITTEN (or ERASED to NA), biasing the simulated-kinship estimate. The surrounding intent overwhelmingly said “impute only UNKNOWN parents”: the original 19-month doc, the still-present @examples comment (“any unknown sire/dam is replaced”), createSimKinships’s @return (“possible parents for animals with unknown parents”), and the builder getPotentialParents (title “for animals with unknown parents”; selects is.na(sire) | is.na(dam) – an OR, so mixed known/unknown animals ARE listed, which is exactly what makes the overwrite bite in the real pipeline). Owner scope-gate (AskUserQuestion) = “Guard makeSimPed” (over fix-getPotentialParents / doc-only). (1) ULTRACODE read-only investigation BEFORE touching code (TDD-safe at pre-RED). A 9-agent read-only Workflow (5 investigate: intent/behavior/blast-radius/methodology/test-impact; 4 adversarial verify incl. a firsthand R reproduction) established it was a real hazard, that the primitive is DEFENSIBLE under the vignette’s “singleton candidate for a known parent” convention, and that the root cause is the getPotentialParents -> makeSimPed boundary. The adversarial split (2 CONFIRMED_BUG / 1 REFUTED_NOT_BUG / 1 DEPENDS_ON_INTENT) CONVERGED once you see WHERE each verifier placed the defect; guarding makeSimPed fixes the wired hazard for free AND makes the primitive robust regardless of caller encoding. Blast radius is narrow – the sim-kinship trio is NOT in the production GVA pipeline (that uses geneDrop + correctUnknownParentMeanKinship); makeSimPed is exported public API reached in-package only via examples/tests + the un-invoked inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R. (2) RED for a “preserve” contract: build a ped with a KNOWN parent and assert it is UNCHANGED after makeSimPed (fails against the overwrite code – 3 such tests: multi-element-pool overwrite, empty-vector erase, both-known), PLUS one “still imputes an unknown parent” guard so GREEN cannot be “never assign anything.” The existing lacy1989Ped tests are unaffected (A/B/E are NA/NA founders -> the guard collapses to prior behavior with the same RNG order). GREEN = guard to assign a parent ONLY when the existing value is NA (robust to an id absent from ped); NAMESPACE diff EMPTY. (3) A behavior change to a SEEDED Monte-Carlo function re-baselines downstream characterization goldens – run the FULL SUITE to find them ALL, don’t trust a partial prediction. The audit predicted 2 shifted goldens; the full suite found 4 files / 7 assertions (createSimKinships, cumulateSimKinships, countKinshipValues, summarizeKinshipValues) – all because smallPed’s A has a KNOWN sire Q now correctly preserved (was overwritten). VERIFY the shift is the CORRECT mechanism (confirmed A’s sire == Q, and raw kinship(A,J)==0 across all sims because A’s fixed founder sire is unrelated to J’s drawn parents) BEFORE re-baselining, and RECOMPUTE each value from the corrected code (do NOT paste an agent’s predicted number). (4) The build-equivalent (--as-cran) is the safety net a behavior change needs – a VIGNETTE can break even when every unit test passes (FM #24). simulatedKValues.Rmd joined two independently-sd>0-filtered summary frames by ROW POSITION (a latent bug); the corrected kinship distribution made the two filtered sets differ in size (84 vs 94 rows) -> data.frame(...) “arguments imply differing number of rows” -> vignette re-build FAILS -> R CMD build FAILS. Fix: merge(stats_short, stats_long, by = c("id_1","id_2"), suffixes = ...) (align by key, robust to size) in BOTH comparison chunks. My initial “no vignette change needed” judgment was WRONG; only the vignette rebuild caught it. A behavior fix is NOT done until the build passes. Verify (firsthand): 4 new makeSimPed tests pass; full suite 0 fail/0 error/0 true offenders; lintr 0 on the changed R file; spell_check_package clean; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; --as-cran (clean tree) GREEN 0/0/2 (standing benign pair) with code/documentation mismatches … OK + Rd cross-references … OK + checking examples [21s] OK + testthat.R [67s] OK + vignettes rebuilt. Phase-3E DONE (not N/A – behavior change): the guarded contract runs against the built+installed package via testthat.R inside –as-cran + the @example + a direct load_all smoke (A’s known sire Q preserved while NA founders still imputed).
